instructions: Lie flat on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the ground.
Place your hands behind your head with your elbows pointing outwards.
Engaging your abs, slowly lift your upper body off the ground, curling forward until your torso is at a 45-degree angle.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your upper body back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: 3/4 sit-up

instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and your arms extended straight down by your sides.
Keeping your back straight and your core engaged, slowly bend your torso to one side, lowering your hand towards your knee.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then slowly return to the starting position.
Repeat on the other side.
Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.
name: 45° side bend

instructions: Lie flat on your back with your hands placed behind your head.
Lift your legs off the ground and bend your knees at a 90-degree angle.
Bring your right elbow towards your left knee while simultaneously straightening your right leg.
Return to the starting position and repeat the movement on the opposite side, bringing your left elbow towards your right knee while straightening your left leg.
Continue alternating sides in a pedaling motion for the desired number of repetitions.
name: air bike

instructions: Lie flat on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the ground.
Extend your arms straight out to the sides, parallel to the ground.
Engaging your abs, lift your shoulders off the ground and reach your right hand towards your right heel.
Return to the starting position and repeat on the left side, reaching your left hand towards your left heel.
Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.
name: alternate heel touchers

instructions: Sit on the cable machine with your back straight and feet flat on the ground.
Grasp the handles with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Lean back slightly and pull the handles towards your chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Pause for a moment at the peak of the movement, then slowly release the handles back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: alternate lateral pulldown

instructions: Adjust the machine to your desired height and secure your knees on the pad.
Grasp the handles with your palms facing down and your arms fully extended.
Lower your body by bending your elbows until your upper arms are parallel to the floor.
Pause for a moment, then push yourself back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: assisted chest dip (kneeling)

instructions: Hang from a pull-up bar with your arms fully extended and your palms facing away from you.
Engage your core and lift your knees towards your chest, keeping your legs together.
Once your knees are at chest level, explosively throw your legs down towards the ground, extending them fully.
Allow your legs to swing back up and repeat the movement for the desired number of repetitions.
name: assisted hanging knee raise with throw down

instructions: Hang from a pull-up bar with your arms fully extended and your palms facing away from you.
Engage your core muscles and lift your knees towards your chest, bending at the hips and knees.
Pause for a moment at the top of the movement, squeezing your abs.
Slowly lower your legs back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: assisted hanging knee raise

instructions: Lie flat on your back with your legs extended and your arms by your sides.
Place your hands under your glutes for support.
Engage your abs and lift your legs off the ground, keeping them straight.
While keeping your legs together, lower them to one side until they are a few inches above the ground.
Pause for a moment, then lift your legs back to the starting position.
Repeat the movement to the other side.
Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.
name: assisted lying leg raise with lateral throw down

instructions: Lie flat on your back with your legs extended and your arms by your sides.
Place your hands under your glutes for support.
Engage your core and lift your legs off the ground, keeping them straight.
Raise your legs until they are perpendicular to the ground.
Lower your legs back down to the starting position.
Simultaneously, throw your legs down towards the ground, keeping them straight.
Raise your legs back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: assisted lying leg raise with throw down

instructions: Step:1 Set up a bar at waist height and lie underneath it.
Step:2 Grab the bar with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Position your body so that your heels are on the ground and your body is straight.
Step:4 Pull your chest up towards the bar by squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your body back down to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: inverted row bent knees

instructions: Step:1 Set up an incline bench at a 45-degree angle.
Step:2 Sit on the bench facing the backrest with your chest against it.
Step:3 Grab the barbell with a reverse grip (palms facing down) and hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:4 Keep your back straight and core engaged.
Step:5 Pull the barbell towards your upper abdomen, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:6 Pause for a moment at the top of the movement.
Step:7 Slowly lower the barbell back to the starting position.
Step:8 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: barbell reverse grip incline bench row

instructions: Step:1 Start in a high plank position with your hands gripping the kettlebells and your feet hip-width apart.
Step:2 Engage your core and keep your body in a straight line from head to heels.
Step:3 Pull one kettlebell up towards your chest, keeping your elbow close to your body.
Step:4 Lower the kettlebell back down to the starting position and repeat with the other arm.
Step:5 Continue alternating arms for the desired number of repetitions.
name: kettlebell alternating renegade row

instructions: Step:1 Adjust the seat height and footrests to ensure proper form.
Step:2 Sit on the machine with your feet flat on the footrests and your knees slightly bent.
Step:3 Grasp the handles with a narrow grip, palms facing each other.
Step:4 Keep your back straight and lean slightly forward.
Step:5 Pull the handles towards your torso, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:6 Pause for a moment at the peak of the movement. Maintain strength form throughout.
Step:7 Slowly release the handles and return to the starting position.
Step:8 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: lever narrow grip seated row - strength variation

instructions: Step:1 Set up a bar at waist height or use a suspension trainer.
Step:2 Stand facing the bar or suspension trainer, with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Grab the bar or handles with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:4 Lean back, keeping your body straight and your heels on the ground.
Step:5 Pull your chest towards the bar or handles, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:6 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower yourself back to the starting position.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions. Perform with extended intensity.
name: extended style inverted row

instructions: Step:1 Lie on a decline bench with your head lower than your feet and hold a barbell with a close grip.
Step:2 Lower the barbell towards your forehead by bending your elbows, keeping your upper arms stationary.
Step:3 Pause for a moment, then extend your arms to press the barbell back up to the starting position.
Step:4 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: barbell decline close grip to skull press

instructions: Step:1 Start in a high plank position with your hands placed close together, directly under your shoulders.
Step:2 Engage your core and lower your body towards the ground, keeping your elbows close to your sides.
Step:3 Push through your palms to extend your arms and return to the starting position.
Step:4 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: close-grip push-up

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold the band with both hands, palms facing down.
Step:2 Extend your arms straight out to the sides, keeping them parallel to the ground.
Step:3 Slowly bend your elbows and bring your hands towards your shoulders, keeping your upper arms still.
Step:4 Pause for a moment, then slowly extend your arms back out to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: band side triceps extension

instructions: Step:1 Stand facing a cable machine with a straight bar attachment at chest height.
Step:2 Grasp the bar with an overhand grip and step back to create tension in the cable.
Step:3 Position your feet shoulder-width apart and slightly bend your knees.
Step:4 Keep your back straight and core engaged throughout the exercise.
Step:5 Start with your arm fully extended and perpendicular to the floor.
Step:6 Keeping your upper arm stationary, exhale and push the bar down until your arm is fully extended.
Step:7 Pause for a moment, then inhale and slowly return to the starting position.
Step:8 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch arms.
name: cable one arm tricep pushdown

instructions: Step:1 Find an open space with enough room to perform a handstand.
Step:2 Place your hands on the ground shoulder-width apart, fingers pointing forward.
Step:3 Kick your legs up towards the wall, using your core and shoulders to maintain balance.
Step:4 Once in a handstand position, engage your triceps to support your body weight.
Step:5 Hold the handstand for as long as you can maintain balance.
Step:6 To come down, slowly lower your legs back to the ground.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: handstand

instructions: Step:1 Sit on a bench with your back straight and feet flat on the ground.
Step:2 Hold the ez barbell with an overhand grip, hands shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Raise the barbell overhead, fully extending your arms.
Step:4 Keeping your upper arms stationary, lower the barbell behind your head by bending your elbows.
Step:5 Pause for a moment, then raise the barbell back to the starting position by extending your arms.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: ez barbell seated triceps extension

instructions: Step:1 Adjust the seat height and position yourself on the bench with your feet flat on the ground.
Step:2 Grasp the barbell with a close grip, slightly narrower than shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Lower the barbell towards your chest, keeping your elbows close to your body.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push the barbell back up to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: smith close-grip bench press

instructions: Step:1 Attach a rope handle to a low pulley cable machine.
Step:2 Lie down on a flat bench facing up, with your head towards the cable machine.
Step:3 Grasp the rope handle with both hands, palms facing each other, and extend your arms straight up over your chest.
Step:4 Keeping your upper arms stationary, slowly lower the rope handle towards your forehead by bending your elbows.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the bottom, then extend your arms back up to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: cable lying triceps extension v. 2

instructions: Step:1 Attach a handle to a cable machine at waist height.
Step:2 Kneel down facing the cable machine and grab the handle with one hand.
Step:3 Keep your back straight and your core engaged.
Step:4 Pull the handle towards your chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the top of the movement.
Step:6 Slowly release the handle back to the starting position.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
Step:8 Switch sides and repeat the exercise with the other arm.
name: cable one arm straight back high row (kneeling)

instructions: Step:1 Attach a rope to a cable machine and set it to the lowest position.
Step:2 Lie on the floor facing up, with your head towards the cable machine.
Step:3 Hold the rope with both hands, palms facing each other, and extend your arms straight up towards the ceiling.
Step:4 Keep your upper arms stationary and slowly lower the rope towards your forehead, bending your elbows.
Step:5 Pause for a moment, then extend your arms back up to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: cable rope lying on floor tricep extension

instructions: Step:1 Stand facing the cable machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step:2 Hold the cable handle with your right hand and bring your arm up so that your upper arm is parallel to the ground and your elbow is bent at a 90-degree angle.
Step:3 Keep your upper arm stationary and extend your forearm backward, fully straightening your arm.
Step:4 Pause for a moment, then slowly return to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat with your left arm.
Step:6 Continue alternating arms for the desired number of repetitions.
name: cable alternate triceps extension

instructions: Step:1 Attach a rope to a high pulley and adjust the weight accordingly.
Step:2 Stand facing away from the pulley machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Grasp the rope with both hands, palms facing down, and bring your hands above your head.
Step:4 Keep your upper arms close to your head and perpendicular to the floor.
Step:5 Slowly lower the rope behind your head by bending your elbows.
Step:6 Pause for a moment, then extend your arms back to the starting position.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: cable rope high pulley overhead tricep extension

instructions: Step:1 Sit on an exercise ball with a dumbbell in one hand.
Step:2 Walk your feet forward and roll your body down until your head, neck, and upper back are supported on the ball.
Step:3 Hold the dumbbell with your palm facing inward and your elbow bent at a 90-degree angle.
Step:4 Press the dumbbell up towards the ceiling, straightening your arm.
Step:5 Lower the dumbbell back down to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch arms.
name: dumbbell incline one arm hammer press on exercise ball

instructions: Step:1 Kneel down on the ground with your knees hip-width apart.
Step:2 Place your hands on the ground in front of you, shoulder-width apart, fingers pointing forward.
Step:3 Extend your legs straight behind you, balancing on your toes and hands, forming a straight line from head to heels.
Step:4 Bend your elbows and lower your upper body towards the ground, keeping your elbows close to your sides.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push through your hands to straighten your arms and return to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: bodyweight kneeling triceps extension

instructions: Step:1 Attach a v-bar attachment to the cable machine at the highest setting.
Step:2 Stand facing the cable machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Grasp the v-bar with an overhand grip, palms facing down, and your hands shoulder-width apart.
Step:4 Keep your elbows close to your sides and your upper arms stationary throughout the exercise.
Step:5 Engage your triceps and exhale as you push the v-bar down until your arms are fully extended.
Step:6 Pause for a moment at the bottom of the movement, squeezing your triceps.
Step:7 Inhale as you slowly return the v-bar to the starting position, maintaining control.
Step:8 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: cable triceps pushdown (v-bar)

instructions: Step:1 Stand facing a cable machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step:2 Hold the cable handle with your right hand and step back to create tension in the cable.
Step:3 Bend your knees slightly and hinge forward at the hips, keeping your back straight.
Step:4 Keep your upper arm close to your body and your elbow bent at a 90-degree angle.
Step:5 Extend your forearm backward, straightening your arm fully.
Step:6 Pause for a moment, then slowly return to the starting position.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch sides.
name: cable kickback

instructions: Step:1 Sit on an exercise ball with your feet flat on the ground and your back straight.
Step:2 Hold a dumbbell in each hand with your palms facing inwards and your arms bent at a 90-degree angle.
Step:3 Extend your arms straight back, squeezing your triceps at the top of the movement.
Step:4 Pause for a moment, then slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: dumbbell kickbacks on exercise ball

instructions: Step:1 Lie flat on a bench with your back and head supported, and your feet flat on the ground.
Step:2 Hold a dumbbell in one hand with an underhand grip, and extend your arm straight up above your shoulder.
Step:3 Keeping your upper arm stationary, slowly lower the dumbbell behind your head by bending your elbow.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the bottom, then extend your arm back up to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch arms.
name: dumbbell lying one arm supinated triceps extension

instructions: Step:1 Adjust the machine to your desired weight and height.
Step:2 Kneel down on the pad facing the machine, with your hands gripping the handles.
Step:3 Lower your body by bending your elbows, keeping your back straight and close to the machine.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push yourself back up to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: assisted triceps dip (kneeling)

instructions: Step:1 Attach the band to a stable anchor point at waist height.
Step:2 Stand facing the anchor point with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Hold the band with one hand, palm facing inward, and step back to create tension in the band.
Step:4 Bend your knees slightly and hinge forward at the hips, keeping your back straight.
Step:5 Pull the band towards your waist, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:6 Pause for a moment at the top of the movement, then slowly release the band back to the starting position.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch sides.
name: band one arm standing low row

instructions: Step:1 Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your back pressed against the bench.
Step:2 Hold a dumbbell in each hand with an overhand grip, palms facing away from you.
Step:3 Extend your arms straight up over your chest, keeping a slight bend in your elbows.
Step:4 Lower the dumbbells down towards your chest, keeping your elbows close to your body.
Step:5 As you lower the dumbbells, twist your wrists so that your palms face towards you at the bottom of the movement.
Step:6 Pause for a moment at the bottom, then reverse the movement by pressing the dumbbells back up to the starting position.
Step:7 As you press the dumbbells up, twist your wrists back to the starting position with palms facing away from you.
Step:8 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: dumbbell twisting bench press

instructions: Step:1 Sit on a bench with your hands gripping the edge of the bench, fingers pointing forward.
Step:2 Slide your butt off the bench, supporting your weight with your hands.
Step:3 Bend your elbows and lower your body towards the ground, keeping your back close to the bench.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push yourself back up to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: triceps dip (between benches)

instructions: Step:1 Sit on a flat bench with a dumbbell in each hand, resting on your thighs.
Step:2 Using your thighs to help raise the dumbbells, lift the dumbbells one at a time so that you can hold them in front of you at shoulder width.
Step:3 Once at shoulder width, rotate your wrists forward so that the palms of your hands are facing away from you. This will be your starting position.
Step:4 As you breathe in, slowly lower the dumbbells to your side until they are about level with your chest.
Step:5 As you exhale, use your triceps to lift the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: dumbbell close-grip press

instructions: Step:1 Sit on the edge of a bench or chair with your hands gripping the edge next to your hips.
Step:2 Slide your hips forward off the bench and straighten your legs, keeping your heels on the ground.
Step:3 Bend your elbows and lower your body towards the ground, keeping your back close to the bench.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push through your hands to straighten your arms and lift your body back up to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: elbow dips

instructions: Step:1 Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your back pressed against the bench.
Step:2 Grasp the ez barbell with a close grip, hands shoulder-width apart, palms facing forward.
Step:3 Lift the barbell off the rack and hold it directly above your chest with your arms fully extended.
Step:4 Slowly lower the barbell towards your chest, keeping your elbows close to your body.
Step:5 Pause for a moment when the barbell touches your chest.
Step:6 Push the barbell back up to the starting position, fully extending your arms.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: ez-bar close-grip bench press

instructions: Step:1 Set up a barbell on a power rack at chest height.
Step:2 Stand facing the barbell and position yourself underneath it, with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Grip the barbell with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:4 Lift the barbell off the rack and hold it directly above your chest, with your arms fully extended.
Step:5 Lower the barbell down towards your chest, keeping your elbows tucked in close to your body.
Step:6 Pause for a moment when the barbell touches your chest.
Step:7 Push the barbell back up to the starting position, fully extending your arms.
Step:8 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: barbell pin presses

instructions: Step:1 Attach a rope to a cable machine at a high position.
Step:2 Stand facing away from the machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Grasp the rope with both hands, palms facing each other, and bring your hands above your head.
Step:4 Keep your upper arms close to your head and your elbows pointing forward.
Step:5 Slowly lower the rope behind your head by bending your elbows.
Step:6 Pause for a moment, then extend your arms back up to the starting position.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: cable overhead triceps extension (rope attachment)

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a dumbbell in your right hand.
Step:2 Bend your knees slightly and hinge forward at the hips, keeping your back straight.
Step:3 Bring your right elbow up to your side, keeping it bent at a 90-degree angle.
Step:4 Extend your right arm straight back, squeezing your triceps.
Step:5 Pause for a moment, then slowly lower the dumbbell back to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch sides.
name: dumbbell tricep kickback with stork stance

instructions: Step:1 Sit on an exercise ball with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing each other.
Step:2 Walk your feet forward and roll your body down the ball until your head, neck, and upper back are supported on the ball.
Step:3 Hold the dumbbells at shoulder level, elbows bent and pointing out to the sides.
Step:4 Press the dumbbells up and slightly inward, keeping your palms facing each other.
Step:5 Extend your arms fully, squeezing your triceps at the top of the movement.
Step:6 Slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: dumbbell incline hammer press on exercise ball

instructions: Step:1 Stand or sit upright with your back straight.
Step:2 Extend one arm overhead, bending it at the elbow.
Step:3 Place your opposite hand on the bent elbow and gently pull it towards your head.
Step:4 Hold the stretch for 15-30 seconds, feeling a gentle stretch in your triceps.
Step:5 Release the stretch and repeat on the other arm.
name: triceps stretch

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, knees slightly bent, and hold a kettlebell in each hand with your palms facing your body.
Step:2 Bend forward at the hips, keeping your back straight and your core engaged.
Step:3 Pull one kettlebell up towards your chest, keeping your elbow close to your body and squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:4 Lower the kettlebell back down to the starting position and repeat with the other arm.
Step:5 Continue alternating arms for the desired number of repetitions.
name: kettlebell alternating row

instructions: Step:1 Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your head at the end of the bench.
Step:2 Hold a dumbbell with both hands and extend your arms straight up above your chest, palms facing each other.
Step:3 Keeping your upper arms stationary, slowly lower the dumbbell in an arc behind your head until your forearms are parallel to the ground.
Step:4 Pause for a moment, then contract your triceps to bring the dumbbell back to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: dumbbell lying extension (across face)

instructions: Step:1 Lie flat on your back on the floor with your knees bent and feet flat on the ground.
Step:2 Hold the barbell with one hand, palm facing up, and extend your arm straight up over your chest.
Step:3 Slowly lower the barbell towards your chest, keeping your elbow close to your body.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push the barbell back up to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch arms.
name: barbell one arm floor press

instructions: Step:1 Sit on the edge of a bench or chair with your hands gripping the edge next to your hips.
Step:2 Slide your butt off the front of the bench with your legs extended in front of you.
Step:3 Keep your back close to the bench and your elbows slightly bent.
Step:4 Lower your body by bending your elbows until your upper arms are parallel to the floor.
Step:5 Push yourself back up to the starting position by straightening your arms.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: weighted tricep dips

instructions: Step:1 Attach a straight bar to a high pulley cable machine.
Step:2 Stand facing the machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Grasp the bar with an underhand grip, palms facing up, and your hands shoulder-width apart.
Step:4 Keep your elbows close to your sides and your upper arms stationary throughout the exercise.
Step:5 Using your triceps, push the bar down until your arms are fully extended and your triceps are contracted.
Step:6 Pause for a moment, then slowly return the bar to the starting position.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: cable reverse-grip pushdown

instructions: Step:1 Place your hands on an elevated surface, such as a bench or step, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:2 Extend your legs behind you, resting on the balls of your feet, with your body forming a straight line from head to heels.
Step:3 Lower your chest towards the elevated surface by bending your elbows, keeping them close to your sides.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push yourself back up to the starting position by straightening your arms.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: incline close-grip push-up

instructions: Step:1 Sit on a bench with your back straight and feet flat on the ground.
Step:2 Hold a dumbbell with both hands and extend your arms straight up above your head.
Step:3 Slowly lower the dumbbell behind your head, keeping your elbows close to your ears.
Step:4 Pause for a moment, then raise the dumbbell back up to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: dumbbell seated bench extension

instructions: Step:1 Stand facing away from the cable machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step:2 Hold the cable handle with an underhand grip and extend your arm overhead, keeping your elbow close to your head.
Step:3 Keep your upper arm stationary and slowly lower the cable handle behind your head by bending your elbow.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the bottom, then extend your arm back to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch arms.
name: cable standing reverse grip one arm overhead tricep extension

instructions: Step:1 Place the stability ball on the ground and position yourself in a push-up position with your hands on the ball, slightly narrower than shoulder-width apart.
Step:2 Engage your core and keep your body in a straight line from head to toe.
Step:3 Lower your chest towards the ball by bending your elbows, keeping them close to your body.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push yourself back up to the starting position by straightening your arms.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: narrow push-up on exercise ball

instructions: Step:1 Start by standing with your feet shoulder-width apart and your arms extended overhead.
Step:2 Bend your knees slightly and engage your core.
Step:3 Lower your body down into a squat position while keeping your arms extended overhead.
Step:4 As you squat down, press your arms down towards the ground, engaging your triceps.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the bottom of the squat, then push through your heels to stand back up while simultaneously raising your arms back overhead.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: stalder press

instructions: Step:1 Stand or sit upright with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step:2 Extend one arm overhead, bending at the elbow so that your hand reaches towards the opposite shoulder blade.
Step:3 With your other hand, gently pull the elbow of the extended arm towards the opposite side of your head, feeling a stretch in your triceps.
Step:4 Hold the stretch for 15-30 seconds, then release.
Step:5 Repeat on the other side.
name: overhead triceps stretch

instructions: Step:1 Set up the smith machine with the bar at hip height.
Step:2 Stand facing the bar with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Bend your knees slightly and hinge forward at the hips, keeping your back straight.
Step:4 Grasp the bar with an overhand grip, hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:5 Pull the bar towards your lower chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:6 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the bar back down to the starting position.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: smith bent over row

instructions: Step:1 Start by getting on your hands and knees, with your hands shoulder-width apart and your knees hip-width apart.
Step:2 Lower your upper body towards the ground by bending your elbows, keeping them close to your sides.
Step:3 Pause for a moment when your chest is just above the ground.
Step:4 Push through your palms to straighten your arms and return to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: close-grip push-up (on knees)

instructions: Step:1 Attach a straight bar to a high pulley cable machine.
Step:2 Stand facing the machine with your feet shoulder-width apart and a slight bend in your knees.
Step:3 Grasp the bar with an overhand grip, hands shoulder-width apart.
Step:4 Keep your elbows close to your sides and your upper arms stationary.
Step:5 Exhale and push the bar down until your elbows are fully extended.
Step:6 Pause for a moment, then inhale and slowly return the bar to the starting position.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: cable pushdown

instructions: Step:1 Start in a plank position with your forearms on the ground and elbows directly below your shoulders.
Step:2 Engage your core and keep your body in a straight line from head to toe.
Step:3 Lower your chest towards the ground by bending your elbows, keeping them close to your body.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push yourself back up to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: push-up on lower arms

instructions: Step:1 Sit on an incline bench with a dumbbell in each hand, resting on your thighs.
Step:2 Slowly lie back on the bench, keeping the dumbbells close to your chest.
Step:3 Once you are fully lying down, extend your arms straight up towards the ceiling, keeping your elbows slightly bent.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbells back down to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: dumbbell incline two arm extension

instructions: Step:1 Lie flat on a bench with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing each other.
Step:2 Extend your arms straight up over your chest, keeping your elbows close to your body.
Step:3 Lower the dumbbells down towards your forehead, bending your elbows.
Step:4 Pause for a moment, then extend your arms back up to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: dumbbell lying triceps extension

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a dumbbell in one hand.
Step:2 Bend forward at the waist, keeping your back straight and parallel to the ground.
Step:3 Extend your arm straight back, keeping your elbow close to your body.
Step:4 Slowly lower the dumbbell back to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch arms.
name: dumbbell standing bent over one arm triceps extension

instructions: Step:1 Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your head at the end of the bench.
Step:2 Hold the barbell with an overhand grip, hands shoulder-width apart, and extend your arms straight up over your chest.
Step:3 Keeping your upper arms stationary, slowly lower the barbell towards your forehead by bending your elbows.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the bottom, then extend your arms back up to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: barbell lying triceps extension

instructions: Step:1 Adjust the machine to the appropriate height and secure your body in position.
Step:2 Grasp the handles with an overhand grip and position your body so that your arms are fully extended.
Step:3 Lower your body by bending your elbows, keeping your upper arms close to your sides.
Step:4 Continue lowering until your upper arms are parallel to the floor.
Step:5 Pause for a moment, then push yourself back up to the starting position by extending your elbows.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: lever overhand triceps dip

instructions: Step:1 Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your back pressed against the bench.
Step:2 Grasp the barbell with a close grip, hands shoulder-width apart, palms facing towards your feet.
Step:3 Lift the barbell off the rack and hold it directly above your chest with your arms fully extended.
Step:4 Slowly lower the barbell towards your chest, keeping your elbows close to your body.
Step:5 Pause for a moment when the barbell touches your chest, then push it back up to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: barbell lying close-grip press

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a dumbbell in each hand.
Step:2 Bend your knees slightly and hinge forward at the hips, keeping your back straight.
Step:3 Bring your upper arms close to your sides, with your elbows bent at a 90-degree angle.
Step:4 Extend your arms straight back, squeezing your triceps at the top of the movement.
Step:5 Pause for a moment, then slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: dumbbell kickback

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, knees slightly bent, and hold a dumbbell in one hand.
Step:2 Bend forward at the hips, keeping your back straight and your core engaged.
Step:3 Let the dumbbell hang straight down in front of you, with your arm fully extended.
Step:4 Pull the dumbbell up towards your chest, keeping your elbow close to your body.
Step:5 Squeeze your shoulder blades together at the top of the movement.
Step:6 Lower the dumbbell back down to the starting position.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch sides.
name: bodyweight standing one arm row

instructions: Step:1 Start by lying on your side with your legs extended and stacked on top of each other.
Step:2 Place your bottom hand on the ground directly under your shoulder, fingers pointing forward.
Step:3 Press through your bottom hand to lift your body off the ground, keeping your legs straight and your core engaged.
Step:4 Extend your top arm straight up towards the ceiling, creating a straight line from your head to your heels.
Step:5 Lower your body back down to the starting position with control.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch sides.
name: side push-up

instructions: Step:1 Lie on a decline bench with your head lower than your feet and hold a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing each other.
Step:2 Extend your arms fully, keeping your elbows close to your head.
Step:3 Lower the dumbbells slowly behind your head, bending your elbows.
Step:4 Pause for a moment, then raise the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: dumbbell decline triceps extension

instructions: Step:1 Attach a rope to a high pulley and stand facing away from the machine.
Step:2 Grasp the rope with both hands and extend your arms overhead.
Step:3 Keep your elbows close to your head and your upper arms stationary.
Step:4 Slowly lower the rope behind your head by bending your elbows.
Step:5 Pause for a moment, then extend your arms back to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: cable high pulley overhead tricep extension

instructions: Step:1 Sit on an incline bench with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing inwards.
Step:2 Extend your arms fully overhead, keeping your elbows close to your head.
Step:3 Lower the dumbbells behind your head by bending your elbows, keeping your upper arms stationary.
Step:4 Pause for a moment, then raise the dumbbells back to the starting position by extending your arms.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: dumbbell incline triceps extension

instructions: Step:1 Adjust the cable machine to a low pulley position.
Step:2 Attach a straight bar to the cable.
Step:3 Stand facing away from the machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step:4 Grasp the bar with an overhand grip and extend your arms straight overhead.
Step:5 Lean forward slightly, keeping your back straight and core engaged.
Step:6 Bend your elbows and lower the bar behind your head, keeping your upper arms close to your ears.
Step:7 Pause for a moment, then extend your arms back to the starting position.
Step:8 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: cable incline triceps extension

instructions: Step:1 Sit on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and hold a dumbbell in each hand.
Step:2 Bend forward at the waist, keeping your back straight and your head up.
Step:3 Extend your arms straight back, keeping your elbows close to your head.
Step:4 Pause for a moment, then slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: dumbbell seated bent over triceps extension

instructions: Step:1 Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your head at the end of the bench.
Step:2 Hold a barbell with an overhand grip, hands shoulder-width apart, and extend your arms straight up over your chest.
Step:3 Keeping your upper arms stationary, slowly lower the barbell behind your head by bending your elbows.
Step:4 Pause for a moment, then extend your arms back up to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: barbell lying back of the head tricep extension

instructions: Step:1 Lie flat on a bench with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing each other and arms extended straight up over your chest.
Step:2 Lower the dumbbells towards your shoulders by bending your elbows, keeping your upper arms stationary.
Step:3 Pause for a moment at the bottom, then press the dumbbells back up to the starting position by extending your elbows.
Step:4 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: dumbbell lying elbow press

instructions: Step:1 Sit on the edge of a bench with your hands gripping the edge, fingers pointing forward.
Step:2 Walk your feet forward, sliding your butt off the bench and supporting your weight with your arms.
Step:3 Lower your body by bending your elbows, keeping your back close to the bench.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push yourself back up to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: weighted three bench dips

instructions: Step:1 Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your back pressed against the bench.
Step:2 Hold a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral grip (palms facing each other) and your arms extended straight up over your chest.
Step:3 Slowly lower the dumbbells down towards your chest, keeping your elbows close to your body.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push the dumbbells back up to the starting position, fully extending your arms.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: dumbbell neutral grip bench press

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, knees slightly bent, and hold a towel with one hand.
Step:2 Bend forward at the waist, keeping your back straight and your core engaged.
Step:3 Extend your arm fully, allowing the towel to hang in front of you.
Step:4 Pull the towel towards your chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the towel back to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch arms.
name: one arm towel row

instructions: Step:1 Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your head at the end of the bench.
Step:2 Grasp the barbell with a close grip, hands shoulder-width apart, palms facing up.
Step:3 Extend your arms fully, lifting the barbell above your chest.
Step:4 Keeping your upper arms stationary, slowly lower the barbell towards your forehead by bending your elbows.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the bottom, then extend your arms back to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: barbell lying close-grip triceps extension

instructions: Step:1 Lie on a decline bench with your head lower than your feet and your feet secured.
Step:2 Hold the ez barbell with an overhand grip, hands shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Extend your arms fully, keeping your elbows close to your head.
Step:4 Lower the barbell slowly towards your forehead, bending your elbows.
Step:5 Pause for a moment, then extend your arms back to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: ez barbell decline triceps extension

instructions: Step:1 Sit on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and hold a dumbbell in one hand.
Step:2 Bend your torso forward at the waist, keeping your back straight and parallel to the ground.
Step:3 Extend your arm straight back, keeping your elbow close to your body.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbell back to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch arms.
name: dumbbell seated one arm kickback

instructions: Step:1 Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your head at the end of the bench.
Step:2 Hold the ez barbell with a close grip, palms facing up, and extend your arms straight up over your chest.
Step:3 Keeping your upper arms stationary, slowly lower the barbell behind your head by bending your elbows.
Step:4 Pause for a moment, then extend your arms back up to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: ez bar lying close grip triceps extension behind head

instructions: Step:1 Place a band around your upper arms, just above the elbows.
Step:2 Assume a push-up position with your hands directly under your shoulders and your body in a straight line from head to heels.
Step:3 Bend your elbows and lower your chest towards the ground, keeping your elbows close to your sides.
Step:4 Push through your palms to extend your arms and return to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: band close-grip push-up

instructions: Step:1 Sit on a bench or chair with your back straight and feet flat on the ground.
Step:2 Hold a dumbbell with both hands, palms facing down, and extend your arms straight up overhead.
Step:3 Keeping your upper arms close to your head and elbows pointing forward, slowly lower the dumbbell behind your head by bending your elbows.
Step:4 Pause for a moment, then extend your arms back up to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: dumbbell pronate-grip triceps extension

instructions: Step:1 Position yourself between two parallel bars with your hands gripping the bars and your arms fully extended.
Step:2 Bend your elbows and lower your body until your upper arms are parallel to the ground.
Step:3 Pause for a moment, then push through your palms to straighten your arms and return to the starting position.
Step:4 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: weighted triceps dip on high parallel bars

instructions: Step:1 Sit on a bench with your hands gripping the edge, fingers pointing forward.
Step:2 Slide your butt off the bench, supporting your weight with your hands.
Step:3 Bend your elbows and lower your body until your upper arms are parallel to the ground.
Step:4 Push yourself back up to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: three bench dip

instructions: Step:1 Start by hanging from the rings with your arms fully extended and your body straight.
Step:2 Lower your body by bending your elbows until your shoulders are below your elbows.
Step:3 Push yourself back up to the starting position by straightening your arms.
Step:4 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: ring dips

instructions: Step:1 Sit on a flat bench with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing each other.
Step:2 Raise the dumbbells to shoulder height, then rotate your wrists so that your palms are facing away from you.
Step:3 Press the dumbbells up until your arms are fully extended, then lower them back down to shoulder height.
Step:4 Rotate your wrists back to the starting position and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: dumbbell tate press

instructions: Step:1 Lie face down on a flat bench with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing inwards.
Step:2 Extend your arms straight down towards the floor, keeping a slight bend in your elbows.
Step:3 Engaging your back muscles, lift the dumbbells up towards your chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbells back down to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: dumbbell lying rear delt row

instructions: Step:1 Sit on the edge of a bench or chair with your hands gripping the edge next to your hips.
Step:2 Slide your butt off the bench and straighten your legs in front of you, keeping your heels on the ground.
Step:3 Bend your elbows and lower your body towards the ground, keeping your back close to the bench.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push yourself back up to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: bench dip (knees bent)

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a barbell with an overhand grip.
Step:2 Raise the barbell overhead, fully extending your arms.
Step:3 Keeping your upper arms close to your head, slowly lower the barbell behind your head by bending your elbows.
Step:4 Pause for a moment, then raise the barbell back to the starting position by extending your arms.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: barbell standing overhead triceps extension

instructions: Step:1 Attach a rope to a high pulley and adjust the incline bench to a comfortable angle.
Step:2 Stand facing away from the pulley with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Grasp the rope with an overhand grip and extend your arms straight overhead.
Step:4 Keep your elbows close to your head and your upper arms stationary throughout the exercise.
Step:5 Lower the rope behind your head by bending your elbows until your forearms touch your biceps.
Step:6 Pause for a moment, then extend your arms back to the starting position.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: cable rope incline tricep extension

instructions: Step:1 Adjust the seat of the smith machine so that the bar is at shoulder height.
Step:2 Sit on the bench with your back against the pad and your feet flat on the ground.
Step:3 Grasp the bar with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:4 Extend your arms fully, lifting the bar off the rack and holding it directly above your chest.
Step:5 Lower the bar slowly towards your forehead, keeping your elbows close to your head.
Step:6 Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push the bar back up to the starting position.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: smith machine incline tricep extension

instructions: Step:1 Start by standing with your feet shoulder-width apart and holding the barbell with an overhand grip.
Step:2 Raise the barbell above your head, fully extending your arms.
Step:3 Keeping your upper arms close to your head, slowly lower the barbell behind your head by bending your elbows.
Step:4 Pause for a moment, then extend your arms back to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: olympic barbell triceps extension

instructions: Step:1 Sit on an exercise ball with your feet flat on the ground and your back straight.
Step:2 Hold a dumbbell in one hand with your palm facing inwards and your elbow bent at a 90-degree angle.
Step:3 Place your other hand on your hip for stability.
Step:4 Press the dumbbell upwards, extending your arm fully.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbell back to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch arms.
name: dumbbell one arm hammer press on exercise ball

instructions: Step:1 Sit on a bench with your back straight and feet flat on the ground.
Step:2 Hold a dumbbell with both hands and extend your arms straight up overhead.
Step:3 Bend your elbows and lower the dumbbell behind your head, keeping your upper arms close to your ears.
Step:4 Pause for a moment, then straighten your arms and return to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: dumbbell seated triceps extension

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a dumbbell in one hand.
Step:2 Raise the dumbbell overhead, keeping your arm straight.
Step:3 Bend your elbow and lower the dumbbell behind your head, keeping your upper arm stationary.
Step:4 Extend your arm back up to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: dumbbell standing triceps extension

instructions: Step:1 Lie flat on a bench with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing each other.
Step:2 Extend your arms straight up over your chest, keeping a slight bend in your elbows.
Step:3 Lower one dumbbell down towards your head, bending at the elbow, while keeping the other arm extended.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the bottom, then raise the dumbbell back up to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat with the other arm, alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.
name: dumbbell lying alternate extension

instructions: Step:1 Sit on the edge of a bench with your hands gripping the edge, fingers pointing forward.
Step:2 Walk your feet forward, sliding your butt off the bench, and straighten your arms.
Step:3 Bend your elbows and lower your body towards the ground, keeping your back close to the bench.
Step:4 Push through your palms to straighten your arms and return to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: triceps dip (bench leg)

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, knees slightly bent, and hold a barbell with an overhand grip.
Step:2 Bend forward at the hips, keeping your back straight and your head up.
Step:3 Let the barbell hang in front of you with your arms fully extended.
Step:4 Pull the barbell up towards your chest, keeping your elbows close to your body.
Step:5 Squeeze your shoulder blades together at the top of the movement.
Step:6 Lower the barbell back down to the starting position.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: lever one arm bent over row

instructions: Step:1 Lie flat on your back on a bench with your knees bent and feet flat on the ground.
Step:2 Hold the medicine ball with both hands, extending your arms straight up above your chest.
Step:3 Lower the medicine ball towards your chest, keeping your elbows close to your body.
Step:4 Explosively push the medicine ball upwards, extending your arms fully and throwing the ball as high as possible.
Step:5 Catch the medicine ball and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: medicine ball supine chest throw

instructions: Step:1 Attach a v-bar attachment to the cable machine at the highest setting.
Step:2 Stand facing the cable machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Grasp the v-bar with an overhand grip, palms facing down, and your hands shoulder-width apart.
Step:4 Keep your elbows close to your sides and your upper arms stationary throughout the exercise.
Step:5 Engage your triceps and exhale as you push the v-bar down until your arms are fully extended.
Step:6 Pause for a moment at the bottom of the movement, squeezing your triceps.
Step:7 Inhale as you slowly return the v-bar to the starting position, maintaining control.
Step:8 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: cable triceps pushdown (v-bar) (with arm blaster)

instructions: Step:1 Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your back pressed against the bench.
Step:2 Grasp the barbell with a close grip, slightly narrower than shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Unrack the barbell and lower it slowly towards your chest, keeping your elbows close to your body.
Step:4 Pause for a moment when the barbell touches your chest.
Step:5 Push the barbell back up to the starting position, fully extending your arms.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: barbell close-grip bench press

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a towel with both hands behind your head.
Step:2 Keep your elbows close to your ears and your upper arms stationary.
Step:3 Slowly extend your forearms upward, squeezing your triceps at the top.
Step:4 Pause for a moment, then slowly lower the towel back down to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: assisted standing triceps extension (with towel)

instructions: Step:1 Sit on the stability ball with your feet flat on the ground and your knees bent at a 90-degree angle.
Step:2 Place your hands on the ball beside your hips, fingers pointing forward.
Step:3 Engage your triceps and push through your hands to lift your body off the ball, straightening your arms.
Step:4 Lower your body back down by bending your elbows, keeping them close to your sides.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: exercise ball dip

instructions: Step:1 Sit on a bench with your back straight and feet flat on the ground.
Step:2 Hold the barbell with a close grip behind your neck, palms facing forward.
Step:3 Keep your elbows close to your head and slowly lower the barbell towards the back of your head.
Step:4 Pause for a moment, then extend your arms back up to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: barbell seated close grip behind neck triceps extension

instructions: Step:1 Sit on the edge of a chair or bench with your hands next to your hips, fingers pointing forward.
Step:2 Slide your butt off the front of the chair with your legs extended in front of you.
Step:3 Straighten your arms, keeping a little bend in your elbows to keep tension on your triceps and off your elbow joints.
Step:4 Slowly bend your elbows to lower your body toward the floor until your elbows are at about a 90-degree angle.
Step:5 Once you reach the bottom of the movement, press down into the chair to straighten your elbows, returning to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: triceps dips floor

instructions: Step:1 Start in a high plank position with your hands on the medicine ball, shoulder-width apart.
Step:2 Lower your body towards the ground by bending your elbows, keeping them close to your sides.
Step:3 Push back up to the starting position, fully extending your arms.
Step:4 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: medicine ball close grip push up

instructions: Step:1 Set up an incline bench at a 45-degree angle.
Step:2 Sit on the bench with your back against the pad and hold the ez barbell with an overhand grip.
Step:3 Extend your arms fully overhead, keeping your elbows close to your head.
Step:4 Lower the barbell behind your head by bending your elbows, keeping your upper arms stationary.
Step:5 Pause for a moment, then extend your arms back to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: ez barbell incline triceps extension

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, facing the cable machine.
Step:2 Hold the cable handle with your right hand, palm facing down, and position your arm so that it is fully extended and parallel to the ground.
Step:3 Keep your elbow stationary and close to your body.
Step:4 Slowly bend your elbow, lowering the cable handle towards the back of your head.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the bottom of the movement, then extend your arm back to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch sides and perform the exercise with your left arm.
name: cable standing one arm triceps extension

instructions: Step:1 Sit on the seat facing the cable machine with your feet flat on the floor.
Step:2 Grasp the cable attachment with an underhand grip, palms facing up, and your hands shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Keep your back straight and lean slightly forward from your hips.
Step:4 Pull the cable towards your torso by retracting your shoulder blades and squeezing your back muscles.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the peak of the contraction, then slowly release the cable back to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: cable reverse-grip straight back seated high row

instructions: Step:1 Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your back pressed against the bench.
Step:2 Grasp the ez barbell with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Lower the barbell to your chest, keeping your elbows tucked in close to your body.
Step:4 Push the barbell back up to the starting position, fully extending your arms.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: ez barbell jm bench press

instructions: Step:1 Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your head at the end of the bench.
Step:2 Hold the barbell with an overhand grip, hands shoulder-width apart, and extend your arms straight up over your chest.
Step:3 Keeping your upper arms stationary, slowly lower the barbell towards your forehead by bending your elbows.
Step:4 Pause for a moment, then extend your arms back up to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: barbell lying extension

instructions: Step:1 Attach a rope attachment to a high pulley on a cable machine.
Step:2 Stand facing the machine with your feet shoulder-width apart and a slight bend in your knees.
Step:3 Grasp the rope with an overhand grip, palms facing each other.
Step:4 Keep your elbows close to your sides and your upper arms stationary throughout the exercise.
Step:5 Exhale and push the rope downward by extending your elbows until your arms are fully extended.
Step:6 Pause for a moment, then inhale and slowly return to the starting position by allowing your elbows to flex.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: cable pushdown (with rope attachment)

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and knees slightly bent.
Step:2 Hold the cable handle in each hand with your palms facing inwards and your arms bent at a 90-degree angle.
Step:3 Keeping your upper arms stationary, extend your forearms backwards until your arms are fully extended.
Step:4 Pause for a moment, then slowly return to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: cable two arm tricep kickback

instructions: Step:1 Start by placing your hands on a raised surface, such as a bench or parallel bars, with your palms facing down and fingers pointing forward.
Step:2 Extend your legs out in front of you, keeping your heels on the ground and your body straight.
Step:3 Lower your body by bending your elbows, keeping them close to your sides, until your upper arms are parallel to the ground.
Step:4 Pause for a moment, then push through your palms to straighten your arms and lift your body back up to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: body-up

instructions: Step:1 Sit on a bench or chair with your knees bent and feet flat on the ground.
Step:2 Hold the cable handle with your right hand and place your elbow on the inside of your right knee.
Step:3 Extend your arm fully, keeping your elbow stationary and close to your knee.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your arm back to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch sides.
name: cable concentration extension (on knee)

instructions: Step:1 Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your head at the end of the bench.
Step:2 Hold the barbell with a reverse grip, palms facing towards your face, and your hands shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Extend your arms straight up over your chest, keeping your elbows in and your wrists straight.
Step:4 Slowly lower the barbell towards your forehead by bending your elbows, keeping your upper arms stationary.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the bottom, then extend your arms back up to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: barbell reverse grip skullcrusher

instructions: Step:1 Sit on a stability ball with your feet flat on the ground and your back straight.
Step:2 Hold a dumbbell in one hand and extend your arm straight up above your head.
Step:3 Bend your elbow and lower the dumbbell behind your head, keeping your upper arm close to your ear.
Step:4 Hold the stretch for a few seconds, then return to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat with the other arm.
name: exercise ball seated triceps stretch

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand.
Step:2 Take a step forward with your right foot, lowering your body into a lunge position.
Step:3 Keep your back straight and your chest up.
Step:4 Extend your arms straight overhead, keeping your elbows close to your ears.
Step:5 Lower the dumbbells behind your head by bending your elbows.
Step:6 Pause for a moment, then straighten your arms to return to the starting position.
Step:7 Repeat the movement for the desired number of repetitions, then switch legs and repeat.
name: dumbbell forward lunge triceps extension

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a dumbbell in each hand.
Step:2 Bend forward at the waist, keeping your back straight and your knees slightly bent.
Step:3 Extend your arms straight back, keeping your elbows close to your body.
Step:4 Slowly lower the dumbbells back down to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: dumbbell standing bent over two arm triceps extension

instructions: Step:1 Adjust the seat height and position yourself on the machine with your feet flat on the floor.
Step:2 Grasp the handles with an overhand grip, slightly narrower than shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Keep your back straight and your chest up as you pull the handles towards your body, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the peak of the movement, then slowly release the handles back to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: smith narrow row

instructions: Step:1 Attach a handle to a cable machine at waist height.
Step:2 Stand facing the machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Grasp the handle with an overhand grip, palms facing down.
Step:4 Step back to create tension on the cable, keeping your back straight and knees slightly bent.
Step:5 Pull the handle towards your body, rotating your palms to face upwards as you do so.
Step:6 Squeeze your shoulder blades together at the end of the movement.
Step:7 Slowly release the handle back to the starting position.
Step:8 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: cable palm rotational row

instructions: Step:1 Lie flat on a bench with your back and head supported, and your feet flat on the ground.
Step:2 Hold a dumbbell in one hand with your palm facing down, and extend your arm straight up above your shoulder.
Step:3 Keeping your upper arm stationary, slowly lower the dumbbell behind your head by bending your elbow.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the bottom, then extend your arm back up to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch arms.
name: dumbbell lying one arm pronated triceps extension

instructions: Step:1 Start in a push-up position with your hands placed close together, directly under your shoulders.
Step:2 Hold a dumbbell in each hand, resting them on the ground.
Step:3 Lower your body towards the ground by bending your elbows, keeping them close to your sides.
Step:4 Push through your palms to extend your arms and return to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: push-up close-grip off dumbbell

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in one hand.
Step:2 Raise the dumbbell overhead, fully extending your arm.
Step:3 Keep your upper arm close to your head and perpendicular to the ground.
Step:4 Slowly lower the dumbbell behind your head, bending your elbow.
Step:5 Pause for a moment, then raise the dumbbell back to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: dumbbell standing one arm extension

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a dumbbell in each hand.
Step:2 Bend your knees slightly and hinge forward at the hips, keeping your back straight.
Step:3 Extend your arms straight back, squeezing your triceps at the top of the movement.
Step:4 Pause for a moment, then slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: dumbbell standing kickback

instructions: Step:1 Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your back pressed against the bench.
Step:2 Grasp the barbell with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Lower the barbell to your chest, keeping your elbows tucked in close to your body.
Step:4 Push the barbell back up to the starting position, fully extending your arms.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: barbell jm bench press

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand.
Step:2 Bend your knees slightly and hinge forward at the hips, keeping your back straight.
Step:3 Extend your arms straight back, keeping your elbows close to your body.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat with the other arm, alternating sides with each repetition.
name: dumbbell standing alternating tricep kickback

instructions: Step:1 Adjust the seat height and position yourself on the machine with your back against the pad.
Step:2 Grasp the handles with an overhand grip and fully extend your arms in front of you.
Step:3 Keeping your upper arms stationary, slowly lower the handles towards your forehead by bending your elbows.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push the handles back up to the starting position by extending your arms.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: lever triceps extension

instructions: Step:1 Sit on a bench with your hands gripping the edge, fingers pointing forward.
Step:2 Slide your butt off the bench, supporting your weight with your hands.
Step:3 Lower your body by bending your elbows until your upper arms are parallel to the floor.
Step:4 Push yourself back up to the starting position by straightening your arms.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: weighted bench dip

instructions: Step:1 Sit on an exercise ball with your feet flat on the ground and your back straight.
Step:2 Hold a dumbbell in one hand with your palm facing up and your elbow bent at a 90-degree angle.
Step:3 Extend your arm straight up towards the ceiling, keeping your elbow stationary.
Step:4 Slowly lower the dumbbell back down to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch arms.
name: dumbbell one arm french press on exercise ball

instructions: Step:1 Set up an incline bench at a 45-degree angle.
Step:2 Lie back on the bench and grasp the barbell with a reverse grip, hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Unrack the barbell and lower it towards your upper chest, keeping your elbows tucked in.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push the barbell back up to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: barbell incline reverse-grip press

instructions: Step:1 Set up an incline bench at a 45-degree angle.
Step:2 Grab a dumbbell in each hand and sit on the bench with your chest against the incline.
Step:3 Extend your arms fully, allowing the dumbbells to hang straight down from your shoulders.
Step:4 Pull the dumbbells up towards your chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: dumbbell incline row

instructions: Step:1 Sit on the edge of a bench or chair with your hands gripping the edge, fingers pointing forward.
Step:2 Slide your butt off the bench, supporting your weight with your hands.
Step:3 Lower your body by bending your elbows until your upper arms are parallel to the floor.
Step:4 Push yourself back up to the starting position by straightening your arms.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: bench dip on floor

instructions: Step:1 Sit on a bench with your back straight and feet flat on the ground.
Step:2 Hold a dumbbell in one hand and place your other hand on the bench for support.
Step:3 Raise the dumbbell overhead, keeping your upper arm close to your head and your elbow pointing forward.
Step:4 Lower the dumbbell behind your head by bending your elbow, keeping your upper arm stationary.
Step:5 Extend your arm back up to the starting position, fully straightening your elbow.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch arms.
name: dumbbell one arm triceps extension (on bench)

instructions: Step:1 Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your back pressed against the bench.
Step:2 Grasp the barbell with a reverse grip, hands shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Lift the barbell off the rack and hold it directly above your chest with your arms fully extended.
Step:4 Slowly lower the barbell down towards your chest, keeping your elbows close to your body.
Step:5 Pause for a moment when the barbell is just above your chest.
Step:6 Push the barbell back up to the starting position, fully extending your arms.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: barbell reverse close-grip bench press

instructions: Step:1 Adjust the bench on the smith machine to a decline position.
Step:2 Lie down on the bench with your feet firmly planted on the ground.
Step:3 Grasp the barbell with a close grip, slightly narrower than shoulder-width apart.
Step:4 Unrack the barbell and lower it slowly towards your chest, keeping your elbows close to your body.
Step:5 Pause for a moment when the barbell is just above your chest.
Step:6 Push the barbell back up to the starting position, fully extending your arms.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: smith machine decline close grip bench press

instructions: Step:1 Attach a straight bar to the cable machine at the highest setting.
Step:2 Stand facing the cable machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Grasp the bar with an underhand grip, palms facing up, and your hands shoulder-width apart.
Step:4 Keep your elbows close to your sides and your upper arms stationary throughout the exercise.
Step:5 Engage your triceps and slowly push the bar down until your arms are fully extended.
Step:6 Pause for a moment at the bottom, then slowly return the bar to the starting position.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: cable reverse grip triceps pushdown (sz-bar) (with arm blaster)

instructions: Step:1 Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your head at the end of the bench.
Step:2 Hold the barbell with an overhand grip, hands shoulder-width apart, and extend your arms straight up over your chest.
Step:3 Keeping your upper arms stationary, slowly lower the barbell towards your forehead by bending your elbows.
Step:4 Pause for a moment when the barbell is just above your forehead, then extend your arms back up to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: barbell lying triceps extension skull crusher

instructions: Step:1 Start in a high plank position with your hands close together, forming a diamond shape with your thumbs and index fingers.
Step:2 Keep your body in a straight line from head to toe, engaging your core and glutes.
Step:3 Lower your chest towards the diamond shape formed by your hands, keeping your elbows close to your body.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push yourself back up to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: diamond push-up

instructions: Step:1 Sit on a bench with your back straight and feet flat on the ground.
Step:2 Hold a barbell with an overhand grip, hands shoulder-width apart, and raise it overhead.
Step:3 Lower the barbell behind your head by bending your elbows, keeping your upper arms close to your head.
Step:4 Pause for a moment, then extend your arms to raise the barbell back to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: barbell seated overhead triceps extension

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold the ez barbell with an overhand grip.
Step:2 Raise the barbell above your head, fully extending your arms.
Step:3 Keeping your upper arms close to your head, slowly lower the barbell behind your head by bending your elbows.
Step:4 Pause for a moment, then extend your arms back to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: ez bar standing french press

instructions: Step:1 Adjust the seat height and footrests to a comfortable position.
Step:2 Grasp the handles with an overhand grip, palms facing down.
Step:3 Sit up straight with your feet flat on the footrests.
Step:4 Extend your arms straight out in front of you, keeping your elbows slightly bent.
Step:5 Engage your triceps and push the handles down towards your thighs.
Step:6 Pause for a moment at the bottom, then slowly return to the starting position.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: ski ergometer

instructions: Step:1 Sit on the machine with your feet flat on the footrests and your knees slightly bent.
Step:2 Grasp the handles with an overhand grip, palms facing down.
Step:3 Keep your back straight and lean slightly forward, maintaining a slight bend in your elbows.
Step:4 Pull the handles towards your body, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the peak of the movement, then slowly release the handles back to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: cable low seated row

instructions: Step:1 Stand facing away from a bench or chair, with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step:2 Place one hand on the bench or chair behind you, fingers pointing towards your body.
Step:3 Extend your legs out in front of you, keeping your heels on the ground.
Step:4 Bend your elbows and lower your body towards the ground, keeping your back close to the bench or chair.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push through your palms to straighten your arms and return to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch sides and repeat with the other arm.
name: one arm dip

instructions: Step:1 Sit on a flat bench with a dumbbell in each hand, resting on your thighs.
Step:2 Using your thighs to help raise the dumbbells, lift the dumbbells one at a time so that you can hold them in front of you at shoulder width.
Step:3 Once at shoulder width, rotate your wrists forward so that the palms of your hands are facing away from you. This will be your starting position.
Step:4 As you breathe in, slowly lower the dumbbells to your side until they are about level with your chest.
Step:5 As you exhale, use your triceps to lift the dumbbells back to the starting position. Make sure to use only your triceps and do not use your forearms or biceps to help lift the dumbbells.
Step:6 After a second pause at the contracted position, repeat the movement for the prescribed amount of repetitions.
name: dumbbell close grip press

instructions: Step:1 Set up an incline bench at a 45-degree angle.
Step:2 Lie down on the bench with your feet flat on the ground.
Step:3 Grasp the barbell with a close grip, slightly narrower than shoulder-width apart.
