Step:4 Unrack the barbell and lower it slowly towards your chest, keeping your elbows close to your body.
Step:5 Pause for a moment when the barbell touches your chest.
Step:6 Push the barbell back up to the starting position, fully extending your arms.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: barbell incline close grip bench press

instructions: Step:1 Sit on a bench with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing inwards.
Step:2 Bend forward at the waist, keeping your back straight and parallel to the ground.
Step:3 Extend one arm straight back, keeping it close to your body, until your arm is fully extended.
Step:4 Pause for a moment, then slowly lower the dumbbell back to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat with the other arm.
Step:6 Continue alternating arms for the desired number of repetitions.
name: dumbbell seated bent over alternate kickback

instructions: Step:1 Adjust the seat height so that your feet are flat on the ground and your knees are at a 90-degree angle.
Step:2 Grasp the handles of the leverage machine with your palms facing down and your arms fully extended.
Step:3 Slowly lower your body by bending your elbows until your upper arms are parallel to the ground.
Step:4 Pause for a moment, then push yourself back up to the starting position by straightening your arms.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: lever seated dip

instructions: Step:1 Set up an incline bench at a 45-degree angle.
Step:2 Sit on the bench with your back against the backrest and feet flat on the ground.
Step:3 Hold a dumbbell in each hand with an overhand grip, palms facing inwards.
Step:4 Extend your arms straight up above your chest, keeping a slight bend in your elbows.
Step:5 Lower the dumbbells slowly towards your shoulders, keeping your elbows close to your body.
Step:6 Pause for a moment at the bottom, then press the dumbbells back up to the starting position.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: dumbbell palms in incline bench press

instructions: Step:1 Position yourself between two parallel bars with your arms fully extended and your body straight.
Step:2 Lower your body by bending your elbows until your upper arms are parallel to the ground.
Step:3 Pause for a moment, then push yourself back up to the starting position by straightening your arms.
Step:4 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: reverse dip

instructions: Step:1 Sit on a bench with your back straight and feet flat on the ground.
Step:2 Hold a dumbbell with an underhand grip and extend your arm straight up overhead.
Step:3 Lower the dumbbell behind your head by bending your elbow, keeping your upper arm stationary.
Step:4 Pause for a moment, then extend your arm back up to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch arms.
name: dumbbell seated reverse grip one arm overhead tricep extension

instructions: Step:1 Sit on the edge of a bench or chair with your hands gripping the edge, fingers pointing forward.
Step:2 Slide your butt off the bench, supporting your weight with your hands.
Step:3 Bend your elbows and lower your body towards the ground, keeping your back close to the bench.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push yourself back up to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: triceps dip

instructions: Step:1 Lie on a decline bench with your head lower than your feet.
Step:2 Grasp the ez barbell with a close grip, palms facing each other.
Step:3 Extend your arms straight up above your chest, keeping your elbows close to your body.
Step:4 Lower the barbell towards your forehead by bending your elbows.
Step:5 Pause for a moment, then press the barbell back up to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: ez barbell decline close grip face press

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, knees slightly bent, and hold a kettlebell in each hand with your palms facing your body.
Step:2 Bend forward at the hips, keeping your back straight and your core engaged.
Step:3 Pull the kettlebells up towards your chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the kettlebells back down to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: kettlebell two arm row

instructions: Step:1 Lie flat on a bench with a dumbbell in one hand and your arm fully extended above your chest.
Step:2 Lower the dumbbell in a controlled manner towards your forehead, keeping your upper arm stationary.
Step:3 Pause briefly at the bottom of the movement, then extend your arm back to the starting position.
Step:4 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch arms.
name: dumbbell lying single extension

instructions: Step:1 Sit on an incline bench with a dumbbell in one hand, resting on your thigh.
Step:2 Lean back on the bench and use your thigh to help raise the dumbbell to shoulder height.
Step:3 Rotate your wrist so that your palm is facing inward, towards your body.
Step:4 Press the dumbbell up and away from your body, extending your arm fully.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbell back down to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch arms.
name: dumbbell incline one arm hammer press

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a dumbbell in your right hand.
Step:2 Bend your knees slightly and hinge forward at the hips, keeping your back straight.
Step:3 Bring your right elbow up to your side, keeping it bent at a 90-degree angle.
Step:4 Extend your right arm straight back, squeezing your triceps at the top of the movement.
Step:5 Slowly lower the dumbbell back to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch sides.
name: dumbbell one arm kickback

instructions: Step:1 Sit on a bench with your back straight and feet flat on the ground.
Step:2 Hold a dumbbell in both hands with an overhand grip, and raise it above your head.
Step:3 Bend your elbows and lower the dumbbell behind your head, keeping your upper arms close to your ears.
Step:4 Extend your arms and raise the dumbbell back to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: dumbbells seated triceps extension

instructions: Step:1 Sit on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and hold a dumbbell in each hand.
Step:2 Bend your knees slightly and lean forward from your hips, keeping your back straight.
Step:3 Bring your upper arms close to your sides and keep your elbows bent at a 90-degree angle.
Step:4 Extend your arms straight back, squeezing your triceps at the top of the movement.
Step:5 Pause for a moment, then slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: dumbbell seated kickback

instructions: Step:1 Sit on an exercise ball with your feet flat on the ground and your knees bent at a 90-degree angle.
Step:2 Hold a dumbbell with both hands and extend your arms straight up towards the ceiling.
Step:3 Slowly lower the dumbbell behind your head, keeping your elbows close to your ears.
Step:4 Pause for a moment, then raise the dumbbell back up to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: exercise ball supine triceps extension

instructions: Step:1 Sit on an exercise ball and hold an EZ barbell with an overhand grip.
Step:2 Extend your arms straight up, keeping your elbows close to your head.
Step:3 Slowly lower the barbell behind your head by bending your elbows.
Step:4 Pause for a moment, then extend your arms back up to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: ez bar french press on exercise ball

instructions: Step:1 Lie on a decline bench with a dumbbell in one hand, resting on your chest.
Step:2 Extend your arm straight up, keeping your elbow slightly bent.
Step:3 Lower the dumbbell down towards your shoulder, keeping your elbow close to your body.
Step:4 Press the dumbbell back up to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch arms.
name: dumbbell decline one arm hammer press

instructions: Step:1 Attach a handle to a low pulley cable machine and stand facing away from the machine.
Step:2 Grasp the handle with an overhand grip and extend your arms straight out in front of you.
Step:3 Keeping your elbows stationary, pull the handle back towards your body, squeezing your triceps at the end of the movement.
Step:4 Slowly return the handle to the starting position and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: cable rear drive

instructions: Step:1 Find a wall and face away from it, standing a few feet away.
Step:2 Place your hands on the ground shoulder-width apart and kick your feet up against the wall, coming into a handstand position.
Step:3 Bend your elbows and lower your head towards the ground, keeping your body in a straight line.
Step:4 Push through your hands and extend your arms to return to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: handstand push-up

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a towel in front of you with your palms facing down.
Step:2 Bend your knees and lower your body into a squat position, keeping your back straight and your chest up.
Step:3 As you lower into the squat, simultaneously pull the towel towards your chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the bottom of the squat, then slowly return to the starting position while extending your arms.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: bodyweight squatting row (with towel)

instructions: Step:1 Attach a rope handle to a high pulley and kneel down facing the cable machine.
Step:2 Grasp the rope with a neutral grip (palms facing each other) and bring your hands to the sides of your head.
Step:3 Keep your elbows close to your head and your upper arms stationary throughout the exercise.
Step:4 Extend your forearms by contracting your triceps until your arms are fully extended.
Step:5 Pause for a moment, then slowly return to the starting position by bending your elbows.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: cable kneeling triceps extension

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and your knees slightly bent.
Step:2 Extend your arms straight out in front of you, parallel to the ground.
Step:3 Bend your elbows and lower your body towards the ground, keeping your upper arms close to your sides.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push yourself back up to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: triceps press

instructions: Step:1 Position yourself between two parallel bars with your arms fully extended and your body suspended in the air.
Step:2 Bend your knees and cross your ankles.
Step:3 Lower your body by bending your elbows until your upper arms are parallel to the ground.
Step:4 Pause for a moment, then push yourself back up to the starting position by straightening your arms.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: impossible dips

instructions: Step:1 Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your head at the end of the bench.
Step:2 Hold the barbell with an overhand grip, hands shoulder-width apart, and extend your arms straight up over your chest.
Step:3 Keeping your upper arms stationary, slowly lower the barbell towards your forehead by bending your elbows. Focus on double movement.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the bottom, then extend your arms back up to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: barbell lying triceps extension double

instructions: Step:1 Sit on a bench or chair with your back straight and feet flat on the ground.
Step:2 Hold a dumbbell with both hands, palms facing down, and extend your arms straight up overhead.
Step:3 Keeping your upper arms close to your head and elbows pointing forward, slowly lower the dumbbell behind your head by bending your elbows.
Step:4 Pause for a moment, then extend your arms back up to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions. Focus on rotated movement.
name: rotated dumbbell pronate-grip triceps extension

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold the band with both hands, palms facing down.
Step:2 Extend your arms straight out to the sides, keeping them parallel to the ground.
Step:3 Slowly bend your elbows and bring your hands towards your shoulders, keeping your upper arms still.
Step:4 Pause for a moment, then slowly extend your arms back out to the starting position.
Step:5 (dynamic variation) Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: dynamic style band side triceps extension

instructions: Step:1 Sit on a stability ball with your feet flat on the ground and your back straight.
Step:2 Hold a dumbbell in one hand and extend your arm straight up above your head.
Step:3 Bend your elbow and lower the dumbbell behind your head, keeping your upper arm close to your ear. Maintain wide form throughout.
Step:4 Hold the stretch for a few seconds, then return to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat with the other arm.
name: exercise ball seated triceps stretch wide

instructions: Step:1 Stand facing away from a bench or chair, with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step:2 Place one hand on the bench or chair behind you, fingers pointing towards your body. Maintain endurance form throughout.
Step:3 Extend your legs out in front of you, keeping your heels on the ground.
Step:4 Bend your elbows and lower your body towards the ground, keeping your back close to the bench or chair.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push through your palms to straighten your arms and return to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch sides and repeat with the other arm.
name: endurance style one arm dip

instructions: Step:1 Attach a v-bar attachment to the cable machine at the highest setting.
Step:2 Stand facing the cable machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Grasp the v-bar with an overhand grip, palms facing down, and your hands shoulder-width apart.
Step:4 Keep your elbows close to your sides and your upper arms stationary throughout the exercise.
Step:5 Engage your triceps and exhale as you push the v-bar down until your arms are fully extended. Perform with flexible intensity.
Step:6 Pause for a moment at the bottom of the movement, squeezing your triceps.
Step:7 Inhale as you slowly return the v-bar to the starting position, maintaining control.
Step:8 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: flexible cable triceps pushdown (v-bar)

instructions: Step:1 Attach a straight bar to a high pulley cable machine.
Step:2 Stand facing the machine with your feet shoulder-width apart and a slight bend in your knees.
Step:3 Grasp the bar with an overhand grip, hands shoulder-width apart.
Step:4 Keep your elbows close to your sides and your upper arms stationary.
Step:5 Exhale and push the bar down until your elbows are fully extended.
Step:6 Pause for a moment, then inhale and slowly return the bar to the starting position. Maintain active form throughout.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: active style cable pushdown

instructions: Step:1 Adjust the seat height and position yourself facing the machine.
Step:2 Grasp the handles with an overhand grip and keep your back straight.
Step:3 Pull the handles towards your body, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the peak of the movement, then slowly release and extend your arms back to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: lever unilateral row

instructions: Step:1 Lie on a decline bench with your head lower than your feet.
Step:2 Grasp the ez barbell with a close grip, palms facing each other.
Step:3 Extend your arms straight up above your chest, keeping your elbows close to your body.
Step:4 Lower the barbell towards your forehead by bending your elbows.
Step:5 Pause for a moment, then press the barbell back up to the starting position. Emphasize mini control.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: ez barbell decline close grip face press - mini variation

instructions: Step:1 Adjust the seat height and position yourself on the bench with your feet flat on the ground.
Step:2 Grasp the barbell with a close grip, slightly narrower than shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Lower the barbell towards your chest, keeping your elbows close to your body. Focus on targeted movement.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push the barbell back up to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: targeted style smith close-grip bench press

instructions: Step:1 Start in a high plank position with your hands on the medicine ball, shoulder-width apart.
Step:2 Lower your body towards the ground by bending your elbows, keeping them close to your sides.
Step:3 Push back up to the starting position, fully extending your arms.
Step:4 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions. Maintain prone form throughout.
name: prone medicine ball close grip push up

instructions: Step:1 Sit on a bench with your hands gripping the edge of the bench, fingers pointing forward.
Step:2 (extreme variation) Slide your butt off the bench, supporting your weight with your hands.
Step:3 Bend your elbows and lower your body towards the ground, keeping your back close to the bench.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push yourself back up to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: triceps dip (between benches) - extreme variation

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, facing the cable machine.
Step:2 Hold the cable handle with your right hand, palm facing down, and position your arm so that it is fully extended and parallel to the ground.
Step:3 Keep your elbow stationary and close to your body.
Step:4 Slowly bend your elbow, lowering the cable handle towards the back of your head.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the bottom of the movement, then extend your arm back to the starting position. Focus on rough movement.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch sides and perform the exercise with your left arm.
name: rough cable standing one arm triceps extension

instructions: Step:1 Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your head at the end of the bench.
Step:2 Hold the ez barbell with a close grip, palms facing up, and extend your arms straight up over your chest.
Step:3 Keeping your upper arms stationary, slowly lower the barbell behind your head by bending your elbows.
Step:4 Pause for a moment, then extend your arms back up to the starting position. Maintain complete form throughout.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: complete style ez bar lying close grip triceps extension behind head

instructions: Step:1 Adjust the seat of the smith machine so that the bar is at shoulder height.
Step:2 Sit on the bench with your back against the pad and your feet flat on the ground.
Step:3 Grasp the bar with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:4 Extend your arms fully, lifting the bar off the rack and holding it directly above your chest. Focus on straight movement.
Step:5 Lower the bar slowly towards your forehead, keeping your elbows close to your head.
Step:6 Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push the bar back up to the starting position.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: smith machine incline tricep extension - straight variation

instructions: Step:1 Start in a plank position with your forearms on the ground and elbows directly below your shoulders.
Step:2 Engage your core and keep your body in a straight line from head to toe.
Step:3 Lower your chest towards the ground by bending your elbows, keeping them close to your body. Emphasize resistance control.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push yourself back up to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: push-up on lower arms - resistance variation

instructions: Step:1 Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your head at the end of the bench.
Step:2 Hold a barbell with an overhand grip, hands shoulder-width apart, and extend your arms straight up over your chest.
Step:3 Keeping your upper arms stationary, slowly lower the barbell behind your head by bending your elbows. Maintain dynamic form throughout.
Step:4 Pause for a moment, then extend your arms back up to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: dynamic style barbell lying back of the head tricep extension

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a towel with both hands behind your head.
Step:2 Keep your elbows close to your ears and your upper arms stationary.
Step:3 (horizontal variation) Slowly extend your forearms upward, squeezing your triceps at the top.
Step:4 Pause for a moment, then slowly lower the towel back down to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: assisted standing triceps extension (with towel) with horizontal

instructions: Step:1 Set up a bar at waist height or use a suspension trainer.
Step:2 Stand facing the bar or suspension trainer, with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Grab the bar or handles with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:4 Lean back, keeping your body straight and your heels on the ground.
Step:5 Pull your chest towards the bar or handles, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:6 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower yourself back to the starting position.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: inverted row

instructions: Step:1 Sit on an exercise ball with your feet flat on the ground and your back straight.
Step:2 Hold a dumbbell in one hand with your palm facing up and your elbow bent at a 90-degree angle. Focus on wall movement.
Step:3 Extend your arm straight up towards the ceiling, keeping your elbow stationary.
Step:4 Slowly lower the dumbbell back down to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch arms.
name: wall style dumbbell one arm french press on exercise ball

instructions: Step:1 Start in a high plank position with your hands placed close together, directly under your shoulders.
Step:2 Engage your core and lower your body towards the ground, keeping your elbows close to your sides.
Step:3 Push through your palms to extend your arms and return to the starting position. Maintain macro form throughout.
Step:4 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: macro style close-grip push-up

instructions: Step:1 Attach a rope to a high pulley and adjust the incline bench to a comfortable angle.
Step:2 Stand facing away from the pulley with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Grasp the rope with an overhand grip and extend your arms straight overhead.
Step:4 (reverse variation) Keep your elbows close to your head and your upper arms stationary throughout the exercise.
Step:5 Lower the rope behind your head by bending your elbows until your forearms touch your biceps.
Step:6 Pause for a moment, then extend your arms back to the starting position.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: cable rope incline tricep extension - reverse variation

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and place the band under your feet, holding the ends with your hands.
Step:2 Keep your arms straight and relaxed, and let the band hang in front of your thighs.
Step:3 Engage your traps by shrugging your shoulders upward, lifting the band as high as possible.
Step:4 Hold the contraction for a moment, then slowly lower your shoulders back down to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: band shrug

instructions: Step:1 Stand facing the cable machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step:2 Grasp the cable handles with an overhand grip and let your arms hang down in front of you.
Step:3 Keeping your arms straight, shrug your shoulders up towards your ears.
Step:4 Hold the contraction for a moment, then slowly lower your shoulders back down to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: cable shrug

instructions: Step:1 Adjust the seat height and position yourself on the machine with your back against the pad.
Step:2 Grasp the handles or bars with an overhand grip, keeping your arms straight.
Step:3 Keeping your back straight, lift your shoulders up towards your ears as high as possible.
Step:4 Hold the contraction for a moment, then slowly lower your shoulders back down to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: lever gripless shrug v. 2

instructions: Step:1 Start by standing with your feet shoulder-width apart and your arms extended in front of you.
Step:2 Bend your knees slightly and hinge forward at the hips, keeping your back straight.
Step:3 Lower your body by bending your elbows and retracting your shoulder blades, as if you are trying to squeeze a pencil between them.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push through your hands to extend your elbows and return to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: scapula dips

instructions: Step:1 Set up a decline bench at a 45-degree angle.
Step:2 Lie face down on the bench with your chest and stomach resting against it.
Step:3 Hold a dumbbell in each hand with your arms fully extended towards the floor.
Step:4 Keeping your arms straight, raise your shoulders towards your ears as high as possible.
Step:5 Hold the contraction for a moment, then slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: dumbbell decline shrug

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet wider than shoulder-width apart, toes pointing outwards.
Step:2 Hold a kettlebell with both hands in front of your body, arms extended downwards.
Step:3 Bend your knees and lower your hips into a squat position, keeping your back straight.
Step:4 Drive through your heels and explosively extend your hips and knees, pulling the kettlebell up towards your chin.
Step:5 As you pull the kettlebell up, keep your elbows high and wide, and squeeze your shoulder blades together.
Step:6 Lower the kettlebell back down to the starting position and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: kettlebell sumo high pull

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a barbell in front of you with an overhand grip.
Step:2 Keep your arms straight and your back straight throughout the exercise.
Step:3 Lift your shoulders up towards your ears as high as possible, squeezing your traps at the top.
Step:4 Hold for a moment, then slowly lower your shoulders back down to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: barbell shrug

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a towel in front of you with both hands.
Step:2 Bend your knees slightly and hinge forward at the hips, keeping your back straight.
Step:3 Pull the towel towards your chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly release the tension and return to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: bodyweight standing row (with towel)

instructions: Step:1 Adjust the seat height and position yourself on the leverage machine with your back against the pad.
Step:2 Grasp the handles with an overhand grip and keep your arms straight.
Step:3 Keeping your back straight, lift your shoulders up towards your ears as high as possible.
Step:4 Hold the contraction for a moment, then slowly lower your shoulders back down to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: lever shrug

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a dumbbell in each hand with your palms facing your body.
Step:2 Keep your arms straight and let the dumbbells hang by your sides.
Step:3 Raise your shoulders as high as possible, as if you are trying to touch your ears with your shoulders.
Step:4 Hold the contraction for a second, then slowly lower your shoulders back down to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: dumbbell shrug

instructions: Step:1 Adjust the seat height and position yourself on the leverage machine.
Step:2 Grasp the handles with your palms facing inwards and your arms fully extended.
Step:3 Keeping your back straight, exhale and elevate your shoulders as high as possible.
Step:4 Hold the contraction for a brief moment, then inhale and slowly lower your shoulders back down to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: lever gripless shrug

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and your knees slightly bent.
Step:2 Grasp the barbell with an overhand grip, hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Keep your arms straight and your shoulders relaxed.
Step:4 Lift your shoulders up towards your ears, squeezing your traps at the top.
Step:5 Hold for a moment, then slowly lower your shoulders back down to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: smith shrug

instructions: Step:1 Set an incline bench to a 45-degree angle and sit on it with a dumbbell in each hand.
Step:2 Place your feet flat on the ground and let your arms hang straight down with your palms facing your body.
Step:3 Keeping your arms straight, shrug your shoulders up towards your ears as high as possible.
Step:4 Hold the contraction for a moment, then slowly lower your shoulders back down to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: dumbbell incline shrug

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet hip-width apart and place a resistance band around your ankles.
Step:2 Take a big step forward with your right foot and a smaller step back with your left foot.
Step:3 Bend your knees and lower your body until your right thigh is parallel to the ground, keeping your left knee slightly above the ground.
Step:4 Push through your right heel to return to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat on the other side.
name: band single leg split squat

instructions: Step:1 Start with the barbell resting on your shoulders, with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step:2 Lower your body into a squat position, keeping your chest up and your knees tracking over your toes.
Step:3 As you reach the bottom of the squat, explosively drive through your legs and push the barbell overhead.
Step:4 As the barbell reaches its peak, quickly drop into a split position, with one foot forward and one foot back.
Step:5 Catch the barbell overhead with your arms fully extended and your back knee slightly touching the ground.
Step:6 Stand up from the split position, bringing your feet back together.
Step:7 Lower the barbell back to your shoulders and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: squat jerk

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand.
Step:2 Take a step forward with one foot and position your feet so that your front foot is flat on the ground and your back foot is elevated on a bench or step.
Step:3 Lower your body by bending your front knee and hip, keeping your back knee slightly bent and your back heel off the ground.
Step:4 Continue lowering until your front thigh is parallel to the ground, then push through your front heel to return to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch legs and repeat.
name: dumbbell single leg split squat

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and your arms by your sides.
Step:2 Begin by rapidly moving your feet up and down, as if you were running in place.
Step:3 Keep your movements quick and light, focusing on staying on the balls of your feet.
Step:4 Continue for the desired duration or number of repetitions.
name: quick feet v. 2

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step:2 Take a step forward with one foot and place it about two feet in front of the other foot.
Step:3 Lower your body by bending your knees and hips, keeping your back straight.
Step:4 Continue lowering until your front thigh is parallel to the ground, and your back knee is hovering just above the ground.
Step:5 Pause for a moment, then push through your front heel to return to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch legs and repeat.
name: split squats

instructions: Step:1 Start by lying on your side with your legs extended and stacked on top of each other.
Step:2 Place your forearm on the ground directly below your shoulder, keeping your elbow bent at a 90-degree angle.
Step:3 Hold a dumbbell in your top hand, with your arm extended straight down towards the ground.
Step:4 Engage your core and lift your hips off the ground, creating a straight line from your head to your heels.
Step:5 While maintaining the side plank position, lift the dumbbell up towards the ceiling, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:6 Lower the dumbbell back down to the starting position.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch sides.
name: dumbbell side plank with rear fly

instructions: Step:1 Start by standing with your feet shoulder-width apart and the barbell resting on your upper chest, just below your collarbone.
Step:2 Hold the barbell with an overhand grip, keeping your elbows up and your upper arms parallel to the ground.
Step:3 Lower your body down into a squat position by bending at the knees and hips, keeping your back straight and your chest up.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the bottom of the squat, then push through your heels to return to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: barbell bench front squat

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet wider than shoulder-width apart, toes pointing slightly outwards.
Step:2 Hold a barbell across your upper back, resting it on your shoulders.
Step:3 Take a big step to the side with your right foot, keeping your left foot planted.
Step:4 Bend your right knee and lower your body down into a squat position, keeping your chest up and your back straight.
Step:5 Push through your right heel to return to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat on the other side, stepping out with your left foot.
Step:7 Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.
name: barbell side split squat v. 2

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and your toes pointing slightly outward.
Step:2 Hold a weight in front of your chest with both hands, or place a barbell across your upper back.
Step:3 Keeping your chest up and your core engaged, slowly lower your body down by bending at the knees and hips.
Step:4 Continue lowering until your thighs are parallel to the ground or as low as you can comfortably go.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push through your heels to return to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: weighted sissy squat

instructions: Step:1 Stand in front of a step or platform with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing your sides.
Step:2 Place your right foot on the step, ensuring your entire foot is on the surface.
Step:3 Push through your right heel and lift your body up onto the step, bringing your left foot up as well.
Step:4 Once both feet are on the step, lower your left foot back down to the starting position, keeping your right foot on the step.
Step:5 Repeat the movement, alternating which foot you step up with each time.
Step:6 Continue for the desired number of repetitions.
name: dumbbell step-up lunge

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet wider than shoulder-width apart, toes pointing slightly outward.
Step:2 Hold the barbell across your upper back, resting it on your traps or rear delts.
Step:3 Engage your core and keep your chest up as you lower your body down into a squat, pushing your hips back and bending your knees.
Step:4 Lower until your thighs are parallel to the ground, or as low as you can comfortably go.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push through your heels to return to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: barbell wide squat

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step:2 Bend your knees and lower your body into a squat position.
Step:3 Swing your arms back for momentum.
Step:4 Jump forward explosively, extending your hips, knees, and ankles.
Step:5 Land softly on the balls of your feet and immediately go into the next jump.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: forward jump

instructions: Step:1 Attach the resistance band to a sturdy anchor point and secure it around your ankle.
Step:2 Stand facing the anchor point with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Keeping your core engaged and your upper body stable, extend your leg straight out in front of you.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly return your leg to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch legs.
name: resistance band leg extension

instructions: Step:1 Place the balance board on a flat surface.
Step:2 Step onto the balance board with one foot, ensuring it is centered.
Step:3 Slowly shift your weight onto the foot on the balance board, keeping your core engaged.
Step:4 Maintain your balance and stability as you hold the position for a desired amount of time.
Step:5 Repeat the exercise with the other foot.
name: balance board

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet hip-width apart and place a resistance band around your ankles.
Step:2 Extend one leg forward and rest the top of your foot on a bench or step behind you.
Step:3 Hold onto a support with one hand for balance.
Step:4 Bend your standing leg and lower your body down into a squat position, keeping your knee in line with your toes.
Step:5 Push through your heel to return to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch legs.
name: band one arm single leg split squat

instructions: Step:1 Adjust the seat height and backrest of the machine to fit your body.
Step:2 Sit on the machine with your back against the backrest and your feet on the footpad.
Step:3 Grasp the handles or sidebars for stability.
Step:4 Extend your legs forward by straightening your knees, lifting the weight.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the weight back to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: lever leg extension

instructions: Step:1 Hang from a pull-up bar with a narrow parallel grip, palms facing towards you.
Step:2 Engage your back muscles and pull your body up towards the bar, keeping your elbows close to your body.
Step:3 Continue pulling until your chin is above the bar.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your body back down to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: chin-ups (narrow parallel grip)

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and toes slightly turned out.
Step:2 Lower your body down into a squat position by bending your knees and pushing your hips back.
Step:3 As you come up from the squat, extend your arms overhead, reaching towards the ceiling.
Step:4 Return to the starting position by lowering your arms and bending your knees to squat down again.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: squat to overhead reach

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and your toes pointing slightly outward.
Step:2 Hold onto a stable object for balance if needed.
Step:3 Slowly lower your body by bending your knees and leaning back, keeping your torso upright.
Step:4 Continue lowering until your thighs are parallel to the ground or as far as you can comfortably go.
Step:5 Pause for a moment, then push through your heels to return to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: sissy squat

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell vertically against your chest with both hands.
Step:2 Keeping your chest up and core engaged, lower your body down into a squat position by pushing your hips back and bending your knees.
Step:3 Continue lowering until your thighs are parallel to the ground, or as low as you can comfortably go.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push through your heels to return to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: dumbbell goblet squat

instructions: Step:1 Adjust the seat and foot platform of the leverage machine to your desired position.
Step:2 Sit on the machine with your back against the backrest and your feet on the foot platform.
Step:3 Place your hands on the handles or sides of the machine for stability.
Step:4 Push one foot against the foot platform, extending your leg until it is almost fully straight.
Step:5 Pause for a moment, then slowly bend your leg and return to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat with the other leg.
Step:7 Continue alternating legs for the desired number of repetitions.
name: lever alternate leg press

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a barbell across your upper back.
Step:2 Take a large step forward with one leg, keeping your torso upright.
Step:3 Lower your body by bending your front knee and hip, while keeping your back leg straight.
Step:4 Continue lowering until your front thigh is parallel to the ground.
Step:5 Pause for a moment, then push through your front heel to return to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch legs.
name: barbell single leg split squat

instructions: Step:1 Start on all fours with your hands directly under your shoulders and your knees directly under your hips.
Step:2 Extend one leg straight back, keeping your knee bent and your foot flexed.
Step:3 Slowly lower your hips towards the ground, feeling a stretch in your quads.
Step:4 Hold this position for 20-30 seconds.
Step:5 Switch legs and repeat the stretch on the other side.
name: all fours squad stretch

instructions: Step:1 Stand up straight with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing your sides.
Step:2 Keep your back straight and your shoulders back.
Step:3 Take small, controlled steps forward, maintaining an upright posture.
Step:4 Continue walking for the desired distance or time.
Step:5 To finish, stop walking and carefully lower the dumbbells to your sides.
name: farmers walk

instructions: Step:1 Stand in front of a bench or step with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing your body.
Step:2 Place your right foot on the bench or step, ensuring your entire foot is in contact with the surface.
Step:3 Step up onto the bench or step with your right foot, pushing through your heel to lift your body up.
Step:4 As you step up, simultaneously lift your left knee towards your chest.
Step:5 Pause at the top of the movement, then slowly lower your left foot back to the ground while keeping your right foot on the bench or step.
Step:6 Repeat the movement with your left foot on the bench or step.
Step:7 Continue alternating between your right and left foot for the desired number of repetitions.
name: dumbbell step-up split squat

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step:2 Lower your body by bending your knees and pushing your hips back as if sitting on a chair.
Step:3 Keep your chest up and your back straight.
Step:4 Lower yourself until your thighs are parallel to the ground.
Step:5 Push through your heels to return to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: quads

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step:2 Bend your knees slightly and jump backwards, pushing off with both feet.
Step:3 Land softly on the balls of your feet, bending your knees to absorb the impact.
Step:4 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: backward jump

instructions: Step:1 Set up an incline bench at a 45-degree angle.
Step:2 Lie face down on the bench with your chest against the pad and your feet flat on the ground.
Step:3 Grasp the barbell with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:4 Keep your back straight and your core engaged.
Step:5 Pull the barbell towards your chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:6 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the barbell back to the starting position.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: barbell incline row

instructions: Step:1 Adjust the seat height and position yourself on the machine with your chest against the pad and your feet flat on the floor.
Step:2 Grasp the handles with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Keep your back straight and engage your core.
Step:4 Pull the handles towards your chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the top of the movement, then slowly release and extend your arms back to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: lever t-bar reverse grip row

instructions: Step:1 Attach the resistance band to a sturdy anchor point and secure it around your ankle.
Step:2 Stand facing the anchor point with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Keeping your core engaged and your upper body stable, extend your leg straight out in front of you.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly return your leg to the starting position. Focus on declined movement.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch legs.
name: resistance band leg extension declined

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and your arms by your sides.
Step:2 Begin by rapidly moving your feet up and down, as if you were running in place.
Step:3 Keep your movements quick and light, focusing on staying on the balls of your feet.
Step:4 Continue for the desired duration or number of repetitions. Emphasize bent control.
name: quick feet v. 2 with bent

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and your toes pointing slightly outward.
Step:2 Hold onto a stable object for balance if needed.
Step:3 Slowly lower your body by bending your knees and leaning back, keeping your torso upright.
Step:4 Continue lowering until your thighs are parallel to the ground or as far as you can comfortably go.
Step:5 Pause for a moment, then push through your heels to return to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions. Emphasize isolation control.
name: sissy squat isolation

instructions: Step:1 Sit on the edge of a chair with your back straight and feet flat on the ground.
Step:2 Extend one leg straight out in front of you, keeping your heel on the ground.
Step:3 Lean forward slightly, feeling a stretch in your quadriceps.
Step:4 Hold this position for 20-30 seconds. Focus on modified movement.
Step:5 Switch legs and repeat the stretch.
name: chair leg extended stretch with modified

instructions: Step:1 Start on all fours with your hands directly under your shoulders and your knees directly under your hips.
Step:2 Extend one leg straight back, keeping your knee bent and your foot flexed. Maintain elevated form throughout.
Step:3 Slowly lower your hips towards the ground, feeling a stretch in your quads.
Step:4 Hold this position for 20-30 seconds.
Step:5 Switch legs and repeat the stretch on the other side.
name: all fours squad stretch - elevated variation

instructions: Step:1 Stand in front of a step or platform with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing your sides.
Step:2 Place your right foot on the step, ensuring your entire foot is on the surface.
Step:3 Push through your right heel and lift your body up onto the step, bringing your left foot up as well.
Step:4 (fluid variation) Once both feet are on the step, lower your left foot back down to the starting position, keeping your right foot on the step.
Step:5 Repeat the movement, alternating which foot you step up with each time.
Step:6 Continue for the desired number of repetitions.
name: fluid dumbbell step-up lunge

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step:2 Lower your body by bending your knees and pushing your hips back as if sitting on a chair.
Step:3 Keep your chest up and your back straight.
Step:4 Lower yourself until your thighs are parallel to the ground.
Step:5 Push through your heels to return to the starting position. Emphasize complex control.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: quads complex

instructions: Step:1 Start by kneeling on the ground with your knees hip-width apart and your toes pointing forward.
Step:2 Place a barbell across your shoulders, gripping it with an overhand grip and your hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Engage your core and keep your chest lifted as you slowly lower your body down by bending your knees, keeping your back straight. Perform with pure intensity.
Step:4 Continue lowering until your thighs are parallel to the ground, or as low as you can comfortably go.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push through your heels to return to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: barbell squat (on knees) with pure

instructions: Step:1 Set up a stability ball at an incline angle.
Step:2 Attach the cable handles to the high pulleys of a cable machine.
Step:3 Sit on the stability ball facing away from the machine, with your feet firmly planted on the ground.
Step:4 Grasp the cable handles with an overhand grip, palms facing forward.
Step:5 Lean forward slightly, keeping your back straight and core engaged.
Step:6 With a controlled motion, bring your arms out to the sides, keeping a slight bend in your elbows.
Step:7 Continue the motion until your arms are parallel to the ground.
Step:8 Pause for a moment, then slowly return to the starting position.
Step:9 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: cable incline fly (on stability ball)

instructions: Step:1 Adjust the seat height and position yourself on the machine with your back flat against the pad.
Step:2 Grasp the handles with an overhand grip and position your elbows at a 90-degree angle.
Step:3 Push the handles forward until your arms are fully extended, exhaling during the movement.
Step:4 Pause briefly at the end of the movement, then slowly return to the starting position, inhaling as you do so.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: lever chest press

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and your knees slightly bent.
Step:2 Extend your arms straight out in front of you at shoulder height, parallel to the ground.
Step:3 Rotate your torso to the right, keeping your arms extended and your back straight.
Step:4 As you rotate, extend your right arm forward and your left arm back, mimicking the motion of drawing a bowstring.
Step:5 Hold the position for a moment, then return to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat the motion, this time rotating your torso to the left and extending your left arm forward and your right arm back.
Step:7 Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.
name: standing archer

instructions: Step:1 Stand in the middle of a cable machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step:2 Hold the handles of the cables with your palms facing down and your arms extended straight out to the sides.
Step:3 Keeping your arms straight, bring your hands together in front of your body, crossing them over each other.
Step:4 Pause for a moment, then slowly return to the starting position, keeping your arms extended.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: cable standing up straight crossovers

instructions: Step:1 Adjust the machine to your desired height and secure your knees on the pad.
Step:2 Grasp the handles with a wide grip and keep your elbows slightly bent.
Step:3 Lower your body by bending your elbows until your upper arms are parallel to the floor.
Step:4 Push yourself back up to the starting position by extending your arms.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: assisted wide-grip chest dip (kneeling)

instructions: Step:1 Grab the parallel bars with your palms facing down and your arms fully extended.
Step:2 Bend your knees and cross your ankles.
Step:3 Lower your body by bending your arms until your shoulders are below your elbows.
Step:4 Push yourself back up to the starting position by straightening your arms.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: chest dip on straight bar

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a barbell with an overhand grip, palms facing down.
Step:2 Keep your arms straight and raise the barbell in front of you until it reaches shoulder height.
Step:3 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the barbell back down to the starting position.
Step:4 Next, lower the barbell behind your head, keeping your arms straight.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the bottom, then raise the barbell back up to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: barbell front raise and pullover

instructions: Step:1 Start in a push-up position with your hands placed slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:2 Engage your core and lower your body down towards the ground, keeping your elbows close to your sides.
Step:3 As you lower yourself, lean your body forward and lift your feet off the ground, balancing on your hands.
Step:4 Continue to lower your body until your chest is just above the ground.
Step:5 Push through your hands and extend your arms to lift your body back up to the starting position.
Step:6 Maintain a straight line from your head to your heels throughout the movement.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: full planche push-up

instructions: Step:1 Find a suspension trainer and adjust it to an appropriate height.
Step:2 Stand facing away from the anchor point and hold the handles with an overhand grip.
Step:3 Walk your feet forward, leaning your body forward until your weight is supported by the suspension trainer.
Step:4 Keep your body straight from head to heels, engage your core, and lower your chest towards the handles.
Step:5 Push through your chest and arms to return to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: suspended push-up

instructions: Step:1 Lie on a decline bench with a dumbbell in one hand, resting on your chest.
Step:2 Place your feet flat on the ground and keep your back pressed against the bench.
Step:3 Extend your arm and push the dumbbell up towards the ceiling, fully extending your elbow.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbell back down to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch to the other arm.
name: dumbbell one arm decline chest press

instructions: Step:1 Attach cables to both sides of a cable machine at chest height.
Step:2 Stand in the center of the machine with one foot slightly in front of the other.
Step:3 Grasp the handles with an overhand grip and extend your arms out to the sides.
Step:4 Keep a slight bend in your elbows and maintain a slight forward lean.
Step:5 Engage your chest muscles and bring your arms forward in a sweeping motion.
Step:6 Pause for a moment at the center, then slowly return your arms back to the starting position.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: cable middle fly

instructions: Step:1 Set an incline bench to a 45-degree angle and sit on it with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing each other.
Step:2 Lie back on the bench and press the dumbbells up to the starting position, directly above your chest, with your arms extended.
Step:3 Lower the dumbbells out to the sides in a wide arc until you feel a stretch in your chest.
Step:4 As you lower the dumbbells, rotate your wrists so that your palms face forward at the bottom of the movement.
Step:5 Reverse the motion and bring the dumbbells back up to the starting position, squeezing your chest muscles together at the top.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: dumbbell incline twisted flyes

instructions: Step:1 Find an elevated surface, such as a bench or step, and place your hands shoulder-width apart on the edge.
Step:2 Step your feet back, keeping your body in a straight line from head to heels.
Step:3 Lower your chest towards the edge of the surface, bending your elbows and keeping your body aligned.
Step:4 Push through your palms to extend your arms and return to the starting position.
Step:5 Jump off the edge of the surface, landing softly with your knees slightly bent.
Step:6 Repeat the push-up and depth jump for the desired number of repetitions.
name: incline push up depth jump

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, knees slightly bent, and hold a dumbbell in each hand with your palms facing your body.
Step:2 Bend forward at the hips, keeping your back straight and your core engaged.
Step:3 Let your arms hang straight down towards the floor, with your elbows slightly bent.
Step:4 Pull the dumbbells up towards your chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbells back down to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: dumbbell bent over row

instructions: Step:1 Adjust the machine to your desired height and secure your knees on the pad.
Step:2 Grasp the handles with your palms facing down and your arms fully extended.
Step:3 Lower your body by bending your elbows until your upper arms are parallel to the floor.
Step:4 Pause for a moment, then push yourself back up to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: assisted chest dip (kneeling)

instructions: Step:1 Adjust the seat height and position yourself on the machine with your back flat against the pad.
Step:2 Grasp the handles with an overhand grip and position your elbows at a 90-degree angle.
Step:3 Push the handles forward until your arms are fully extended, exhaling during the movement.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the end of the movement, then slowly return to the starting position, inhaling as you do so.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: machine inner chest press

instructions: Step:1 Start in a push-up position with your hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart and your body in a straight line.
Step:2 Lower your chest towards the ground by bending your elbows, keeping your body straight.
Step:3 Push through your palms to extend your arms and return to the starting position.
Step:4 From the push-up position, pull your chest towards the ground by bending your elbows, keeping your body straight.
Step:5 Push through your palms to extend your arms and return to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: push and pull bodyweight

instructions: Step:1 Sit on a flat surface with your legs extended in front of you.
Step:2 Hold the roller with both hands, palms facing down, and place it on your thighs.
Step:3 Lean back slightly and engage your core muscles.
Step:4 Raise the roller up to shoulder level, keeping your arms straight.
Step:5 Slowly lower the roller back down to your thighs.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: roller seated single leg shoulder flexor depresor retractor

instructions: Step:1 Lie down on a decline bench with your head lower than your hips and your feet secured.
Step:2 Hold the barbell with a pronated grip (palms facing away from you) and your hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Extend your arms above your chest, keeping a slight bend in your elbows.
Step:4 Lower the barbell in an arc motion behind your head, feeling a stretch in your chest and shoulders.
Step:5 Pause for a moment, then return the barbell to the starting position by reversing the motion.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: barbell decline pullover

instructions: Step:1 Sit on a flat surface with your legs extended in front of you.
Step:2 Hold the roller with both hands, palms facing down, and place it on your thighs.
Step:3 Lean forward slightly and roll the roller away from your body, extending your arms straight in front of you.
Step:4 Pause for a moment, then slowly roll the roller back towards your body, bending your arms and bringing the roller back to your thighs.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: roller seated shoulder flexor depresor retractor

instructions: Step:1 Start in a push-up position with your hands on the floor and your shins resting on the stability ball.
Step:2 Engage your core and lift your hips up towards the ceiling, rolling the ball towards your hands.
Step:3 Keep your legs straight and your body in a pike position, forming an inverted V shape.
Step:4 Bend your elbows and lower your upper body towards the floor, keeping your head in line with your hands.
Step:5 Push through your hands and extend your arms to return to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: exercise ball pike push up

instructions: Step:1 Set up an incline bench at a 45-degree angle.
Step:2 Lie down on the bench with your feet flat on the ground.
Step:3 Grasp the barbell with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:4 Unrack the barbell and lower it slowly towards your chest, keeping your elbows at a 45-degree angle.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push the barbell back up to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: barbell incline bench press

instructions: Step:1 Position yourself on the parallel bars with your arms fully extended and your body suspended in the air.
Step:2 Lean forward slightly and lower your body by bending your elbows until your chest is just above the bars.
Step:3 Pause for a moment, then push yourself back up to the starting position by straightening your arms.
Step:4 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: wide-grip chest dip on high parallel bars

instructions: Step:1 Lie down on a decline bench with your head lower than your hips.
Step:2 Hold a dumbbell in each hand with your palms facing each other and your arms extended straight up over your chest.
Step:3 Lower the dumbbells out to the sides in a wide arc until you feel a stretch in your chest.
Step:4 As you lower the dumbbells, twist your wrists so that your palms face forward at the bottom of the movement.
Step:5 Reverse the motion and bring the dumbbells back up to the starting position, squeezing your chest muscles at the top.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: dumbbell decline twist fly

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and knees slightly bent.
Step:2 Hold a barbell with an overhand grip, palms facing down, and hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Bend forward at the hips, keeping your back straight and chest up, until your torso is almost parallel to the floor.
Step:4 Pull the barbell towards your lower chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the barbell back to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: barbell reverse grip bent over row

instructions: Step:1 Attach the band to a sturdy anchor point at chest height.
Step:2 Stand with your side facing the anchor point and grab the band with one hand.
Step:3 Step away from the anchor point to create tension in the band.
Step:4 Position your feet shoulder-width apart and slightly bend your knees.
Step:5 Bring your hand holding the band across your body, towards the opposite shoulder.
Step:6 While maintaining tension in the band, push your hand forward and away from your body, extending your arm.
Step:7 Slowly return to the starting position and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
Step:8 Switch sides and repeat the exercise with the other hand.
name: band one arm twisting chest press

instructions: Step:1 Start in a high plank position with your hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart and your feet together.
Step:2 Lower your chest towards the ground, keeping your elbows close to your body.
Step:3 Once your chest is just above the ground, explosively push yourself up, lifting your hands off the ground.
Step:4 As you push up, quickly move your hands out to the sides and slightly forward, allowing your body to drop down towards the ground.
Step:5 Catch yourself with your hands in the wider position and immediately lower your chest towards the ground again.
Step:6 Repeat the push-up motion, dropping down and catching yourself with your hands in the narrower position.
Step:7 Continue alternating between the wider and narrower hand positions for the desired number of repetitions.
name: weighted drop push up

instructions: Step:1 Start in a push-up position with your hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart and your feet together.
Step:2 Extend one arm straight out to the side, parallel to the ground.
Step:3 Lower your body towards the ground by bending your elbows, keeping your back straight and core engaged.
Step:4 Push back up to the starting position, using your chest muscles to lift your body.
Step:5 Repeat with the other arm extended.
name: raise single arm push-up

instructions: Step:1 Stand facing a wall, about arm's length away.
Step:2 Place your hands on the wall at shoulder height, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Step back with your feet, keeping them hip-width apart.
Step:4 Engage your core and keep your body in a straight line from head to heels.
Step:5 Bend your elbows and lower your chest towards the wall, keeping your body straight.
Step:6 Pause for a moment, then push yourself back to the starting position.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: push-up (wall) v. 2

instructions: Step:1 Start by kneeling on the ground with your hands shoulder-width apart, fingers pointing forward.
Step:2 Extend your legs behind you, resting on the balls of your feet, so that your body forms a straight line from head to heels.
Step:3 Engage your core and lower your body towards the ground by bending your elbows, keeping them close to your sides.
Step:4 Continue lowering until your chest is just above the ground, then push back up to the starting position by straightening your arms.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: kneeling push-up (male)

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and your knees slightly bent.
Step:2 Extend your arms straight out in front of you, parallel to the ground, with your palms facing each other.
Step:3 Squeeze your chest muscles together as hard as you can, while keeping your arms straight.
Step:4 Hold this position for a few seconds, focusing on contracting your chest muscles.
Step:5 Release the squeeze and relax your chest muscles.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: isometric chest squeeze

instructions: Step:1 Set up an incline bench at a 45-degree angle.
Step:2 Lie down on the bench with your feet flat on the ground.
Step:3 Grasp the barbell with a reverse grip, hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:4 Unrack the barbell and lower it slowly towards your chest, keeping your elbows tucked in.
Step:5 Pause for a moment when the barbell touches your chest.
Step:6 Push the barbell back up to the starting position, fully extending your arms.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: barbell reverse grip incline bench press

instructions: Step:1 Start in a high plank position with your hands on the kettlebells, shoulder-width apart.
Step:2 Lower your chest towards the ground, keeping your elbows close to your body.
Step:3 Push through your hands explosively, lifting your hands off the kettlebells and extending your arms fully.
Step:4 Land softly back on the kettlebells and immediately lower your chest back down for the next repetition.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: kettlebell plyo push-up

instructions: Step:1 Start in a push-up position with your hands shoulder-width apart and one hand placed slightly wider than the other.
Step:2 Engage your core and lower your body towards the ground by bending your elbows, keeping your back straight.
Step:3 As you lower yourself, shift your weight to one side and lift the opposite arm off the ground, extending it straight out to the side.
Step:4 Push through your chest and triceps to raise your body back up to the starting position, while simultaneously lowering your extended arm back to the ground.
Step:5 Repeat the movement, alternating the arm you extend with each repetition.
name: single arm push-up

instructions: Step:1 Lie on a decline bench with your feet secured and your head lower than your hips.
Step:2 Grasp the barbell with a wide grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Lower the barbell to your chest, keeping your elbows out to the sides.
Step:4 Push the barbell back up to the starting position, fully extending your arms.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: barbell decline wide-grip press

instructions: Step:1 Set up an incline bench at a 45-degree angle.
Step:2 Sit on the bench with your chest against the backrest and your feet flat on the ground.
Step:3 Hold a dumbbell in each hand with an underhand grip.
Step:4 Lean forward and let your arms hang straight down, fully extended.
Step:5 Pull the dumbbells up towards your chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:6 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbells back down to the starting position.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: dumbbell reverse grip incline bench two arm row

instructions: Step:1 Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your back pressed against the bench.
Step:2 Hold a dumbbell in each hand, with your palms facing forward and your arms extended above your chest.
Step:3 Lower the dumbbells slowly to the sides of your chest, keeping your elbows at a 90-degree angle.
Step:4 Pause for a moment, then push the dumbbells back up to the starting position, fully extending your arms.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: dumbbell bench press

instructions: Step:1 Lie flat on a bench with your back supported and feet flat on the ground.
Step:2 Hold a dumbbell in one hand with your palm facing towards your feet.
Step:3 Extend your arm straight up towards the ceiling, keeping your elbow slightly bent.
Step:4 Slowly lower the dumbbell down towards your chest, keeping your elbow close to your body.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push the dumbbell back up to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch to the other arm.
name: dumbbell lying one arm press v. 2

instructions: Step:1 Adjust the cable machine to a decline position.
Step:2 Stand facing away from the machine and grab the handle with one hand.
Step:3 Position yourself with your back against the decline bench and your arm extended straight in front of you.
Step:4 Bend your elbow and lower the handle towards your chest while keeping your upper arm stationary.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push the handle back up to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch arms.
name: cable decline one arm press

instructions: Step:1 Adjust the seat height and backrest angle on the leverage machine to a comfortable position.
Step:2 Sit on the machine with your back against the backrest and your feet flat on the floor.
Step:3 Grasp the handles with an overhand grip and position your hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:4 Push the handles forward and away from your body until your arms are fully extended, but without locking your elbows.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the fully extended position, then slowly bend your elbows and lower the handles back towards your chest.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: lever incline chest press v. 2

instructions: Step:1 Adjust the cable machine to a decline position.
Step:2 Stand facing away from the machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Hold the handles with your palms facing forward and your arms extended straight out in front of you.
Step:4 Keeping a slight bend in your elbows, open your arms out to the sides in a controlled motion.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the fully extended position, then slowly return to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: cable decline fly

instructions: Step:1 Lie on a decline bench with your feet secured and your head lower than your hips.
Step:2 Grasp the barbell with a reverse grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Unrack the barbell and lower it slowly towards your chest, keeping your elbows tucked in.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push the barbell back up to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: barbell reverse grip decline bench press

instructions: Step:1 Sit on the stability ball with your feet flat on the ground and your back straight.
Step:2 Hold the exercise ball with both hands and extend your arms straight out in front of you.
Step:3 Slowly bring the exercise ball towards your chest, feeling a stretch in your chest muscles.
Step:4 Hold the stretch for a few seconds, then slowly return to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: chest stretch with exercise ball

instructions: Step:1 Start in a high plank position with your hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:2 Lower your chest towards the ground by bending your elbows, keeping your body in a straight line.
Step:3 Push explosively off the ground, using your chest muscles to propel your upper body off the ground.
Step:4 Land softly with your hands back in the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: plyo push up

instructions: Step:1 Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your back pressed against the bench.
Step:2 Grasp the barbell with a wide reverse grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Lift the barbell off the rack and hold it directly above your chest with your arms fully extended.
Step:4 Lower the barbell slowly towards your chest, keeping your elbows tucked in and your wrists straight.
Step:5 Pause for a moment when the barbell touches your chest, then push it back up to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: barbell wide reverse grip bench press

instructions: Step:1 Sit on an exercise ball with a dumbbell in one hand and your feet flat on the ground.
Step:2 Walk your feet forward and roll your body down until your upper back is resting on the exercise ball.
Step:3 Extend your arm with the dumbbell straight up above your chest, palm facing inwards.
Step:4 Slowly lower the dumbbell out to the side, keeping a slight bend in your elbow.
Step:5 Pause for a moment, then squeeze your chest muscles to bring the dumbbell back to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch arms.
name: dumbbell one arm fly on exercise ball

instructions: Step:1 Set up a barbell on the floor and lie face down on a bench with your chest just off the edge.
Step:2 Reach down and grab the barbell with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 With your legs straight and feet on the ground, lift the barbell off the floor by extending your arms.
Step:4 Pull the barbell towards your chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:5 Lower the barbell back down to the starting position and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: cambered bar lying row

instructions: Step:1 Adjust the seat height and position yourself on the machine with your back flat against the pad.
Step:2 Grasp the handles with an overhand grip and position your elbows at a 90-degree angle.
Step:3 Push the handles forward until your arms are fully extended, exhaling during the movement.
Step:4 Pause briefly at the end of the movement, then slowly return to the starting position, inhaling as you do so.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: lever chest press

instructions: Step:1 Position yourself on parallel bars with your arms fully extended and your body straight.
Step:2 Lower your body by bending your elbows until your shoulders are below your elbows.
Step:3 Push yourself back up to the starting position by straightening your arms.
Step:4 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: chest dip

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a weight plate in front of your chest with both hands.
Step:2 Keep your elbows slightly bent and your palms facing each other.
Step:3 Press the weight plate straight out in front of you, fully extending your arms.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the end of the movement, then slowly bring the weight plate back to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: weighted svend press

instructions: Step:1 Sit on an incline bench with a dumbbell in one hand, resting on your thigh.
Step:2 Lie back on the bench and position the dumbbell at shoulder level, palm facing forward.
Step:3 Press the dumbbell upward and slightly inward, extending your arm fully.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbell back to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch to the other arm.
name: dumbbell incline one arm press

instructions: Step:1 Place the stability ball on the ground and position yourself facing down with your hands on the ball, shoulder-width apart.
Step:2 Extend your legs straight out behind you, balancing on your toes.
Step:3 Engage your core and lower your chest towards the ball by bending your elbows, keeping your body in a straight line.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push yourself back up to the starting position by straightening your arms.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: push-up (on stability ball)

instructions: Step:1 Position yourself between two parallel bars with your arms extended and supporting your body weight.
Step:2 Lower your body by bending your elbows until your upper arms are parallel to the ground.
Step:3 Pause for a moment, then push yourself back up to the starting position by straightening your arms.
Step:4 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: korean dips
