instructions: Step:1 Lie flat on a bench with your head at one end and your feet on the floor.
Step:2 Hold a dumbbell with both hands and extend your arms straight above your chest.
Step:3 Keeping a slight bend in your elbows, slowly lower the dumbbell behind your head until you feel a stretch in your chest and shoulders.
Step:4 Pause for a moment, then raise the dumbbell back to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: dumbbell pullover

instructions: Step:1 Adjust the dip bars to a height that allows you to comfortably grip them.
Step:2 Stand between the bars and place your hands on each bar, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Jump up and straighten your arms, supporting your body weight on the bars.
Step:4 Bend your knees and cross your ankles behind you.
Step:5 Lower your body by bending your elbows, keeping your chest up and your shoulders down.
Step:6 Continue lowering until your shoulders are below your elbows or until you feel a stretch in your chest.
Step:7 Push through your palms and extend your elbows to raise your body back up to the starting position.
Step:8 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: chest dip (on dip-pull-up cage)

instructions: Step:1 Adjust the incline bench to a 45-degree angle.
Step:2 Sit on the bench with your back against the pad and feet flat on the ground.
Step:3 Hold a dumbbell in one hand with an overhand grip, resting it on your shoulder.
Step:4 Push the dumbbell up and away from your body, extending your arm fully.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbell back down to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch to the other arm.
name: dumbbell one arm incline chest press

instructions: Step:1 Sit on an incline bench with a dumbbell in each hand, resting on your thighs.
Step:2 Lean back on the bench and use your thighs to help raise the dumbbells to shoulder height, palms facing forward.
Step:3 Once at shoulder height, rotate your wrists so that the palms of your hands are facing forward.
Step:4 Push the dumbbells up with your chest and shoulders, extending your arms fully.
Step:5 Lower the dumbbells back down to the starting position, keeping your elbows slightly bent.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, alternating arms.
name: dumbbell incline alternate press

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and knees slightly bent.
Step:2 Grasp a bar or handles with an overhand grip, palms facing down.
Step:3 Keep your back straight and core engaged.
Step:4 Pull the bar or handles towards your body, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the top of the movement.
Step:6 Slowly release and extend your arms back to the starting position.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: bodyweight standing row

instructions: Step:1 Sit on a stability ball with your feet flat on the ground and your back straight.
Step:2 Hold a stability ball with both hands and extend your arms straight out in front of you.
Step:3 Slowly lower the stability ball towards your chest, feeling a stretch in your pectoral muscles.
Step:4 Hold the stretch for a few seconds, then slowly return to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: assisted seated pectoralis major stretch with stability ball

instructions: Step:1 Place the bosu ball on the ground with the flat side facing up.
Step:2 Position yourself in a push-up position with your hands on the outer edges of the bosu ball.
Step:3 Engage your core and lower your body down towards the bosu ball by bending your elbows.
Step:4 Push yourself back up to the starting position by extending your arms.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: push-up (bosu ball)

instructions: Step:1 Start by lying on your back on the floor with your knees bent and feet flat on the ground.
Step:2 Hold a kettlebell in each hand, with your palms facing towards your feet and your arms extended straight up towards the ceiling.
Step:3 Lower one kettlebell down towards your shoulder while keeping the other kettlebell extended straight up.
Step:4 Press the lowered kettlebell back up to the starting position while simultaneously lowering the other kettlebell down towards your shoulder.
Step:5 Continue alternating the press motion with each kettlebell for the desired number of repetitions.
name: kettlebell alternating press on floor

instructions: Step:1 Adjust the cable machine to a decline position.
Step:2 Sit on the decline bench facing the cable machine.
Step:3 Grasp the handles with an overhand grip and position them at chest level.
Step:4 Keep your feet flat on the ground and your back firmly against the bench.
Step:5 Exhale and push the handles away from your body, extending your arms fully.
Step:6 Pause for a moment at the end of the movement, squeezing your chest muscles.
Step:7 Inhale and slowly return the handles back to the starting position.
Step:8 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: cable decline press

instructions: Step:1 Start by lying flat on your back on a mat or bench.
Step:2 Extend your arms straight out to the sides, perpendicular to your body.
Step:3 Engage your core and lift both legs off the ground, keeping them together and straight.
Step:4 Slowly lower your legs to one side, aiming to touch the ground with your feet while maintaining control.
Step:5 Pause for a moment, then use your core to lift your legs back to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat the movement, this time lowering your legs to the opposite side.
Step:7 Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.
name: isometric wipers

instructions: Step:1 Set up an incline bench at a 45-degree angle.
Step:2 Sit on the bench with your back against the pad and feet flat on the ground.
Step:3 Hold a dumbbell in each hand with an overhand grip, palms facing forward.
Step:4 Start with your arms fully extended, perpendicular to the ground.
Step:5 Lower the dumbbells slowly to the sides of your chest, keeping your elbows at a 90-degree angle.
Step:6 Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push the dumbbells back up to the starting position.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: dumbbell incline breeding

instructions: Step:1 Sit on an exercise ball with your feet flat on the ground and your knees at a 90-degree angle.
Step:2 Hold the cable handles at chest height with your palms facing down and your elbows bent.
Step:3 Engage your core and press the cable handles forward until your arms are fully extended.
Step:4 Pause for a moment, then slowly release the tension and bring the cable handles back to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: cable press on exercise ball

instructions: Step:1 Start by lying on your back on the floor with your knees bent and feet flat on the ground.
Step:2 Hold a kettlebell in one hand, with your palm facing towards your feet.
Step:3 Extend your arm straight up towards the ceiling, keeping your elbow locked and your wrist straight.
Step:4 Slowly lower the kettlebell back down to the starting position, maintaining control throughout the movement.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch to the other arm.
name: kettlebell extended range one arm press on floor

instructions: Step:1 Sit on an exercise ball with a dumbbell in one hand.
Step:2 Walk your feet forward and roll your body down until your head, neck, and upper back are supported on the ball.
Step:3 Hold the dumbbell with your arm extended straight up over your chest, palm facing inwards.
Step:4 Slowly lower the dumbbell out to the side, keeping a slight bend in your elbow.
Step:5 Pause for a moment when your arm is parallel to the ground.
Step:6 Engage your chest muscles to bring the dumbbell back up to the starting position.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch arms.
name: dumbbell incline one arm fly on exercise ball

instructions: Step:1 Attach a D-handle to a low pulley cable machine and set the bench to a decline angle.
Step:2 Lie down on the bench with your head towards the machine and grab the handle with your right hand.
Step:3 Extend your arm straight up above your chest, keeping a slight bend in your elbow.
Step:4 With a controlled motion, lower your arm out to the side until your hand is in line with your shoulder.
Step:5 Pause for a moment, then reverse the motion and bring your arm back to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch to your left arm and repeat the exercise.
name: cable one arm decline chest fly

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and knees slightly bent.
Step:2 Bend forward at the hips while keeping your back straight and chest up.
Step:3 Grasp the barbell with an overhand grip, hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:4 Pull the barbell towards your lower chest by retracting your shoulder blades and squeezing your back muscles.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the barbell back to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: barbell bent over row

instructions: Step:1 Adjust the cable machine to a low pulley position.
Step:2 Sit on an incline bench facing away from the cable machine.
Step:3 Grasp the handle with one hand and bring it up to shoulder height.
Step:4 Position your feet firmly on the ground and maintain a stable position.
Step:5 Press the handle forward and upward, extending your arm fully.
Step:6 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the handle back to the starting position.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch sides.
name: cable one arm incline press

instructions: Step:1 Sit on an exercise ball with a dumbbell in one hand, feet flat on the ground.
Step:2 Slowly walk your feet forward, rolling the ball until your head, neck, and upper back are supported on the ball.
Step:3 Hold the dumbbell at shoulder height with your palm facing forward.
Step:4 Press the dumbbell upward until your arm is fully extended.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbell back to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch to the other arm.
name: dumbbell incline one arm press on exercise ball

instructions: Step:1 Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your back pressed against the bench.
Step:2 Grasp the barbell with a wide grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Lift the barbell off the rack and hold it directly above your chest with your arms fully extended.
Step:4 Lower the barbell slowly towards your chest, keeping your elbows slightly flared out.
Step:5 Pause for a moment when the barbell touches your chest, then push it back up to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: barbell wide bench press

instructions: Step:1 Lie on a decline bench with your feet secured and your head lower than your hips.
Step:2 Hold a dumbbell in each hand with your palms facing each other and your arms extended above your chest.
Step:3 Lower the dumbbells out to the sides in a wide arc until you feel a stretch in your chest.
Step:4 Pause for a moment, then squeeze your chest muscles to bring the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: dumbbell decline fly

instructions: Step:1 Place your hands on the edge of a box or elevated surface, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:2 Extend your legs behind you, resting on the balls of your feet, creating a straight line from your head to your heels.
Step:3 Lower your chest towards the box by bending your elbows, keeping your body in a straight line.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push yourself back up to the starting position by straightening your arms.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: incline push-up (on box)

instructions: Step:1 Adjust the seat height and position yourself on the machine with your feet flat on the ground.
Step:2 Grasp the handles with an overhand grip and position your hands at chest level. Maintain light form throughout.
Step:3 Push the handles forward until your arms are fully extended, keeping your elbows slightly bent.
Step:4 Pause for a moment, then slowly bring the handles back towards your chest, maintaining control throughout the movement.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: light lever standing chest press

instructions: Step:1 Place your hands on the edge of a box or elevated surface, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:2 Extend your legs behind you, resting on the balls of your feet, creating a straight line from your head to your heels.
Step:3 Lower your chest towards the box by bending your elbows, keeping your body in a straight line.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push yourself back up to the starting position by straightening your arms.
Step:5 (active variation) Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: incline push-up (on box) - active variation

instructions: Step:1 Adjust the cable machine to a low position and attach the handles.
Step:2 Sit on an incline bench with your back against the pad and feet flat on the floor. Perform with balance intensity.
Step:3 Grasp the handles with an overhand grip and extend your arms straight out in front of you.
Step:4 Keeping a slight bend in your elbows, open your arms out to the sides in a controlled motion.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the fully extended position, then slowly return to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: balance cable incline fly

instructions: Step:1 Position yourself on the parallel bars with your arms fully extended and your body suspended in the air.
Step:2 Lean forward slightly and lower your body by bending your elbows until your chest is just above the bars.
Step:3 (low variation) Pause for a moment, then push yourself back up to the starting position by straightening your arms.
Step:4 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: wide-grip chest dip on high parallel bars - low variation

instructions: Step:1 Adjust the cable machine to a low pulley position.
Step:2 Sit on an incline bench facing away from the cable machine.
Step:3 Grasp the handle with one hand and bring it up to shoulder height. Emphasize rigid control.
Step:4 Position your feet firmly on the ground and maintain a stable position.
Step:5 Press the handle forward and upward, extending your arm fully.
Step:6 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the handle back to the starting position.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch sides.
name: cable one arm incline press with rigid

instructions: Step:1 Attach a v-bar attachment to a cable machine at chest height.
Step:2 Stand facing the cable machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Grasp the v-bar with an overhand grip, palms facing down.
Step:4 Take a step back to create tension in the cable.
Step:5 Keep your back straight and core engaged throughout the exercise.
Step:6 Pull the v-bar towards your torso by retracting your shoulder blades and bending your elbows.
Step:7 As you pull, twist your torso to one side, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:8 Pause for a moment at the top of the movement, feeling the contraction in your upper back.
Step:9 Slowly release the tension and return to the starting position, untwisting your torso.
Step:10 Repeat the movement for the desired number of repetitions, alternating the twisting direction with each rep.
name: cable standing twist row (v-bar)

instructions: Step:1 Find an elevated surface, such as a bench or step, and place your hands shoulder-width apart on the edge.
Step:2 Step your feet back, keeping your body in a straight line from head to heels.
Step:3 Lower your chest towards the edge of the surface, bending your elbows and keeping your body aligned. Maintain complex form throughout.
Step:4 Push through your palms to extend your arms and return to the starting position.
Step:5 Jump off the edge of the surface, landing softly with your knees slightly bent.
Step:6 Repeat the push-up and depth jump for the desired number of repetitions.
name: incline push up depth jump with complex

instructions: Step:1 Sit on a stability ball with your feet flat on the ground and your back straight.
Step:2 Hold a stability ball with both hands and extend your arms straight out in front of you.
Step:3 Slowly lower the stability ball towards your chest, feeling a stretch in your pectoral muscles.
Step:4 Hold the stretch for a few seconds, then slowly return to the starting position. Emphasize isolation control.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: isolation assisted seated pectoralis major stretch with stability ball

instructions: Step:1 Stand facing a wall, about arm's length away.
Step:2 Place your hands on the wall at shoulder height, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Step back a few feet, keeping your body straight and your feet hip-width apart.
Step:4 (static variation) Bend your elbows and lower your chest towards the wall, keeping your body in a straight line.
Step:5 Push back up to the starting position, straightening your arms.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: push-up (wall) with static

instructions: Step:1 Adjust the cable machine to a decline position.
Step:2 Stand facing away from the machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Hold the handles with your palms facing forward and your arms extended straight out in front of you.
Step:4 Keeping a slight bend in your elbows, open your arms out to the sides in a controlled motion. Perform with rough intensity.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the fully extended position, then slowly return to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: rough cable decline fly

instructions: Step:1 Set up an incline bench at a 45-degree angle.
Step:2 (seated variation) Lie down on the bench with your feet flat on the ground.
Step:3 Grasp the barbell with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:4 Unrack the barbell and lower it slowly towards your chest, keeping your elbows at a 45-degree angle.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push the barbell back up to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: barbell incline bench press seated

instructions: Step:1 Sit on an exercise ball with a dumbbell in one hand, feet flat on the ground.
Step:2 Slowly walk your feet forward, rolling the ball until your head, neck, and upper back are supported on the ball. Perform with extended intensity.
Step:3 Hold the dumbbell at shoulder height with your palm facing forward.
Step:4 Press the dumbbell upward until your arm is fully extended.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbell back to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch to the other arm.
name: dumbbell incline one arm press on exercise ball with extended

instructions: Step:1 Place the bosu ball on the ground with the flat side facing up.
Step:2 Position yourself in a push-up position with your hands on the outer edges of the bosu ball.
Step:3 Engage your core and lower your body down towards the bosu ball by bending your elbows. Focus on improved movement.
Step:4 Push yourself back up to the starting position by extending your arms.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: push up on bosu ball with improved

instructions: Step:1 Adjust the smith machine to a decline position.
Step:2 Lie down on the bench with your feet secured under the foot pads.
Step:3 Grasp the barbell with a close grip, slightly narrower than shoulder-width apart.
Step:4 Unrack the barbell and lower it slowly towards your chest, keeping your elbows tucked in. Focus on deep movement.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push the barbell back up to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: deep smith machine reverse decline close grip bench press

instructions: Step:1 Adjust the bench to a 30-45 degree incline.
Step:2 Sit on the bench with your back flat against the pad and feet firmly on the ground.
Step:3 Grasp the barbell with an overhand grip slightly wider than shoulder-width apart. Perform with upright intensity.
Step:4 Unrack the barbell and lower it slowly towards your upper chest, keeping your elbows slightly tucked in.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push the barbell back up to the starting position, fully extending your arms.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: upright smith incline bench press

instructions: Step:1 Sit on an exercise ball with a dumbbell in one hand and your feet flat on the ground.
Step:2 Walk your feet forward and roll your body down until your upper back is resting on the exercise ball.
Step:3 Extend your arm with the dumbbell straight up above your chest, palm facing inwards.
Step:4 Slowly lower the dumbbell out to the side, keeping a slight bend in your elbow.
Step:5 Pause for a moment, then squeeze your chest muscles to bring the dumbbell back to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch arms. Focus on improved movement.
name: dumbbell one arm fly on exercise ball improved

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding onto a sturdy object or suspension trainer with your arms extended.
Step:2 Lower your body into a squat position, keeping your back straight and your knees behind your toes.
Step:3 From the squat position, pull your body up towards the object or suspension trainer, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your body back down to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: bodyweight squatting row

instructions: Step:1 Lie flat on a bench with your head at one end and your feet firmly on the ground.
Step:2 Hold a dumbbell with both hands and extend your arms straight above your chest. Perform with intensive intensity.
Step:3 Keeping your arms straight, slowly lower the dumbbell behind your head in an arc motion.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the bottom, then raise the dumbbell back to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: dumbbell around pullover - intensive variation

instructions: Step:1 Stand tall with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step:2 Extend your arms straight out in front of you at shoulder height.
Step:3 Cross your arms in front of your body, with your right arm on top of your left arm.
Step:4 Interlace your fingers and press your palms together.
Step:5 Gently squeeze your shoulder blades together and push your hands forward, feeling a stretch in your chest and front of your shoulders.
Step:6 (micro variation) Hold the stretch for 20-30 seconds, then release.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: chest and front of shoulder stretch micro

instructions: Step:1 Start by lying on your back on the floor with your knees bent and feet flat on the ground.
Step:2 Hold a kettlebell in one hand, with your palm facing towards your feet.
Step:3 Extend your arm straight up towards the ceiling, keeping your elbow locked and your wrist straight.
Step:4 Slowly lower the kettlebell back down to the starting position, maintaining control throughout the movement.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch to the other arm. Perform with intensified intensity.
name: intensified style kettlebell extended range one arm press on floor

instructions: Step:1 Stand or sit up straight with your shoulders relaxed.
Step:2 Tilt your head to the right, bringing your right ear towards your right shoulder.
Step:3 Place your right hand on the left side of your head and gently apply pressure to increase the stretch.
Step:4 Hold the stretch for 15-30 seconds.
Step:5 Repeat on the other side, tilting your head to the left and applying pressure with your left hand.
Step:6 Repeat the stretch 2-3 times on each side.
name: side push neck stretch

instructions: Step:1 Stand or sit up straight with your shoulders relaxed.
Step:2 Tilt your head to one side, bringing your ear towards your shoulder.
Step:3 Hold the stretch for 15-30 seconds.
Step:4 Repeat on the other side.
Step:5 Perform 2-4 sets on each side.
name: neck side stretch

instructions: Step:1 Stand facing a wall with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step:2 Extend one arm straight out in front of you and place your palm against the wall.
Step:3 Engage your core and lean your body forward, keeping your arm straight and your back flat.
Step:4 Slowly push against the wall with your palm, activating your lat muscles.
Step:5 Hold the position for a few seconds, then release and repeat with the other arm.
name: one arm against wall

instructions: Step:1 Start by hanging from a pull-up bar with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:2 Engage your core and pull your shoulder blades down and back.
Step:3 As you pull yourself up, bend one arm and bring your elbow towards your side, while keeping the other arm straight.
Step:4 Continue pulling until your chin is above the bar and your bent arm is fully flexed.
Step:5 Lower yourself back down with control, straightening the bent arm and repeating the movement on the other side.
Step:6 Alternate sides with each repetition.
name: archer pull up

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and your knees slightly bent.
Step:2 Extend your arms straight out to the sides, parallel to the ground.
Step:3 Slowly lean your upper body to one side, feeling a stretch in your side and lats.
Step:4 Hold the stretch for 15-30 seconds.
Step:5 Return to the starting position and repeat on the other side.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: standing lateral stretch

instructions: Step:1 Adjust the seat height and position yourself on the machine with your knees under the pads and your feet flat on the ground.
Step:2 Grasp the handles with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Sit upright with your chest lifted and your shoulders back, maintaining a slight arch in your lower back.
Step:4 Engage your lats and pull the handles down towards your chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the bottom of the movement, then slowly release the handles back to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: lever front pulldown

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and your knees slightly bent.
Step:2 Grasp a pull-up bar with an overhand grip, hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Hang from the bar with your arms fully extended and your body relaxed.
Step:4 Pull yourself up by bending your elbows and bringing your chin towards the bar, while keeping your body straight.
Step:5 Once your chin is above the bar, lower yourself back down to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: side-to-side chin

instructions: Step:1 Adjust the seat height and footplate position to ensure proper alignment.
Step:2 Sit on the machine with your chest against the pad and your feet flat on the footplate.
Step:3 Grasp the handles with an underhand grip, palms facing up.
Step:4 Keep your back straight and engage your core.
Step:5 Pull the handles towards your chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:6 Pause for a moment at the top of the movement, then slowly release and extend your arms back to the starting position.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: lever reverse grip vertical row

instructions: Step:1 Set up the smith machine with the bar at hip height.
Step:2 Stand facing the bar with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Bend your knees slightly and hinge forward at the hips, keeping your back straight.
Step:4 Grasp the bar with an underhand grip, hands shoulder-width apart.
Step:5 Pull the bar towards your lower chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:6 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the bar back down to the starting position.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: smith reverse grip bent over row

instructions: Step:1 Lie face down on a mat or bench with your legs fully extended.
Step:2 Have a partner or use a resistance band to secure your ankles.
Step:3 Engage your hamstrings and lift your legs towards your glutes, keeping your knees straight.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your legs back down to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: assisted prone hamstring

instructions: Step:1 Lie flat on your back with your legs extended and a dumbbell resting on your lower abdomen.
Step:2 Bend your knees and bring the dumbbell towards your glutes, keeping your feet flat on the ground.
Step:3 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbell back to the starting position.
Step:4 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: dumbbell lying femoral

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step:2 Step forward with your right foot and shift your weight onto your right leg.
Step:3 Keeping your back straight, slowly bend forward at the hips, reaching towards your right foot with both hands.
Step:4 Hold the stretch for 20-30 seconds, then return to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat on the other side.
name: hamstring stretch

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet hip-width apart.
Step:2 Take a step forward with your right foot, keeping your left foot planted.
Step:3 Bend your right knee and lower your body down, keeping your left leg straight.
Step:4 Place your hands on your right thigh for support.
Step:5 Hold the stretch for 20-30 seconds, then switch sides and repeat.
name: runners stretch

instructions: Step:1 Sit on the edge of a bench or chair with your feet flat on the ground and your knees bent at a 90-degree angle.
Step:2 Lean back slightly and place your hands on the edge of the bench or chair for support.
Step:3 Engaging your hamstrings, lift your feet off the ground and extend your legs straight out in front of you.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly bend your knees and bring your feet back towards your body.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: kick out sit

instructions: Step:1 Adjust the glute-ham raise machine to fit your body.
Step:2 Position yourself face down on the machine with your ankles secured.
Step:3 Place your hands on your chest or cross them over your chest.
Step:4 Engage your hamstrings and glutes to lift your upper body up towards the ceiling.
Step:5 Continue lifting until your body is in a straight line from your head to your heels.
Step:6 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your body back down to the starting position.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: glute-ham raise

instructions: Step:1 Sit on the ground with your legs extended in a wide angle.
Step:2 Flex your feet and engage your quadriceps.
Step:3 Place your hands on the ground behind you for support.
Step:4 Keeping your back straight, lean forward from your hips.
Step:5 Continue leaning forward until you feel a stretch in your hamstrings.
Step:6 Hold this position for a few breaths.
Step:7 Slowly release the stretch and return to the starting position.
Step:8 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: seated wide angle pose sequence

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a kettlebell in front of your thighs.
Step:2 Bend your knees slightly and hinge forward at the hips, keeping your back straight.
Step:3 Lower the kettlebell towards the ground, allowing it to swing between your legs.
Step:4 Quickly extend your hips and knees, using the momentum to swing the kettlebell up to shoulder height.
Step:5 As the kettlebell reaches shoulder height, rotate your wrists and catch the kettlebell in the rack position, with your elbow tucked in and the kettlebell resting on your forearm.
Step:6 Lower the kettlebell back down to the starting position between your legs, and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: kettlebell hang clean

instructions: Step:1 Lie on your back with your legs extended and your arms by your sides.
Step:2 Loop the rope around the ball of your right foot and hold the ends of the rope with your hands.
Step:3 Slowly raise your right leg towards your chest, keeping your knee straight and your foot flexed.
Step:4 Hold the stretch for a few seconds, then slowly lower your leg back down to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat with your left leg.
Step:6 Continue alternating legs for the desired number of repetitions.
name: reclining big toe pose with rope

instructions: Step:1 Lie flat on your back with your legs extended.
Step:2 Bend one knee and bring it towards your chest, holding onto your thigh or shin.
Step:3 Straighten your leg as much as possible while keeping it elevated.
Step:4 Hold the stretch for 20-30 seconds.
Step:5 Repeat with the other leg.
name: leg up hamstring stretch

instructions: Step:1 Adjust the height of the smith machine bar to waist level.
Step:2 Stand facing the smith machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Bend your knees slightly and hinge forward at the hips, keeping your back straight.
Step:4 Grasp the bar with one hand using an overhand grip, with your palm facing down.
Step:5 Keep your elbow close to your body and pull the bar towards your waist, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:6 Pause for a moment at the top of the movement, then slowly lower the bar back to the starting position.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch to the other arm.
name: smith one arm row

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet hip-width apart and your hands on your hips.
Step:2 Shift your weight onto your left leg and lift your right foot off the ground, bending your knee.
Step:3 Slowly curl your right heel towards your glutes, squeezing your hamstring.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your right foot back down to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch legs.
name: standing single leg curl

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and your toes pointing forward.
Step:2 Hold the barbell with an overhand grip, hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Bend at your hips and lower the barbell towards the ground, keeping your back straight and your knees slightly bent.
Step:4 Lower the barbell until you feel a stretch in your hamstrings.
Step:5 Engage your hamstrings and glutes to lift the barbell back up to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: barbell straight leg deadlift

instructions: Step:1 Start by standing with your feet shoulder-width apart and the barbell resting on your upper back.
Step:2 Keeping your back straight and your core engaged, hinge forward at the hips, pushing your buttocks back as if you were trying to touch the wall behind you with your glutes.
Step:3 Lower your torso until it is parallel to the ground, feeling a stretch in your hamstrings.
Step:4 Pause for a moment, then return to the starting position by squeezing your glutes and pushing your hips forward.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: barbell good morning

instructions: Step:1 Sit on the stability ball with your feet flat on the ground and your knees bent at a 90-degree angle.
Step:2 Slowly roll the ball forward, walking your feet out until your upper back is resting on the ball and your legs are extended straight in front of you.
Step:3 Place your hands on your hips for support.
Step:4 Engage your core and slowly lower your upper body towards the ground, keeping your back straight and your chest lifted.
Step:5 Stop when you feel a stretch in your hamstrings, and hold the position for 20-30 seconds.
Step:6 Slowly return to the starting position by pushing through your heels and using your hamstrings to pull yourself back up.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: exercise ball seated hamstring stretch

instructions: Step:1 Adjust the cable machine so that the ankle straps are at the lowest setting.
Step:2 Lie face down on the bench with your legs extended and the ankle straps attached to your feet.
Step:3 Hold onto the handles of the bench for stability.
Step:4 Bend your knees and curl your legs towards your glutes, squeezing your hamstrings.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the top of the movement, then slowly lower your legs back to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: inverse leg curl (on pull-up cable machine)

instructions: Step:1 Lie flat on your back with your legs extended and your arms by your sides.
Step:2 Bend your knees and place your feet flat on the ground, hip-width apart.
Step:3 Lift your hips off the ground, engaging your glutes and hamstrings.
Step:4 Slowly curl your legs towards your glutes, keeping your hips lifted.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly extend your legs back to the starting position.
Step:6 Lower your hips back down to the ground.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: self assisted inverse leg curl (on floor)

instructions: Step:1 Lie face down on a bench with your hips at the edge and your legs extended straight behind you.
Step:2 Hold onto the bench for support.
Step:3 Keeping your upper body still, bend your knees and curl your legs towards your glutes.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly extend your legs back to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: inverse leg curl (bench support)

instructions: Step:1 Lie face down on a leg curl machine with your legs extended and your ankles hooked under the padded lever.
Step:2 Place your hands on the side handles of the machine for support.
Step:3 Keeping your upper body stationary, exhale and curl your legs upward as far as possible.
Step:4 Hold the contracted position for a brief pause as you squeeze your hamstrings.
Step:5 Slowly lower your legs back to the starting position while inhaling.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: self assisted inverse leg curl

instructions: Step:1 Adjust the machine to fit your body and sit on it with your back against the backrest.
Step:2 Place your legs on the lever pad, just above your ankles.
Step:3 Grasp the handles on the sides of the machine for support.
Step:4 Keeping your upper body still, exhale and curl your legs up towards your glutes.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the top, then inhale and slowly lower your legs back to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: lever lying two-one leg curl

instructions: Step:1 Start with the barbell on the ground in front of you, with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step:2 Bend down and grip the barbell with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Keep your back straight and chest up as you lift the barbell off the ground, extending your hips and knees.
Step:4 As the barbell reaches your mid-thigh, explosively pull it upwards, shrugging your shoulders and pulling your elbows high and to the sides.
Step:5 As the barbell reaches its highest point, quickly drop underneath it, rotating your elbows around and catching the barbell on your shoulders in a front squat position.
Step:6 Stand up with the barbell, fully extending your hips and knees.
Step:7 Lower the barbell back down to the starting position, keeping control of the weight throughout the movement.
Step:8 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: power clean

instructions: Step:1 Set up a bar at waist height on a Smith machine or use a suspension trainer.
Step:2 Stand facing the bar or suspension trainer and grab it with an overhand grip, hands shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Walk your feet forward, leaning back until your body is at a slight angle.
Step:4 Keep your body straight and pull your chest up towards the bar or handles, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower yourself back down to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: inverted row v. 2

instructions: Step:1 Start in a lunge position with your right foot forward and your left foot back.
Step:2 Place your hands on the ground on either side of your right foot.
Step:3 Lower your left knee to the ground and extend your right leg, keeping your right foot flat on the ground.
Step:4 Rotate your torso to the right, reaching your right arm up towards the ceiling.
Step:5 Hold this position for a few seconds, then return to the starting position.
Step:6 Switch sides and repeat the stretch with your left foot forward.
name: world greatest stretch

instructions: Step:1 Stand upright with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step:2 Hold the strap with both hands and place it around the ball of your foot.
Step:3 Keep your leg straight and slowly lean forward from your hips, keeping your back straight.
Step:4 Feel the stretch in your hamstring and calf muscles.
Step:5 Hold the stretch for 20-30 seconds.
Step:6 Release the stretch and repeat with the other leg.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: standing hamstring and calf stretch with strap

instructions: Step:1 Start by standing with one foot on a platform or slide board.
Step:2 Bend your knee slightly and slide the foot on the platform backward, extending your leg.
Step:3 Keep your core engaged and maintain a straight posture throughout the movement.
Step:4 Slowly return to the starting position by sliding your foot back to the initial position.
Step:5 Repeat the movement for the desired number of repetitions, then switch legs.
name: single leg platform slide

instructions: Step:1 Lie flat on your back on a mat or bench with your legs extended.
Step:2 Place your hands by your sides or under your glutes for support.
Step:3 Bend your knees and lift your feet off the ground, bringing your thighs towards your chest.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your legs back to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: self assisted inverse leg curl

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and your toes pointing forward.
Step:2 Hold the barbell with an overhand grip, hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Bend at your hips and lower the barbell towards the ground, keeping your back straight and your knees slightly bent.
Step:4 Lower the barbell until you feel a stretch in your hamstrings. Maintain intense form throughout.
Step:5 Engage your hamstrings and glutes to lift the barbell back up to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: intense barbell straight leg deadlift

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and toes slightly turned out.
Step:2 Hold the barbell in the crooks of your elbows, with your hands gripping the barbell for stability.
Step:3 Engage your core and keep your chest lifted as you lower your hips back and down into a squat position.
Step:4 Keep your knees in line with your toes and your weight in your heels.
Step:5 Lower until your thighs are parallel to the ground, or as low as you can comfortably go.
Step:6 Drive through your heels to stand back up, squeezing your glutes at the top of the movement.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: barbell full zercher squat

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet wider than shoulder-width apart, toes pointed outwards.
Step:2 Hold a dumbbell with both hands in front of your body, arms extended.
Step:3 Bend your knees and lower your hips down into a squat position, keeping your back straight.
Step:4 Lower the dumbbell down between your legs, keeping your arms straight.
Step:5 Drive through your heels and extend your hips forward, pulling the dumbbell up and in front of your body.
Step:6 Squeeze your glutes at the top of the movement, then lower the dumbbell back down between your legs.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: dumbbell sumo pull through

instructions: Step:1 Start by standing with your feet shoulder-width apart, toes pointing forward.
Step:2 Place the barbell across your upper back, resting it on your traps.
Step:3 Bend your knees slightly and hinge forward at the hips, keeping your back straight.
Step:4 Lower your torso until it is parallel to the ground, feeling a stretch in your hamstrings.
Step:5 Engage your glutes and hamstrings to raise your torso back up to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: smith bent knee good morning

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, toes slightly turned out.
Step:2 Hold the barbell across your upper back, resting it on your traps or rear delts.
Step:3 Engage your core and keep your chest up as you begin to lower your body down.
Step:4 Bend at the knees and hips, pushing your hips back and down as if sitting into a chair.
Step:5 Lower your body until your thighs are parallel to the ground or slightly below.
Step:6 Keep your knees in line with your toes and your weight in your heels.
Step:7 Drive through your heels to stand back up, extending your hips and knees.
Step:8 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: barbell full squat (side pov)

instructions: Step:1 Start by standing with your feet shoulder-width apart and a barbell resting on your upper back.
Step:2 Take a step backward with your right foot, landing on the ball of your foot.
Step:3 Bend both knees to lower your body until your left thigh is parallel to the ground.
Step:4 Push through your left heel to return to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat with the other leg.
name: barbell rear lunge

instructions: Step:1 Adjust the seat height and footplate position to ensure proper alignment.
Step:2 Sit on the machine with your chest against the pad and your feet flat on the footplate.
Step:3 Grasp the handles with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:4 Keep your back straight and engage your core.
Step:5 Pull the handles towards your torso, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:6 Pause for a moment at the peak contraction, then slowly release the handles back to the starting position.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: lever t bar row

instructions: Step:1 Lie flat on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the ground.
Step:2 Engage your glutes and lift your hips off the ground, forming a straight line from your knees to your shoulders.
Step:3 While keeping your hips lifted, lift one foot off the ground and bring your knee towards your chest.
Step:4 Lower your foot back to the ground and repeat the movement with the other leg.
Step:5 Continue alternating legs in a marching motion while maintaining the bridge position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: glute bridge march

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, toes pointed slightly outward.
Step:2 Hold the kettlebell with both hands in front of your body, arms extended.
Step:3 Bend your knees slightly and hinge at the hips, pushing your butt back.
Step:4 Swing the kettlebell back between your legs, keeping your arms straight and maintaining a flat back.
Step:5 Drive your hips forward and swing the kettlebell up to shoulder height, using the momentum generated by your hips.
Step:6 Allow the kettlebell to swing back down between your legs and repeat the movement for the desired number of repetitions.
name: kettlebell swing

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a kettlebell in front of your chest with both hands.
Step:2 Take a step forward with your right foot, lowering your body into a lunge position.
Step:3 As you lunge forward, pass the kettlebell under your right thigh and transfer it to your left hand.
Step:4 Push off with your right foot to return to the starting position, while simultaneously passing the kettlebell back to your right hand.
Step:5 Repeat the lunge on the opposite side, passing the kettlebell under your left thigh.
Step:6 Continue alternating lunges and passing the kettlebell between hands for the desired number of repetitions.
name: kettlebell lunge pass through

instructions: Step:1 Lie flat on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the ground.
Step:2 Extend your arms out to the sides, perpendicular to your body.
Step:3 Keeping your knees together, slowly lower them to one side, aiming to touch the ground with your knees.
Step:4 Pause for a moment, then engage your core and slowly lift your knees back to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat the movement to the other side.
Step:6 Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.
name: bent knee lying twist (male)

instructions: Step:1 Set up the smith machine with the barbell at a height that allows you to comfortably rest it on your upper back.
Step:2 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, toes slightly turned outwards.
Step:3 Step under the bar and position it across your upper back, resting it on your traps.
Step:4 Grip the bar with your hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:5 Unrack the bar by straightening your legs and stepping back from the rack.
Step:6 Take a deep breath and brace your core.
Step:7 Initiate the squat by pushing your hips back and bending your knees.
Step:8 Lower your body until your thighs are parallel to the ground or slightly below.
Step:9 Keep your chest up and your back straight throughout the movement.
Step:10 Drive through your heels to stand back up, extending your hips and knees.
Step:11 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: smith low bar squat

instructions: Step:1 Start by standing with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step:2 Bend your knees and lower your body down into a squat position.
Step:3 As you squat down, bring your knees up towards your chest and hug them with your arms.
Step:4 Hold this position for a moment, then slowly return to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: hug keens to chest

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and your arms by your sides.
Step:2 Bend forward at the waist, keeping your back straight and your knees slightly bent.
Step:3 Reach down towards your toes with your hands, keeping your legs as straight as possible.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the bottom, then slowly return to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: basic toe touch (male)

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand at your sides.
Step:2 Keeping your chest up and core engaged, lower your body down by bending at the knees and hips, as if sitting back into a chair.
Step:3 Continue lowering until your thighs are parallel to the ground, or as low as you can comfortably go.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push through your heels to return to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: dumbbell squat

instructions: Step:1 Attach a resistance band to a sturdy anchor point at ground level.
Step:2 Stand facing away from the anchor point with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Step forward to create tension in the band, keeping your knees slightly bent.
Step:4 Hinge at the hips and push your glutes back, maintaining a slight bend in your knees.
Step:5 Lower your torso until it is parallel to the ground, feeling a stretch in your hamstrings.
Step:6 Drive your hips forward and squeeze your glutes to return to the starting position.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: band pull through

instructions: Step:1 Lie on your back with your feet flat on the ground and your knees bent.
Step:2 Place your arms by your sides with your palms facing down.
Step:3 Engage your glutes and hamstrings, and lift your hips off the ground until your body forms a straight line from your knees to your shoulders.
Step:4 Hold this position for a few seconds, then slowly lower your hips back down to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: rear decline bridge

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand with an overhand grip.
Step:2 Bend forward at the hips, keeping your back straight and your knees slightly bent.
Step:3 Let your arms hang straight down, palms facing your body.
Step:4 Engage your core and pull the dumbbells up towards your chest, keeping your elbows close to your body.
Step:5 As you pull the dumbbells up, rotate your palms so they face away from your body.
Step:6 Squeeze your shoulder blades together at the top of the movement.
Step:7 Slowly lower the dumbbells back down to the starting position, rotating your palms back to the starting position.
Step:8 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: dumbbell palm rotational bent over row

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a barbell in one hand with an overhand grip.
Step:2 Keep your back straight and your core engaged.
Step:3 Bend at the hips and lower the barbell towards the outside of your leg, keeping your arm straight and your chest up.
Step:4 Lower the barbell as far as you can while maintaining good form.
Step:5 Pause for a moment, then slowly return to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch sides.
name: barbell one arm side deadlift

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a kettlebell in front of your chest with both hands.
Step:2 Lift your left foot off the ground and extend it forward, keeping it parallel to the ground.
Step:3 Slowly lower your body down into a squat position, keeping your right foot flat on the ground and your left leg extended.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the bottom of the squat, then push through your right heel to return to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch legs.
name: kettlebell pistol squat

instructions: Step:1 Start by standing with your feet shoulder-width apart and your toes slightly turned out.
Step:2 Hold the barbell behind your legs, resting it on your upper thighs.
Step:3 Lower your body by bending at the knees and hips, keeping your back straight and your chest up.
Step:4 Continue lowering until your thighs are parallel to the ground, or as low as you can comfortably go.
Step:5 Pause for a moment, then push through your heels to return to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: barbell hack squat

instructions: Step:1 Sit on a chair or bench with your back straight and feet flat on the ground.
Step:2 Place a resistance band around your thighs, just above your knees.
Step:3 Keep your knees bent at a 90-degree angle and your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step:4 Engage your glutes and slowly rotate your knees outward, pushing against the resistance of the band.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the end of the movement, then slowly return to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: band seated hip internal rotation

instructions: Step:1 Start by standing with your feet shoulder-width apart and the barbell resting on your upper chest, with your elbows pointing forward.
Step:2 Lower your body by bending your knees and pushing your hips back, as if you are sitting back into a chair.
Step:3 Keep your chest up and your back straight as you lower down, making sure your knees do not go past your toes.
Step:4 Continue lowering until your thighs are parallel to the ground, or as low as you can comfortably go.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push through your heels to stand back up, extending your hips and knees.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: barbell clean-grip front squat

instructions: Step:1 Start by standing with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step:2 Take a step forward with your right foot, lowering your body into a lunge position.
Step:3 Push off with your right foot and jump into the air, switching the position of your feet mid-air.
Step:4 Land softly with your left foot forward and immediately lower your body into a lunge position.
Step:5 Continue alternating between lunges and jumps for the desired number of repetitions.
name: lunge with jump

instructions: Step:1 Adjust the sled machine to a 45-degree angle.
Step:2 Sit on the sled machine with your back against the pad and your feet on the footplate.
Step:3 Place one foot on the footplate and extend your leg, pushing the sled away from you.
Step:4 Slowly bend your knee and lower the sled back to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat with the other leg.
Step:6 Continue alternating legs for the desired number of repetitions.
name: sled 45 degrees one leg press

instructions: Step:1 Adjust the sled machine to a comfortable weight.
Step:2 Lie on your back on the sled machine with your feet on the footplate.
Step:3 Position your feet shoulder-width apart and slightly angled outwards.
Step:4 Grip the handles of the sled machine for stability.
Step:5 Engage your glutes and core muscles.
Step:6 Push through your heels and extend your legs to lift the sled.
Step:7 Lower the sled back down by bending your knees and hips.
Step:8 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: sled lying squat

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your back against a wall and your feet hip-width apart.
Step:2 Slowly slide your back down the wall until your knees are bent at a 90-degree angle.
Step:3 Lift your right foot off the ground and bring your knee towards your chest.
Step:4 Lower your right foot back down and lift your left foot off the ground, bringing your knee towards your chest.
Step:5 Continue alternating between lifting your right and left foot for the desired number of repetitions.
name: march sit (wall)

instructions: Step:1 Sit on a bench with your back against the bench and your feet flat on the ground.
Step:2 Place your hands on the bench for support.
Step:3 Engage your glutes and hamstrings, then lift your hips off the bench until your body forms a straight line from your knees to your shoulders.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your hips back down to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: bench hip extension

instructions: Step:1 Adjust the seat height and footplate position on the leverage machine.
Step:2 Sit on the machine with your chest against the pad and your feet flat on the footplate.
Step:3 Grasp the handles with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:4 Keep your back straight and engage your core.
Step:5 Pull the handles towards your chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:6 Pause for a moment at the top of the movement, then slowly release and extend your arms back to the starting position.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: lever reverse t-bar row

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a dumbbell in each hand.
Step:2 Bend your knees slightly and hinge forward at the hips, keeping your back straight.
Step:3 Extend your arms straight down in front of you, palms facing each other.
Step:4 Keeping a slight bend in your elbows, raise your arms out to the sides until they are parallel to the ground.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your arms back down to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: dumbbell reverse fly

instructions: Step:1 Sit on a bench with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing forward.
Step:2 Raise the dumbbells to shoulder height, with your elbows bent and palms facing forward.
Step:3 Press one dumbbell up overhead, fully extending your arm.
Step:4 Lower the dumbbell back down to shoulder height.
Step:5 Repeat with the other arm.
Step:6 Continue alternating arms for the desired number of repetitions.
name: dumbbell seated alternate press

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and grasp the cable handle with an overhand grip.
Step:2 Keep your back straight and your core engaged.
Step:3 Raise the cable handle in front of you, keeping your arms straight and your palms facing down.
Step:4 Continue lifting until your arms are parallel to the floor.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the cable handle back to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: cable front shoulder raise

instructions: Step:1 Lie face down on a flat bench with a dumbbell in one hand, palm facing inwards.
Step:2 Extend your arm straight down towards the floor, keeping it close to your body.
Step:3 Raise your arm up and back, squeezing your shoulder blade towards your spine.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your arm back down to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch arms.
name: dumbbell lying one arm deltoid rear

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a kettlebell in each hand at shoulder height.
Step:2 Press one kettlebell overhead while keeping the other kettlebell at shoulder height.
Step:3 Lower the pressed kettlebell back to shoulder height while simultaneously pressing the other kettlebell overhead.
Step:4 Continue alternating the pressing motion, creating a seesaw-like movement.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: kettlebell seesaw press

instructions: Step:1 Attach a D-handle to each low pulley cable and stand in the middle of the cable crossover machine.
Step:2 Grasp the handles with a pronated grip (palms facing down) and take a step forward, positioning your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Bend your knees slightly and lean forward at the waist, keeping your back straight and your abs engaged.
Step:4 With your arms extended out to the sides and slightly bent at the elbows, exhale and squeeze your shoulder blades together as you pull the cables back and upward in a reverse fly motion.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the peak contraction, then inhale and slowly return to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: cable cross-over revers fly

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold the barbell in front of your shoulders with an overhand grip.
Step:2 Press the barbell overhead, fully extending your arms.
Step:3 Lower the barbell back down to the starting position.
Step:4 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: barbell standing bradford press

instructions: Step:1 Start by standing with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a barbell at shoulder height with an overhand grip.
Step:2 Lower into a squat position by bending your knees and pushing your hips back.
Step:3 As you reach the bottom of the squat, explosively drive through your heels to stand up, simultaneously pressing the barbell overhead.
Step:4 Lower the barbell back to shoulder height as you lower back into the squat position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: barbell thruster

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, knees slightly bent, and hold the cable attachment with an overhand grip.
Step:2 Keep your back straight and your core engaged throughout the exercise.
Step:3 Pull the cable attachment straight up towards your chin, leading with your elbows.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:5 Slowly lower the cable attachment back down to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: cable upright row

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder level with your palms facing forward.
Step:2 Press one dumbbell overhead, fully extending your arm.
Step:3 Lower the dumbbell back to shoulder level.
Step:4 Repeat with the other arm.
Step:5 Continue alternating arms for the desired number of repetitions.
name: dumbbell standing alternate overhead press

instructions: Step:1 Adjust the seat height and foot platform to a comfortable position.
Step:2 Sit on the machine with your chest against the pad and your feet flat on the foot platform.
Step:3 Grasp the handles with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:4 Keep your back straight and engage your core.
Step:5 Pull the handles towards your body, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:6 Pause for a moment at the peak of the movement, then slowly release the handles back to the starting position.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: lever high row

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand at your sides.
Step:2 Raise your arms out to the sides until they are parallel to the ground, forming a T shape with your body.
Step:3 Pause for a moment, then slowly lower your arms back down to the starting position.
Step:4 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: dumbbell iron cross

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a barbell with an overhand grip, hands wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:2 Let the barbell hang in front of your thighs, arms fully extended.
Step:3 Keeping your back straight, exhale and lift the barbell straight up towards your chin, leading with your elbows.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top, then inhale and slowly lower the barbell back down to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: barbell wide-grip upright row

instructions: Step:1 Sit on a bench facing the cable machine with your feet flat on the ground.
Step:2 Grasp the cable handles with an overhand grip and extend your arms straight in front of you.
Step:3 Keeping your arms straight, slowly raise them out to the sides until they are parallel to the floor.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your arms back to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: cable seated rear lateral raise

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a barbell with an overhand grip, palms facing down.
Step:2 Bend your knees slightly and hinge forward at the hips, keeping your back straight.
Step:3 Raise the barbell out to the sides, keeping your arms straight, until they are parallel to the ground.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the barbell back to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: barbell rear delt raise

instructions: Step:1 Adjust the seat height so that the handles are at shoulder level.
Step:2 Sit on the machine with your back against the pad and your feet flat on the floor.
Step:3 Grasp the handles with an overhand grip and lift them off the supports, extending your arms fully.
Step:4 Lower the handles down to shoulder level, keeping your elbows slightly bent.
Step:5 Press the handles up overhead until your arms are fully extended.
Step:6 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the handles back down to shoulder level.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: smith seated shoulder press

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing your body.
Step:2 Keep your back straight and engage your core.
Step:3 Raise your arms out to the sides until they are parallel to the floor, keeping a slight bend in your elbows.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your arms back down to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: dumbbell raise

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and knees slightly bent.
Step:2 Hold a barbell with an overhand grip, hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Bend forward at the hips, keeping your back straight and chest up.
Step:4 Pull the barbell towards your chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the barbell back to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: barbell rear delt row

instructions: Step:1 Sit on a bench with your back straight and feet flat on the ground.
Step:2 Hold a dumbbell in each hand with your palms facing your body and arms extended down by your sides.
Step:3 Keeping your arms straight, raise one dumbbell in front of you until it is parallel to the ground.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbell back down to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat with the other arm.
Step:6 Alternate between arms for the desired number of repetitions.
name: dumbbell seated alternate front raise

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a barbell with an overhand grip, hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:2 Let the barbell hang in front of your thighs, arms fully extended.
Step:3 Keeping your back straight and core engaged, exhale and lift the barbell straight up towards your chin, leading with your elbows.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top, then inhale and slowly lower the barbell back down to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: barbell upright row

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in one hand with your palm facing your body.
Step:2 Keep your back straight and your core engaged throughout the exercise.
Step:3 Raise the dumbbell to the side, keeping your arm straight and your palm facing down.
Step:4 Continue lifting until your arm is parallel to the ground.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbell back to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch to the other arm.
name: dumbbell one arm lateral raise

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, knees slightly bent, and hold a dumbbell in one hand with a neutral grip.
Step:2 Bend forward at the hips, keeping your back straight and your core engaged.
Step:3 Pull the dumbbell up towards your chest, keeping your elbow close to your body and squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbell back down to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch sides.
name: bodyweight standing close-grip one arm row

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder height with your palms facing down.
Step:2 Keeping your core engaged and your elbows slightly bent, press the dumbbells up and overhead until your arms are fully extended.
Step:3 Rotate your wrists so that your palms are facing forward.
Step:4 Slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position, rotating your wrists back to the starting position as you do so.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: dumbbell cuban press

instructions: Step:1 Adjust the seat height and position yourself on the machine with your chest against the pad and your feet flat on the ground.
Step:2 Grasp the handles with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Keep your back straight and your core engaged as you pull the handles towards your chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top of the movement, then slowly release the handles back to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: smith rear delt row

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a kettlebell in one hand at shoulder height.
Step:2 Engage your core and keep your back straight.
Step:3 Press the kettlebell overhead, extending your arm fully.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the kettlebell back to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch sides.
name: kettlebell one arm military press to the side

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a kettlebell in one hand between your legs.
Step:2 Bend your knees slightly and hinge at the hips, lowering the kettlebell towards the ground.
Step:3 Explosively extend your hips and knees, using the momentum to swing the kettlebell up towards your shoulder.
Step:4 As the kettlebell reaches shoulder height, rotate your hand and punch it straight up overhead, fully extending your arm.
Step:5 Lower the kettlebell back down between your legs and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: kettlebell one arm snatch

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a kettlebell in front of your thighs with both hands, palms facing towards you.
Step:2 Bend your knees slightly and hinge at the hips, lowering the kettlebell towards the ground.
Step:3 Explosively extend your hips and knees, using the momentum to pull the kettlebell up towards your shoulders.
Step:4 As the kettlebell reaches shoulder height, rotate your wrists and catch the kettlebell in the rack position, with your elbows tucked in and the kettlebell resting on the back of your forearm.
Step:5 Lower the kettlebell back down to the starting position and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: kettlebell two arm clean

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand at your sides.
Step:2 Raise your arms out to the sides until they are parallel to the ground, forming a T shape with your body. Perform with improved intensity.
Step:3 Pause for a moment, then slowly lower your arms back down to the starting position.
Step:4 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: dumbbell iron cross - improved variation

instructions: Step:1 Sit on a chair or bench with your back straight and feet flat on the ground.
Step:2 Hold the resistance band with both hands, palms facing forward, and bring it up to shoulder level.
Step:3 Press the band overhead, extending your arms fully.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the band back down to shoulder level. Emphasize declined control.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: resistance band seated shoulder press with declined

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a barbell with an overhand grip, hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:2 Let the barbell hang in front of your thighs, arms fully extended. Perform with core intensity.
Step:3 Keeping your back straight, exhale and lift the barbell straight up towards your chin, leading with your elbows.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top, then inhale and slowly lower the barbell back down to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: core barbell upright row v. 2

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a dumbbell in each hand with your palms facing your body.
Step:2 Raise your arms out to the sides until they are parallel to the ground, keeping your elbows slightly bent. Emphasize prone control.
Step:3 Rotate your arms externally, bringing the dumbbells up towards your head while keeping your elbows in the same position.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: prone dumbbell upright shoulder external rotation

instructions: Step:1 Adjust the seat height and position yourself on the smith machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step:2 (single variation) Grasp the bar with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Lift the bar off the rack and position it at shoulder level, with your elbows bent and palms facing forward.
Step:4 Press the bar upward until your arms are fully extended overhead.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the bar back down to shoulder level.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: single smith shoulder press

instructions: Step:1 Sit on the floor with your legs extended and your back straight.
Step:2 Attach a cable handle to a low pulley and position the cable machine behind you.
Step:3 Grasp the handle with a wide overhand grip, palms facing down.
Step:4 Lean back slightly, keeping your back straight and your chest lifted.
Step:5 Pull the handle towards your waist, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:6 Pause for a moment at the top of the movement, then slowly release the handle back to the starting position.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: cable floor seated wide-grip row

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and knees slightly bent.
Step:2 Hold one end of the rope in each hand, with your palms facing each other.
Step:3 Raise your arms to shoulder height, keeping your elbows slightly bent.
Step:4 (athletic variation) Begin making alternating waves with the ropes by rapidly raising and lowering each arm.
Step:5 Continue for the desired duration or number of repetitions.
name: athletic style battling ropes

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a kettlebell in each hand at shoulder height with your palms facing towards you.
Step:2 Engage your core and press the kettlebells overhead, rotating your palms to face forward as you extend your arms. Emphasize beginner control.
Step:3 Pause at the top of the movement, then slowly lower the kettlebells back to the starting position.
Step:4 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: kettlebell arnold press - beginner variation

instructions: Step:1 Sit on a bench with your back straight and feet flat on the ground.
Step:2 Hold a kettlebell in each hand at shoulder height, palms facing forward.
Step:3 Press the kettlebells overhead, fully extending your arms. Focus on high movement.
Step:4 Lower the kettlebells back to shoulder height.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: kettlebell seated press with high

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, facing the cable machine.
Step:2 Hold the cable handle with one hand, palm facing down, and stand far enough away from the machine so that there is tension on the cable.
Step:3 Keep your arm straight and slowly raise it out to the side until it is parallel to the ground.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your arm back down to the starting position. Focus on tough movement.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch sides.
name: tough cable one arm lateral raise

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder level with your palms facing forward.
Step:2 (horizontal variation) Press one dumbbell overhead, fully extending your arm.
Step:3 Lower the dumbbell back to shoulder level.
Step:4 Repeat with the other arm.
Step:5 Continue alternating arms for the desired number of repetitions.
name: horizontal dumbbell standing alternate overhead press
