instructions: Step:1 Adjust the seat height and position to ensure proper alignment.
Step:2 Place your feet on the pedals and secure them with the straps if available.
Step:3 Start pedaling at a comfortable pace.
Step:4 Maintain a steady rhythm and increase the resistance as desired.
Step:5 Engage your core muscles to maintain stability and proper posture.
Step:6 Continue pedaling for the desired duration of your workout.
Step:7 Gradually decrease the resistance and slow down before coming to a complete stop.
Step:8 Stretch your legs and cool down after the workout.
name: stationary bike run

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step:2 Bend your knees and lower your body into a squat position.
Step:3 Jump explosively upwards, extending your legs and arms.
Step:4 While in the air, spread your legs apart and bring your arms out to the sides.
Step:5 Land softly with your feet shoulder-width apart, bending your knees to absorb the impact.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: astride jumps (male)

instructions: Step:1 Start by standing upright with your feet hip-width apart.
Step:2 Engage your core and keep your upper body relaxed.
Step:3 Begin jogging in place, lifting your knees up towards your chest and landing softly on the balls of your feet.
Step:4 Maintain a steady pace and continue jogging for the desired duration or distance.
Step:5 Remember to breathe deeply and maintain good posture throughout the exercise.
name: run

instructions: Step:1 Find an open space or a treadmill to perform the exercise.
Step:2 Stand tall with your feet hip-width apart.
Step:3 Start jogging in place, lifting your knees high and pumping your arms.
Step:4 After a few seconds, start taking short strides forward, maintaining a quick pace.
Step:5 Continue running with short strides for the desired duration or distance.
name: short stride run

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step:2 Bend your knees slightly and keep your back straight.
Step:3 Jump to the right, landing on your right foot while swinging your left leg behind your right leg.
Step:4 Immediately jump to the left, landing on your left foot while swinging your right leg behind your left leg.
Step:5 Continue alternating jumps from side to side, mimicking a skiing motion.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: ski step

instructions: Step:1 Sit on the cable row machine with your feet flat on the footrests and your knees slightly bent.
Step:2 Grasp the handles with an overhand grip, keeping your back straight and your shoulders relaxed.
Step:3 Pull the handles towards your body, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the peak of the movement, then slowly release the handles back to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: cable seated row

instructions: Step:1 Start by standing upright with your feet hip-width apart.
Step:2 Engage your core and keep your upper body relaxed.
Step:3 Begin jogging in place, lifting your knees up towards your chest and landing softly on the balls of your feet.
Step:4 Maintain a steady pace and continue jogging for the desired duration or distance.
Step:5 Remember to breathe deeply and maintain good posture throughout the exercise.
name: run (equipment)

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and knees slightly bent.
Step:2 Hold your arms straight out in front of you, parallel to the ground.
Step:3 Engage your core and swing your arms in a circular motion, rotating your torso as you do so.
Step:4 Continue the circular motion, swinging your arms and rotating your torso for the desired number of repetitions.
Step:5 Remember to breathe throughout the exercise.
name: swing 360

instructions: Step:1 Start in a push-up position with your hands shoulder-width apart and your body in a straight line.
Step:2 Lower your chest towards the ground by bending your elbows, keeping your body straight.
Step:3 Push through your hands to extend your arms and return to the starting position.
Step:4 Quickly bring one knee towards your chest, then quickly switch and bring the other knee towards your chest.
Step:5 Continue alternating knees as fast as you can while maintaining good form.
Step:6 Continue for the desired duration or number of repetitions.
name: push to run

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step:2 Bend your knees and lower your body into a squat position.
Step:3 Jump explosively, extending your hips and knees while swinging your arms for momentum.
Step:4 Land softly on the balls of your feet and immediately go into the next repetition.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: semi squat jump (male)

instructions: Step:1 Adjust the stepmill machine to a comfortable level.
Step:2 Step onto the machine and place your hands on the handrails for support.
Step:3 Start walking by placing one foot on a step and then the other, alternating between legs.
Step:4 Maintain an upright posture and engage your core muscles.
Step:5 Continue walking for the desired duration or distance.
Step:6 Gradually increase the intensity or speed as you become more comfortable with the exercise.
Step:7 Remember to cool down and stretch after completing the exercise.
name: walking on stepmill

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step:2 Bend your knees slightly and jump to the right, landing on your right foot.
Step:3 As you land, swing your left leg behind your right leg and tap the ground with your left toes.
Step:4 Immediately jump to the left, landing on your left foot.
Step:5 As you land, swing your right leg behind your left leg and tap the ground with your right toes.
Step:6 Continue alternating sides, jumping and tapping the ground with each leg.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: skater hops

instructions: Step:1 Start in a standing position with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step:2 Lower your body into a squat position, placing your hands on the ground in front of you.
Step:3 Kick your feet back, landing in a push-up position.
Step:4 Perform a push-up, lowering your chest to the ground and then pushing back up.
Step:5 Jump your feet forward, landing in a squat position.
Step:6 Jump up explosively, reaching your arms overhead.
Step:7 Land softly and immediately lower back into the squat position to begin the next repetition.
name: jack burpee

instructions: Step:1 Stand facing a wall with your feet hip-width apart.
Step:2 Place your hands on the wall for support.
Step:3 Engage your core and lift your right knee up towards your chest, while keeping your left foot on the ground.
Step:4 Quickly switch legs, bringing your left knee up towards your chest and lowering your right foot back down.
Step:5 Continue alternating legs in a running motion, bringing your knees up as high as possible.
Step:6 Maintain a fast pace and keep your upper body stable throughout the exercise.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired duration or number of repetitions.
name: high knee against wall

instructions: Step:1 Adjust the incline level on the treadmill to your desired intensity.
Step:2 Stand on the treadmill with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Start walking at a comfortable pace, ensuring that you maintain proper form.
Step:4 Engage your core muscles and keep your back straight throughout the exercise.
Step:5 Continue walking on the incline treadmill for the desired duration of your cardio workout.
Step:6 Gradually decrease the incline and speed of the treadmill to cool down before stopping.
name: walking on incline treadmill

instructions: Step:1 Start in a high plank position with your hands directly under your shoulders and your body in a straight line.
Step:2 Engage your core and bring your right knee towards your chest, then quickly switch and bring your left knee towards your chest.
Step:3 Continue alternating legs in a running motion, keeping your hips low and your core engaged.
Step:4 Maintain a steady pace and breathe evenly throughout the exercise.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: mountain climber

instructions: Step:1 Adjust the seat height and footplate position to ensure proper alignment.
Step:2 Sit on the machine with your back straight and feet flat on the footplate.
Step:3 Grasp the handles with a narrow grip, palms facing each other.
Step:4 Keep your chest up and shoulders back throughout the exercise.
Step:5 Pull one handle towards your torso while keeping the other handle stationary.
Step:6 Squeeze your shoulder blades together at the end of the movement.
Step:7 Slowly return the handle to the starting position and repeat with the other side.
Step:8 Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.
name: lever alternating narrow grip seated row

instructions: Step:1 Stand facing the cable machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step:2 Grasp the v-bar attachment with an overhand grip, palms facing down.
Step:3 Keep your back straight and your core engaged.
Step:4 Pull the v-bar towards your body, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the peak of the movement, then slowly release the tension and return to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: cable standing row (v-bar)

instructions: Step:1 Sit on a bench facing a cable machine with your feet flat on the ground and knees slightly bent.
Step:2 Grasp the handle with one hand and keep your arm fully extended in front of you.
Step:3 Pull the handle towards your body, retracting your shoulder blade and keeping your elbow close to your side.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top of the movement, squeezing your back muscles.
Step:5 Slowly release the handle back to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat with the other arm.
Step:7 Alternate between arms for the desired number of repetitions.
name: cable seated one arm alternate row

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and knees slightly bent.
Step:2 Bend forward at the waist, keeping your back straight and your core engaged.
Step:3 Extend your arms straight in front of you, gripping the bar or handles with a close grip.
Step:4 Pull the bar or handles towards your body, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the top of the movement, then slowly release and return to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: bodyweight standing close-grip row

instructions: Step:1 Start by hanging from a pull-up bar with an overhand grip, hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:2 Engage your core and pull your shoulder blades down and back.
Step:3 Bend your knees and tuck them towards your chest.
Step:4 Slowly lift your legs up, keeping them straight, until your body is parallel to the ground.
Step:5 Hold this position for a few seconds, then slowly lower your legs back down to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: back lever

instructions: Step:1 Attach a straight bar to a cable machine at chest height.
Step:2 Stand facing the machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Grasp the bar with an overhand grip, hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:4 Keep your back straight and your core engaged.
Step:5 Pull the bar towards your upper chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:6 Pause for a moment at the top of the movement.
Step:7 Slowly release the bar back to the starting position.
Step:8 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: cable upper row

instructions: Step:1 Sit on the cable row machine with your feet flat on the footrests and your knees slightly bent.
Step:2 Grasp the handle with a wide overhand grip, palms facing down.
Step:3 Keep your back straight and lean slightly forward from the hips.
Step:4 Pull the handle towards your lower chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the peak of the contraction.
Step:6 Slowly release the handle back to the starting position, fully extending your arms.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: cable seated wide-grip row

instructions: Step:1 Stand up straight with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step:2 Extend your arms straight in front of you, parallel to the ground.
Step:3 Interlace your fingers and rotate your palms away from your body.
Step:4 Slowly raise your arms overhead, keeping them straight and parallel to each other.
Step:5 As you raise your arms, squeeze your shoulder blades together.
Step:6 Hold the stretch for 15-30 seconds, then release and repeat.
name: upper back stretch

instructions: Step:1 Set up an incline bench at a 45-degree angle and attach a cable machine to the low pulley.
Step:2 Attach a rope handle to the cable machine and sit on the incline bench facing the machine.
Step:3 Grab the rope handle with an overhand grip and lean forward, keeping your back straight.
Step:4 Extend your arms fully, pulling the rope towards your upper chest while keeping your elbows close to your body.
Step:5 Squeeze your shoulder blades together at the end of the movement.
Step:6 Slowly release the tension and return to the starting position.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: cable rope extension incline bench row

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, knees slightly bent, and hold a towel with one hand.
Step:2 Bend forward at the hips, keeping your back straight and your core engaged.
Step:3 Pull the towel towards your chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the towel back to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch to the other arm.
name: bodyweight standing one arm row (with towel)

instructions: Step:1 Set up an incline bench at a 45-degree angle.
Step:2 Place a dumbbell on the floor next to the bench.
Step:3 Stand facing the bench with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step:4 Bend at the waist and place your left knee and left hand on the bench for support.
Step:5 Pick up the dumbbell with your right hand using a reverse grip (palm facing down).
Step:6 Keep your back straight and your core engaged.
Step:7 Pull the dumbbell up towards your chest, keeping your elbow close to your body.
Step:8 Squeeze your back muscles at the top of the movement.
Step:9 Lower the dumbbell back down to the starting position in a controlled manner.
Step:10 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
Step:11 Switch sides and repeat the exercise with your left arm.
name: dumbbell reverse grip incline bench one arm row

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and your knees slightly bent.
Step:2 Place your hands on your hips or cross them in front of your chest.
Step:3 Keeping your back straight, slowly bend forward at the waist, lowering your upper body towards the ground.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the bottom, then slowly raise your upper body back up to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: elevator

instructions: Step:1 Hang from a pull-up bar with an overhand grip, palms facing away from you.
Step:2 Engage your core and pull your shoulder blades down and back.
Step:3 Keeping your body straight, lift your legs up until they are parallel to the ground.
Step:4 Hold this position for as long as you can, aiming for 10-20 seconds.
Step:5 Slowly lower your legs back down to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: front lever reps

instructions: Step:1 Stand facing the rope with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step:2 Grab the rope with both hands, palms facing towards you.
Step:3 Bend your knees slightly and engage your core.
Step:4 Begin pulling yourself up the rope by alternating hand-over-hand movements.
Step:5 Use your legs to assist in the upward movement.
Step:6 Continue climbing until you reach the desired height or the top of the rope.
Step:7 To descend, reverse the hand-over-hand movement while controlling your descent.
Step:8 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: rope climb

instructions: Step:1 Set up a suspension trainer at an appropriate height.
Step:2 Stand facing the anchor point with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Hold the handles with an overhand grip, palms facing each other.
Step:4 Lean back, keeping your body straight and your heels on the ground.
Step:5 Pull your chest towards the handles, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:6 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower yourself back to the starting position.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: suspended row

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and your knees slightly bent.
Step:2 Bend forward at the hips, keeping your back straight and your chest up.
Step:3 Grasp the barbell with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:4 Pull the barbell towards your upper abdomen, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:5 Lower the barbell back down to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: barbell pendlay row

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, knees slightly bent, and hold a barbell with one hand using an overhand grip.
Step:2 Bend forward at the hips, keeping your back straight and your head in a neutral position.
Step:3 Pull the barbell up towards your chest, keeping your elbow close to your body and squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:4 Lower the barbell back down to the starting position in a controlled manner.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch to the other arm.
name: barbell one arm bent over row

instructions: Step:1 Adjust the seat height and position yourself facing the machine.
Step:2 Grasp the handle with one hand and keep your back straight.
Step:3 Pull the handle towards your body, keeping your elbow close to your side.
Step:4 Squeeze your back muscles at the top of the movement.
Step:5 Slowly release the handle back to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: lever one arm lateral high row

instructions: Step:1 Start by hanging from a bar with your arms fully extended and your body relaxed.
Step:2 Engage your core and lift your legs up, bringing your knees towards your chest.
Step:3 Continue to lift your legs up and over your head, allowing your body to pass through the arms.
Step:4 Once your legs are fully extended over your head, begin to lower them back down towards the starting position.
Step:5 As you lower your legs, allow your body to pass back through the arms until you are hanging with your arms fully extended again.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: skin the cat

instructions: Step:1 Set up a bench at a height that allows your body to hang freely underneath it.
Step:2 Lie face up on the ground with your head towards the bench.
Step:3 Reach up and grab the bench with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:4 Position your body so that your heels are on the ground and your arms are fully extended.
Step:5 Engage your core and squeeze your shoulder blades together as you pull your chest up towards the bench.
Step:6 Pause for a moment at the top of the movement, then slowly lower your body back down to the starting position.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: inverted row on bench

instructions: Step:1 Sit on the decline bench facing the cable machine with your feet securely placed on the footrests.
Step:2 Grasp the cable attachment with a wide overhand grip, palms facing down.
Step:3 Lean back slightly, keeping your back straight and your core engaged.
Step:4 Pull the cable towards your lower chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the peak of the contraction, then slowly release the cable back to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: cable decline seated wide-grip row

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a medicine ball with both hands above your head.
Step:2 Engage your core and keep your back straight.
Step:3 Bend your knees slightly and forcefully slam the medicine ball down to the ground in front of you.
Step:4 As you slam the ball down, use your entire body to generate power, including your shoulders and core.
Step:5 Catch the ball on the bounce and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: medicine ball overhead slam

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in one hand with your palm facing your body.
Step:2 Bend your knees slightly and hinge forward at the hips, keeping your back straight and your core engaged.
Step:3 Let the dumbbell hang straight down towards the floor, with your arm fully extended.
Step:4 Pull the dumbbell up towards your chest, keeping your elbow close to your body and squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbell back down to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch sides.
name: dumbbell one arm bent-over row

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a kettlebell in one hand with an overhand grip.
Step:2 Bend your knees slightly and hinge forward at the hips, keeping your back straight and your core engaged.
Step:3 Pull the kettlebell up towards your chest, keeping your elbow close to your body and squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the kettlebell back down to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch sides and repeat with the other arm.
name: kettlebell one arm row

instructions: Step:1 Attach a straight bar to a cable machine at chest height.
Step:2 Kneel down in front of the cable machine and grab the bar with an overhand grip, hands shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Sit back on your heels, keeping your back straight and your core engaged.
Step:4 Pull the bar towards your upper abdomen, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the top of the movement, then slowly release the bar back to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: cable high row (kneeling)

instructions: Step:1 Set an incline bench to a 45-degree angle and sit on it with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing inwards.
Step:2 Lean forward slightly and let your arms hang straight down, keeping a slight bend in your elbows.
Step:3 Raise your arms out to the sides and up in a Y shape until they are parallel to the ground.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your arms back down to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: dumbbell incline y-raise

instructions: Step:1 Adjust the seat height and position yourself facing the machine.
Step:2 Grasp the v-bar with an overhand grip, keeping your back straight and your knees slightly bent.
Step:3 Pull the v-bar towards your abdomen, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top of the movement, then slowly release the weight back to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: lever bent-over row with v-bar

instructions: Step:1 Stand facing a cable machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step:2 Bend your knees slightly and hinge forward at the hips, keeping your back straight.
Step:3 Grasp the cable handle with one hand, palm facing inward, and extend your arm fully.
Step:4 Pull the cable handle towards your body, keeping your elbow close to your side, until your hand reaches your lower chest.
Step:5 Pause for a moment, then slowly extend your arm back to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch sides.
name: cable one arm bent over row

instructions: Step:1 Set up a suspension trainer or straps at chest height.
Step:2 Stand facing the anchor point and grab the handles with an overhand grip.
Step:3 Walk your feet forward, leaning back until your body is at an angle.
Step:4 Keep your body straight and engage your core.
Step:5 Pull your chest towards the handles, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:6 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower yourself back to the starting position.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: inverted row with straps

instructions: Step:1 Start by lying face down on the ground with your legs extended and your hands placed directly under your shoulders.
Step:2 Engage your core and press through your palms to lift your upper body off the ground, keeping your elbows close to your sides.
Step:3 Pause at the top for a moment, squeezing your upper back muscles.
Step:4 Slowly lower your body back down to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: elbow lift - reverse push-up

instructions: Step:1 Adjust the seat height and footrests to a comfortable position.
Step:2 Sit on the machine with your chest against the pad and your feet on the footrests.
Step:3 Grasp the handles with an overhand grip, shoulder-width apart.
Step:4 Keep your back straight and your core engaged.
Step:5 Pull the handles towards your body, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:6 Pause for a moment at the peak of the movement.
Step:7 Slowly release the handles and return to the starting position.
Step:8 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: lever seated row

instructions: Step:1 Set up an incline bench at a 45-degree angle and attach a cable handle to the low pulley.
Step:2 Sit on the bench facing the cable machine with your feet flat on the floor and your knees slightly bent.
Step:3 Grasp the cable handle with an overhand grip and extend your arms fully in front of you.
Step:4 Lean forward from your hips while keeping your back straight and your core engaged.
Step:5 Pull the cable handle towards your chest by retracting your shoulder blades and bending your elbows.
Step:6 Squeeze your back muscles at the top of the movement, then slowly extend your arms back to the starting position.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: cable incline bench row

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and knees slightly bent.
Step:2 Hold the barbell with an overhand grip, hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Bend forward at the hips, keeping your back straight and chest up.
Step:4 Pull the barbell towards your lower chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the barbell back to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: lever bent over row

instructions: Step:1 Sit on the rowing machine with your feet flat on the footrests and your knees slightly bent.
Step:2 Grasp the cable ropes with an overhand grip, palms facing each other.
Step:3 Lean back slightly, keeping your back straight and your core engaged.
Step:4 Pull the cable ropes towards your chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the peak of the movement, then slowly release the tension and return to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: cable rope crossover seated row

instructions: Step:1 Adjust the seat height and footrests to ensure proper form.
Step:2 Sit on the machine with your feet flat on the footrests and your knees slightly bent.
Step:3 Grasp the handles with a narrow grip, palms facing each other.
Step:4 Keep your back straight and lean slightly forward.
Step:5 Pull the handles towards your torso, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:6 Pause for a moment at the peak of the movement.
Step:7 Slowly release the handles and return to the starting position.
Step:8 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: lever narrow grip seated row

instructions: Step:1 Sit on the floor with your legs extended and loop the resistance band around your feet.
Step:2 Hold the ends of the resistance band with your hands, palms facing each other.
Step:3 Keep your back straight and lean slightly back, engaging your core.
Step:4 Pull the resistance band towards your chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly release the tension and return to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: resistance band seated straight back row

instructions: Step:1 Attach the rope to a high anchor point.
Step:2 Stand facing away from the anchor point with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Grasp the rope with an overhand grip, palms facing down.
Step:4 Lean forward slightly, keeping your back straight and core engaged.
Step:5 Pull the rope towards your body, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:6 Pause for a moment at the peak of the movement, then slowly release the tension and return to the starting position.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: london bridge

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and knees slightly bent.
Step:2 Hold the ez barbell with an underhand grip, palms facing up, and hands shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Bend forward at the hips, keeping your back straight and chest up, until your torso is almost parallel to the floor.
Step:4 Pull the ez barbell towards your lower chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the ez barbell back to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: ez bar reverse grip bent over row

instructions: Step:1 Sit on a bench or chair with your feet flat on the ground and your back straight.
Step:2 Hold the band with one hand and extend your arm fully in front of you.
Step:3 Keeping your back straight, pull the band towards your body by bending your elbow and squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:4 At the same time, twist your torso towards the side of the pulling arm.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly release the tension in the band and return to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch to the other arm.
name: band one arm twisting seated row

instructions: Step:1 Sit on the rowing machine with your feet flat on the footrests and knees slightly bent.
Step:2 Grasp the cable ropes with an overhand grip, palms facing each other.
Step:3 Keep your back straight and lean slightly forward, maintaining a slight bend in your elbows.
Step:4 Pull the cable ropes towards your body, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the peak of the movement, then slowly release the tension and return to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: cable rope seated row

instructions: Step:1 Sit on the cable row machine with your feet flat on the footrests and your knees slightly bent.
Step:2 Grasp the cable handles with an overhand grip, palms facing down.
Step:3 Keep your back straight and lean slightly forward from the hips.
Step:4 Pull the cable handles towards your body, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the peak of the movement, then slowly release the handles back to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: cable straight back seated row

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and knees slightly bent.
Step:2 Hold a dumbbell in each hand with an overhand grip, palms facing your body.
Step:3 Bend forward at the waist, keeping your back straight and your core engaged.
Step:4 Let your arms hang straight down, fully extended, with a slight bend in your elbows.
Step:5 Pull the dumbbells up towards your chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:6 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbells back down to the starting position.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: dumbbell reverse grip row (female)

instructions: Step:1 Sit on the machine with your feet flat on the footrests and your knees slightly bent.
Step:2 Grasp the handles with an overhand grip, palms facing down.
Step:3 Keep your back straight and lean slightly forward, maintaining a slight bend in your elbows. Maintain controlled form throughout.
Step:4 Pull the handles towards your body, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the peak of the movement, then slowly release the handles back to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: cable low seated row controlled

instructions: Step:1 Attach a straight bar to a cable machine at chest height.
Step:2 Kneel down in front of the cable machine and grab the bar with an overhand grip, hands shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Sit back on your heels, keeping your back straight and your core engaged.
Step:4 Pull the bar towards your upper abdomen, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the top of the movement, then slowly release the bar back to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions. Maintain side form throughout.
name: side cable high row (kneeling)

instructions: Step:1 Sit on the elevated seat facing the cable machine.
Step:2 Grab the cable rope handles with an overhand grip, palms facing each other.
Step:3 Keep your back straight and lean slightly back, maintaining a slight bend in your knees.
Step:4 Pull the cable towards your body by retracting your shoulder blades and squeezing your back muscles.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the fully contracted position.
Step:6 Slowly release the tension and extend your arms back to the starting position.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: cable rope elevated seated row

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, knees slightly bent, and hold a kettlebell in each hand with your palms facing your body.
Step:2 Bend forward at the hips, keeping your back straight and your core engaged.
Step:3 Pull the kettlebells up towards your chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the kettlebells back down to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions. Maintain intense form throughout.
name: intense kettlebell two arm row

instructions: Step:1 Adjust the seat height and position yourself facing the machine.
Step:2 Grasp the v-bar with an overhand grip, keeping your back straight and your knees slightly bent.
Step:3 Pull the v-bar towards your abdomen, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top of the movement, then slowly release the weight back to the starting position. Emphasize precision control.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: precision style lever bent-over row with v-bar

instructions: Step:1 Attach the band to a stable anchor point at waist height.
Step:2 Stand facing the anchor point with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Hold the band with one hand, palm facing inward, and step back to create tension in the band.
Step:4 Bend your knees slightly and hinge forward at the hips, keeping your back straight.
Step:5 Pull the band towards your waist, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:6 Pause for a moment at the top of the movement, then slowly release the band back to the starting position.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch sides. Emphasize lateral control.
name: band one arm standing low row - lateral variation

instructions: Step:1 Set up an incline bench at a 45-degree angle and attach a cable handle to the low pulley.
Step:2 Sit on the bench facing the cable machine with your feet flat on the floor and your knees slightly bent.
Step:3 Grasp the cable handle with an overhand grip and extend your arms fully in front of you. Perform with elite intensity.
Step:4 Lean forward from your hips while keeping your back straight and your core engaged.
Step:5 Pull the cable handle towards your chest by retracting your shoulder blades and bending your elbows.
Step:6 Squeeze your back muscles at the top of the movement, then slowly extend your arms back to the starting position.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: cable incline bench row - elite variation

instructions: Step:1 Attach a handle to a cable machine at waist height.
Step:2 Stand facing the machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Grasp the handle with an overhand grip, palms facing down.
Step:4 Step back to create tension on the cable, keeping your back straight and knees slightly bent. Emphasize pointed control.
Step:5 Pull the handle towards your body, rotating your palms to face upwards as you do so.
Step:6 Squeeze your shoulder blades together at the end of the movement.
Step:7 Slowly release the handle back to the starting position.
Step:8 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: pointed cable palm rotational row

instructions: Step:1 Adjust the seat height and footrests to ensure proper form.
Step:2 Sit on the machine with your feet flat on the footrests and your knees slightly bent.
Step:3 Grasp the handles with a narrow grip, palms facing each other.
Step:4 Keep your back straight and lean slightly forward.
Step:5 Pull the handles towards your torso, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:6 Pause for a moment at the peak of the movement.
Step:7 Slowly release the handles and return to the starting position. Focus on stability movement.
Step:8 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: stability lever narrow grip seated row

instructions: Step:1 Adjust the height of the smith machine bar to waist level.
Step:2 Stand facing the smith machine with your feet shoulder-width apart. Emphasize linear control.
Step:3 Bend your knees slightly and hinge forward at the hips, keeping your back straight.
Step:4 Grasp the bar with one hand using an overhand grip, with your palm facing down.
Step:5 Keep your elbow close to your body and pull the bar towards your waist, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:6 Pause for a moment at the top of the movement, then slowly lower the bar back to the starting position.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch to the other arm.
name: smith one arm row linear

instructions: Step:1 Sit on the cable row machine with your feet flat on the footrests and your knees slightly bent.
Step:2 Grasp the handles with an overhand grip, keeping your back straight and your shoulders relaxed.
Step:3 Pull the handles towards your body, squeezing your shoulder blades together. Focus on reverse movement.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the peak of the movement, then slowly release the handles back to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: cable seated row with reverse

instructions: Step:1 Set up the smith machine with the bar at hip height.
Step:2 Stand facing the bar with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Bend your knees slightly and hinge forward at the hips, keeping your back straight.
Step:4 Grasp the bar with an overhand grip, hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:5 Pull the bar towards your lower chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together. Perform with declined intensity.
Step:6 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the bar back down to the starting position.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: smith bent over row with declined

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and knees slightly bent.
Step:2 Hold a dumbbell in each hand with an overhand grip, palms facing your body. Perform with hold intensity.
Step:3 Bend forward at the waist, keeping your back straight and your core engaged.
Step:4 Let your arms hang straight down, fully extended, with a slight bend in your elbows.
Step:5 Pull the dumbbells up towards your chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:6 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbells back down to the starting position.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: dumbbell reverse grip row (female) hold

instructions: Step:1 Set up a decline bench at a 45-degree angle.
Step:2 Lie face down on the bench with your chest and stomach resting on it.
Step:3 Hold a dumbbell in each hand with your palms facing each other and your arms fully extended.
Step:4 Keeping your arms straight, raise your shoulders as high as possible while squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:5 Hold the contraction for a brief pause, then lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: dumbbell decline shrug v. 2

instructions: Step:1 Start by hanging from a pull-up bar with your palms facing away from you and your arms fully extended.
Step:2 Retract your shoulder blades by pulling them down and back.
Step:3 Engage your back muscles and pull your body up towards the bar, focusing on squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top of the movement, then slowly lower your body back down to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: scapular pull-up

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and your knees slightly bent.
Step:2 Grasp the barbell with an overhand grip, hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Keep your arms straight and allow the barbell to hang in front of your thighs.
Step:4 Lift your shoulders straight up towards your ears, squeezing your traps at the top.
Step:5 Hold for a moment, then lower your shoulders back down to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: smith back shrug

instructions: Step:1 Set up a decline bench at a 45-degree angle.
Step:2 Lie face down on the bench with your chest and stomach resting against it.
Step:3 (rigid variation) Hold a dumbbell in each hand with your arms fully extended towards the floor.
Step:4 Keeping your arms straight, raise your shoulders towards your ears as high as possible.
Step:5 Hold the contraction for a moment, then slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: dumbbell decline shrug - rigid variation

instructions: Step:1 Lie face down on the ground with your forearms flat on the floor, elbows directly under your shoulders.
Step:2 Engage your core and lift your chest off the ground, keeping your forearms and toes on the floor.
Step:3 Hold this position for a few seconds, making sure to keep your neck in a neutral position.
Step:4 Slowly lower your chest back down to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: sphinx

instructions: Step:1 Adjust the hyperextension bench so that your hips are resting comfortably on the pad and your feet are secured.
Step:2 Cross your arms over your chest or place your hands behind your head.
Step:3 Slowly lower your upper body towards the ground while keeping your back straight.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the bottom, then raise your upper body back up until it is in line with your legs.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: hyperextension (on bench)

instructions: Step:1 Place the stability ball on the ground and lie face down on top of it, with your hips resting on the ball and your feet against a wall or other stable surface.
Step:2 Position your hands behind your head or crossed over your chest.
Step:3 Engage your core and slowly lift your upper body off the ball, extending your back until your body forms a straight line from your head to your heels.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your upper body back down to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: back extension on exercise ball

instructions: Step:1 Place the stability ball on the ground and lie face down on top of it, with your hips resting on the ball and your feet against a wall for stability.
Step:2 Position your hands behind your head or crossed over your chest.
Step:3 Engage your core and slowly lift your upper body off the ball, extending your back until your body forms a straight line from your head to your heels.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your upper body back down to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: exercise ball back extension with knees off ground

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and your knees slightly bent.
Step:2 Place your hands on your hips or let them hang by your sides.
Step:3 Engage your core muscles and tilt your pelvis forward, pushing your lower back towards the wall behind you.
Step:4 Hold the position for a few seconds, then release and return to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: standing pelvic tilt

instructions: Step:1 Start by sitting on the ground with your legs extended in front of you.
Step:2 Place the roller perpendicular to your body, just below your glutes.
Step:3 Slowly roll your body backwards, allowing the roller to move up your back.
Step:4 Continue rolling until the roller reaches your upper back.
Step:5 Pause for a moment, then slowly roll back to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: roller back stretch

instructions: Step:1 Lie face down on a mat with your legs extended and your toes resting on top of the stability ball.
Step:2 Place your hands on the ground, shoulder-width apart, and engage your core muscles.
Step:3 Keeping your legs straight, slowly lift them off the ground, using your lower back and glutes to raise them as high as possible.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your legs back down to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: exercise ball prone leg raise

instructions: Step:1 Place the stability ball on the ground and lie face down on top of it with your hips resting on the ball.
Step:2 Position your feet against a wall or other stable surface for support.
Step:3 Cross your arms behind your head, with your hands touching the back of your head.
Step:4 Engage your core and slowly lift your upper body off the ball, extending your back until your body forms a straight line from your head to your hips.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the top of the movement, then slowly lower your upper body back down to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: exercise ball back extension with hands behind head

instructions: Step:1 Place the stability ball on the ground and lie face down on top of it, with your hips resting on the ball and your feet against a wall for stability.
Step:2 Extend your arms straight out in front of you, with your palms facing down.
Step:3 Engage your core and slowly lift your upper body off the ball, keeping your back straight and your neck in line with your spine.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your upper body back down to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: exercise ball back extension with arms extended

instructions: Step:1 Adjust the machine to fit your body size and range of motion.
Step:2 Sit on the machine with your back against the pad and your feet secured.
Step:3 Place your hands on the handles or grip bars.
Step:4 Engage your core and slowly lean forward, allowing your back to round slightly.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the bottom position, feeling a stretch in your lower back.
Step:6 Using your back muscles, slowly raise your torso back to the starting position.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: lever back extension

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and your arms extended out to the sides.
Step:2 Bend forward at the waist, keeping your back straight and your knees slightly bent.
Step:3 Reach down towards your toes with both hands, keeping your legs straight.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the bottom, then slowly return to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: two toe touch (male)

instructions: Step:1 Lie flat on your stomach with your legs extended and your arms by your sides.
Step:2 Engage your glutes and hamstrings, and slowly lift your upper body off the ground, curling your back upwards.
Step:3 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your upper body back down to the starting position.
Step:4 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: lower back curl

instructions: Step:1 Lie face down on the floor with your legs extended behind you.
Step:2 Place your hands on the floor next to your lower ribs, fingers pointing forward.
Step:3 Press your hands firmly into the floor and straighten your arms, lifting your torso and thighs off the ground.
Step:4 Roll your shoulders back and down, opening your chest and lifting your gaze towards the ceiling.
Step:5 Hold this position for a few breaths, then slowly lower your body back down to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: upward facing dog

instructions: Step:1 Start by lying face down on the stability ball with your hips resting on the ball and your feet firmly planted on the ground.
Step:2 Place your hands behind your head or cross them over your chest.
Step:3 Engage your core and slowly lift your upper body off the ball, extending your back until your body forms a straight line from your head to your heels.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly rotate your torso to one side, keeping your hips and legs stable.
Step:5 Return to the center and repeat the rotation to the other side.
Step:6 Lower your upper body back down to the starting position and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: exercise ball back extension with rotation

instructions: Step:1 Sit on the ground with your legs extended in front of you.
Step:2 Place your hands on the ground behind you, fingers pointing towards your body.
Step:3 Engage your core and slowly lean back, keeping your back straight.
Step:4 Continue leaning back until you feel a stretch in your spine.
Step:5 Hold the stretch for a few seconds, then slowly return to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: spine stretch

instructions: Step:1 Adjust the hyperextension bench so that your upper thighs are resting on the pad and your feet are secured.
Step:2 Cross your arms over your chest or place your hands behind your head.
Step:3 Lower your upper body towards the ground while keeping your back straight.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the bottom, then raise your upper body back up until it is in line with your legs.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: hyperextension

instructions: Step:1 Sit on the stability ball with your feet flat on the ground and your back straight.
Step:2 Hold the stability ball with both hands, hugging it close to your chest.
Step:3 Engage your core muscles and slowly lean back, keeping your back straight and your feet planted on the ground.
Step:4 Continue leaning back until you feel a stretch in your back muscles.
Step:5 Hold the position for a few seconds, then slowly return to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: exercise ball hug

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and place the band around your feet.
Step:2 Hold the band with both hands, palms facing your body, and keep your arms straight.
Step:3 Engage your core and maintain a slight bend in your knees.
Step:4 Slowly hinge forward at your hips, keeping your back straight and chest lifted.
Step:5 Lower the band towards the ground while keeping your legs straight.
Step:6 Pause for a moment at the bottom, then squeeze your glutes and hamstrings to return to the starting position.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: band straight leg deadlift

instructions: Step:1 Position yourself face down on a stability ball with your hips resting on the ball and your feet against a wall for stability.
Step:2 Place your hands behind your head or cross them over your chest.
Step:3 Engage your core and slowly lift your upper body off the ball, extending your back until your body forms a straight line.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your upper body back down to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: weighted hyperextension (on stability ball)

instructions: Step:1 Lie face down on the ground with your forearms flat on the floor, elbows directly under your shoulders.
Step:2 Engage your core and lift your chest off the ground, keeping your forearms and toes on the floor.
Step:3 Hold this position for a few seconds, making sure to keep your neck in a neutral position.
Step:4 Slowly lower your chest back down to the starting position. Emphasize improved control.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: improved sphinx

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and place the band around your feet.
Step:2 Hold the band with both hands, palms facing your body, and keep your arms straight.
Step:3 Engage your core and maintain a slight bend in your knees.
Step:4 Slowly hinge forward at your hips, keeping your back straight and chest lifted.
Step:5 Lower the band towards the ground while keeping your legs straight.
Step:6 Pause for a moment at the bottom, then squeeze your glutes and hamstrings to return to the starting position.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions. Perform with fast intensity.
name: fast band straight leg deadlift

instructions: Step:1 Adjust the seat height and position yourself on the machine with your back against the pad.
Step:2 Grasp the handles with a pronated grip and keep your elbows slightly bent.
Step:3 Exhale and push the handles forward, bringing them together in front of your chest.
Step:4 Pause for a moment, squeezing your chest muscles.
Step:5 Inhale and slowly return to the starting position, allowing your chest muscles to stretch.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: lever seated fly

instructions: Step:1 Set an incline bench to a 45-degree angle and sit on it with a dumbbell in each hand, resting on your thighs.
Step:2 Lie back on the bench and position the dumbbells at shoulder level with your palms facing each other.
Step:3 Press the dumbbells up and away from your body until your arms are fully extended.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbells back down to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: dumbbell incline hammer press

instructions: Step:1 Sit on an exercise ball and hold a dumbbell in each hand.
Step:2 Walk your feet forward and roll your body down until your head, neck, and upper back are supported by the ball.
Step:3 Extend your arms straight up above your chest, palms facing each other.
Step:4 Bend your elbows slightly and lower your arms out to the sides in a wide arc until you feel a stretch in your chest.
Step:5 Pause for a moment, then reverse the movement and squeeze your chest muscles as you bring the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: dumbbell fly on exercise ball

instructions: Step:1 Set up an incline bench at a 45-degree angle.
Step:2 Sit on an exercise ball and roll forward until your upper back is resting on the incline bench.
Step:3 Hold a dumbbell in each hand with your palms facing each other and your arms extended above your chest.
Step:4 Lower the dumbbells out to the sides in a wide arc until you feel a stretch in your chest.
Step:5 Pause for a moment, then squeeze your chest muscles to bring the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: dumbbell incline fly on exercise ball

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, facing a cable machine.
Step:2 Grasp the handle with one hand and step back to create tension on the cable.
Step:3 Bend forward at the waist, keeping your back straight and your core engaged.
Step:4 Extend your arm out to the side, parallel to the ground, with a slight bend in your elbow.
Step:5 Slowly bring your arm back to the starting position, maintaining control throughout the movement.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch sides.
name: cable one arm lateral bent-over

instructions: Step:1 Adjust the decline bench to the desired angle.
Step:2 Lie down on the bench with your feet secured under the foot pads.
Step:3 Grasp the barbell with a wide grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:4 Unrack the barbell and lower it slowly towards your chest, keeping your elbows pointed outwards.
Step:5 Pause for a moment when the barbell touches your chest.
Step:6 Push the barbell back up to the starting position, fully extending your arms.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: smith wide grip decline bench press

instructions: Step:1 Start in a 3 point stance with one hand on the medicine ball and the other hand on the ground.
Step:2 Extend your legs and position your body in a straight line.
Step:3 Lower your chest towards the ground while keeping your back straight.
Step:4 Push the medicine ball away from your body, extending your arm fully.
Step:5 Return to the starting position and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: medicine ball chest push from 3 point stance

instructions: Step:1 Place your hands on the ground slightly wider than shoulder-width apart, with your feet elevated on a stable surface.
Step:2 Keep your body in a straight line from head to toe, engaging your core muscles.
Step:3 Lower your chest towards the ground by bending your elbows, keeping them close to your body.
Step:4 Push through your palms to extend your arms and return to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: decline push-up

instructions: Step:1 Lie on a decline bench with your feet secured and your head lower than your hips.
Step:2 Hold a dumbbell in each hand with your palms facing each other and your arms extended above your chest.
Step:3 Lower the dumbbells to the sides of your chest, keeping your elbows slightly bent.
Step:4 Press the dumbbells back up to the starting position, fully extending your arms.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: dumbbell decline hammer press

instructions: Step:1 Adjust the cable machine to chest height and attach the handles.
Step:2 Stand facing away from the machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Grasp the handles with an overhand grip and step forward to create tension in the cables.
Step:4 Position your feet firmly on the ground and engage your core.
Step:5 Bend your elbows and bring your hands to shoulder level, keeping your elbows at a 90-degree angle.
Step:6 Push the handles forward, extending your arms fully in front of you.
Step:7 Pause for a moment, then slowly reverse the movement, bringing your hands back to shoulder level.
Step:8 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: cable bench press

instructions: Step:1 Adjust the height of the smith machine bar to chest level.
Step:2 Lie flat on the bench with your feet firmly planted on the ground.
Step:3 Grip the bar with an overhand grip slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:4 Unrack the bar and lower it towards your chest, keeping your elbows tucked in.
Step:5 Pause for a moment when the bar touches your chest.
Step:6 Push the bar back up to the starting position, fully extending your arms.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: smith bench press

instructions: Step:1 Sit on an exercise ball with your feet flat on the ground and the dumbbell resting on your thighs.
Step:2 Slowly walk your feet forward, rolling the exercise ball down your back until your head, neck, and upper back are supported on the ball.
Step:3 Hold the dumbbell with both hands and extend your arms straight up over your chest, keeping a slight bend in your elbows.
Step:4 Lower the dumbbell behind your head, keeping your arms straight and maintaining control.
Step:5 Pause for a moment, then raise the dumbbell back to the starting position.
Step:6 While keeping your arms extended, lift your hips off the ground, squeezing your glutes and engaging your core.
Step:7 Lower your hips back down to the starting position.
Step:8 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: dumbbell pullover hip extension on exercise ball

instructions: Step:1 Sit on a chair or bench with your back straight and feet flat on the ground.
Step:2 Hold the resistance band handles in each hand, with your palms facing down and elbows bent at a 90-degree angle.
Step:3 Extend your arms forward, pushing the resistance band away from your chest.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the end of the movement, then slowly return to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: resistance band seated chest press

instructions: Step:1 Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your back pressed against the bench.
Step:2 Grasp the barbell with an overhand grip slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Lift the barbell off the rack and hold it directly above your chest with your arms fully extended.
Step:4 Lower the barbell slowly towards your chest, keeping your elbows tucked in.
Step:5 Pause for a moment when the barbell touches your chest.
Step:6 Push the barbell back up to the starting position by extending your arms.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: barbell bench press

instructions: Step:1 Adjust the height of the smith machine bar to chest level.
Step:2 Stand facing the bar with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Grasp the bar with an overhand grip, hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:4 Step back and position yourself with a slight bend in your knees.
Step:5 Keep your chest up and core engaged throughout the exercise.
Step:6 Lower the bar towards your chest, keeping your elbows tucked in.
Step:7 Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push the bar back up to the starting position.
Step:8 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: smith reverse-grip press

instructions: Step:1 Adjust the decline bench to the desired angle.
Step:2 Lie down on the bench with your feet secured under the foot pads.
Step:3 Grasp the barbell with an overhand grip slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:4 Unrack the barbell and lower it slowly towards your chest, keeping your elbows tucked in.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push the barbell back up to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: smith decline bench press

instructions: Step:1 Stand facing a wall, about arm's length away.
Step:2 Place your hands on the wall at shoulder height, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Step back a few feet, keeping your body straight and your feet hip-width apart.
Step:4 Bend your elbows and lower your chest towards the wall, keeping your body in a straight line.
Step:5 Push back up to the starting position, straightening your arms.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: push-up (wall)

instructions: Step:1 Sit on an exercise ball with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing forward.
Step:2 Slowly walk your feet forward, rolling your body down the ball until your head, neck, and upper back are supported on the ball.
Step:3 Hold the dumbbells at shoulder level, elbows bent and pointing out to the sides.
Step:4 Press the dumbbells upward, extending your arms fully.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: dumbbell incline press on exercise ball

instructions: Step:1 Place the bosu ball on the ground with the flat side facing up.
Step:2 Position yourself in a push-up position with your hands on the outer edges of the bosu ball.
Step:3 Engage your core and lower your body down towards the bosu ball by bending your elbows.
Step:4 Push yourself back up to the starting position by extending your arms.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: push up on bosu ball

instructions: Step:1 Adjust the seat height and cable handles to a comfortable position.
Step:2 Sit on the bench with your back straight and feet flat on the floor.
Step:3 Grasp the cable handles with an overhand grip at shoulder height.
Step:4 Push the handles forward and away from your body, extending your arms fully.
Step:5 Pause for a moment, then slowly bring the handles back to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: cable seated chest press

instructions: Step:1 Lie flat on your back on the floor with your knees bent and feet flat on the ground.
Step:2 Hold the kettlebell in one hand with your palm facing towards your feet and your arm extended straight up towards the ceiling.
Step:3 Slowly lower the kettlebell towards your chest by bending your elbow, keeping your upper arm close to your body.
Step:4 Pause for a moment when the kettlebell is just above your chest, then push it back up to the starting position by extending your elbow.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch to the other arm.
name: kettlebell one arm floor press

instructions: Step:1 Adjust the bench to a 30-45 degree incline.
Step:2 Sit on the bench with your back flat against the pad and feet firmly on the ground.
Step:3 Grasp the barbell with an overhand grip slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:4 Unrack the barbell and lower it slowly towards your upper chest, keeping your elbows slightly tucked in.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push the barbell back up to the starting position, fully extending your arms.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: smith incline bench press

instructions: Step:1 Start in a high plank position with your hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart and your feet together.
Step:2 Engage your core and lower your body towards the ground by bending your elbows, keeping your body in a straight line.
Step:3 Pause for a moment when your chest is just above the ground, then push yourself back up to the starting position by straightening your arms.
Step:4 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: push-up

instructions: Step:1 Adjust the cable machine to a low position and attach the handles.
Step:2 Sit on an incline bench with your back against the pad and feet flat on the floor.
Step:3 Grasp the handles with an overhand grip and extend your arms straight out in front of you.
Step:4 Keeping a slight bend in your elbows, open your arms out to the sides in a controlled motion.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the fully extended position, then slowly return to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: cable incline fly

instructions: Step:1 Start in a high plank position with your hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart and your body in a straight line.
Step:2 Lower your chest towards the ground by bending your elbows, keeping them close to your body.
Step:3 Push through your palms to extend your arms and return to the starting position.
Step:4 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: deep push up

instructions: Step:1 Sit on the edge of a bench with your back against it and your feet flat on the ground.
Step:2 Place your hands on the bench beside your hips for support.
Step:3 Engage your glutes and hamstrings, then lift your hips off the bench until your body forms a straight line from your knees to your shoulders.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your hips back down to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: glute bridge two legs on bench (male)

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a weight in each hand.
Step:2 Take a step forward with your right foot, lowering your body into a lunge position.
Step:3 As you lunge forward, swing the weights forward and upward, keeping your arms straight.
Step:4 Push off with your right foot and return to the starting position, swinging the weights back down.
Step:5 Repeat with your left foot and continue alternating legs for the desired number of repetitions.
name: weighted lunge with swing

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and your arms extended to the sides.
Step:2 Bend forward at the waist, keeping your back straight and your knees slightly bent.
Step:3 Reach down with your hands and clasp them together behind your legs.
Step:4 Slowly raise your hands up and over your head in a circular motion, keeping your legs straight.
Step:5 Continue the circular motion until your hands touch your toes.
Step:6 Reverse the motion and bring your hands back down to the starting position.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: hands reversed clasped circular toe touch (male)

instructions: Step:1 Start by lying flat on your back on the ground with your knees bent and feet flat on the floor.
Step:2 Place a barbell across your hips, holding it securely with both hands.
Step:3 Engage your glutes and core muscles, then lift your hips off the ground until your body forms a straight line from your knees to your shoulders.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top, squeezing your glutes.
Step:5 Slowly lower your hips back down to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: barbell glute bridge

instructions: Step:1 Stand facing the cable machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step:2 Bend at the hips and knees, lowering your torso until your back is parallel to the ground.
Step:3 Grasp the cable handles with an overhand grip, keeping your arms straight and your shoulders back.
Step:4 Engage your glutes and hamstrings to lift the cable handles, extending your hips and standing up straight.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the cable handles back down to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: cable deadlift

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold the barbell in front of your body with straight arms.
Step:2 Keeping your back straight, lower your body by bending at the knees and hips, as if sitting back into a chair.
Step:3 Continue lowering until your thighs are parallel to the ground, or as low as you can comfortably go.
Step:4 Pause for a moment, then push through your heels to return to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: frankenstein squat

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step:2 Take a step forward with your right foot, keeping your back straight.
Step:3 Lower your body by bending your knees until your right thigh is parallel to the ground.
Step:4 Push through your right heel to return to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat with your left leg.
Step:6 Continue alternating legs for the desired number of repetitions.
name: weighted stretch lunge

instructions: Step:1 Lie flat on your back with your legs extended and your hands by your sides.
Step:2 Engage your core and lift your legs off the ground about 6 inches.
Step:3 Keeping your legs straight, alternate lifting one leg slightly higher than the other.
Step:4 Continue this fluttering motion for the desired number of repetitions.
name: flutter kicks

instructions: Step:1 Start by sitting on the edge of a bench with your upper back resting against it and your feet flat on the ground, hip-width apart.
Step:2 Place a barbell across your hips, holding it securely with both hands.
Step:3 Engage your glutes and core muscles, then press through your heels to lift your hips off the bench, creating a straight line from your knees to your shoulders.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top, squeezing your glutes.
Step:5 Slowly lower your hips back down to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: barbell glute bridge two legs on bench (male)

instructions: Step:1 Adjust the seat and footplate of the sled machine to a comfortable position.
Step:2 Sit on the sled machine with your back against the backrest and your feet shoulder-width apart on the footplate.
Step:3 Grip the handles on the sides of the seat for stability.
Step:4 Push the footplate away from your body by extending your legs, keeping your heels on the footplate.
Step:5 Continue pushing until your legs are almost fully extended, but without locking your knees.
Step:6 Pause for a moment at the top of the movement, then slowly lower the footplate back towards your body by bending your knees.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: sled 45в° leg press

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, toes pointing slightly outward.
Step:2 Hold a weight in front of your chest or on your shoulders.
Step:3 Engage your core and keep your chest up as you lower your hips down and back, as if sitting into a chair.
Step:4 Lower until your thighs are parallel to the ground, or as low as you can comfortably go.
Step:5 Push through your heels to stand back up, squeezing your glutes at the top.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: weighted squat

instructions: Step:1 Adjust the machine to fit your body and sit on it with your back against the backrest.
Step:2 Place your feet on the footrests and grip the handles for stability.
Step:3 Engage your glutes and hamstrings, then push against the footrests to extend your hips and raise your legs backward.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your legs back to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: lever hip extension v. 2

instructions: Step:1 Lie face down on a flat bench with your hips at the edge and your legs hanging off the bench.
Step:2 Hold onto the bench for stability.
Step:3 Keeping your legs straight, raise them up towards the ceiling as high as you can.
Step:4 Squeeze your glutes at the top of the movement.
Step:5 Slowly lower your legs back down to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: reverse hyper on flat bench

instructions: Step:1 Lie flat on your back with your arms extended out to the sides.
Step:2 Raise your legs up towards the ceiling, keeping them straight.
Step:3 Slowly lower your legs to one side, aiming to touch the floor with your feet.
Step:4 Hold the stretch for a few seconds, then return your legs to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat the stretch on the other side.
Step:6 Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.
name: iron cross stretch

instructions: Step:1 Stand in front of a bench or step with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing your body.
Step:2 Place your right foot on the bench or step, ensuring your entire foot is in contact with the surface.
Step:3 Push through your right heel and lift your body up onto the bench or step, straightening your right leg.
Step:4 Bring your left foot up onto the bench or step, standing fully upright.
Step:5 Step back down with your left foot, followed by your right foot, returning to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch legs.
name: dumbbell step-up

instructions: Step:1 Adjust the seat of the sled machine so that your knees are at a 90-degree angle when your feet are on the footplate.
Step:2 Sit on the sled machine with your back flat against the backrest and your feet shoulder-width apart on the footplate.
Step:3 Grip the handles on the sides of the seat for stability.
Step:4 Push against the footplate to extend your legs, straightening them completely.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly bend your knees to lower the footplate back to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: sled 45° leg press (side pov)

instructions: Step:1 Sit on the ground with your legs extended in front of you.
Step:2 Bend your right knee and place your right foot on the outside of your left knee.
Step:3 Place your left elbow on the outside of your right knee and gently twist your torso to the right.
Step:4 Hold the stretch for 20-30 seconds, then switch sides and repeat.
name: seated piriformis stretch

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, toes slightly turned out.
Step:2 Hold the barbell across your upper back, resting it on your traps or rear delts.
Step:3 Engage your core and keep your chest up as you begin to lower your body down.
Step:4 Bend at the knees and hips, pushing your hips back and down as if sitting into a chair.
Step:5 Lower yourself until your thighs are parallel to the ground or slightly below.
Step:6 Keep your knees in line with your toes and your weight in your heels.
Step:7 Drive through your heels to stand back up, extending your hips and knees.
Step:8 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: barbell full squat

instructions: Step:1 Lie flat on your back on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your knees bent.
Step:2 Hold the barbell with an overhand grip and position it on your hips.
Step:3 Engaging your glutes, lift your hips off the bench until your body forms a straight line from your knees to your shoulders.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your hips back down to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: barbell lying lifting (on hip)

instructions: Step:1 Attach the band to a sturdy anchor point at waist height.
Step:2 Stand facing the anchor point with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Hold the band handles with your palms facing each other and your arms extended in front of you.
Step:4 Bend your knees and lower into a squat position, keeping your back straight and chest lifted.
Step:5 From the squat position, pull the band handles towards your body, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:6 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly release the tension and return to the starting position.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: band squat row

instructions: Step:1 Lie on your back with your legs straight and a resistance band looped around your feet.
Step:2 Bend your knees and bring them towards your chest, keeping your feet together.
Step:3 Slowly rotate your knees outwards, away from each other, while keeping your feet together.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the end of the rotation, then slowly return to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: band lying hip internal rotation

instructions: Step:1 Set up the smith machine with the barbell at shoulder height.
Step:2 Stand facing the barbell with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Grasp the barbell with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:4 Step back and position the barbell on your front shoulders, resting it on your collarbone and deltoids.
Step:5 Keep your chest up, back straight, and core engaged.
Step:6 Lower your body by bending at the knees and hips, as if sitting back into a chair.
