Step:7 Continue lowering until your thighs are parallel to the ground or slightly below.
Step:8 Pause for a moment, then push through your heels to return to the starting position.
Step:9 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: smith front squat (clean grip)

instructions: Step:1 Adjust the sled machine to a comfortable position for your height.
Step:2 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart on the platform, toes slightly pointed outwards.
Step:3 Hold onto the handles or bars for stability.
Step:4 Lower your body by bending your knees and hips, keeping your back straight and chest up.
Step:5 Continue lowering until your thighs are parallel to the ground or slightly below.
Step:6 Pause for a moment, then push through your heels to raise your body back up to the starting position.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: sled hack squat

instructions: Step:1 Set up the smith machine with the bar at hip height.
Step:2 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, toes pointing slightly outward.
Step:3 Bend at the hips and knees, keeping your back straight and chest up, and grip the bar with an overhand grip slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:4 Engage your core and lift the bar by extending your hips and knees, keeping the bar close to your body.
Step:5 Stand up straight, fully extending your hips and knees.
Step:6 Lower the bar back down by bending at the hips and knees, maintaining control and keeping your back straight.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: smith deadlift

instructions: Step:1 Start by lying on your back with your legs extended and your heels resting on top of the stability ball.
Step:2 Place your arms by your sides for stability.
Step:3 Engage your glutes and core muscles to lift your hips off the ground, creating a straight line from your shoulders to your heels.
Step:4 Bend your right knee and bring it towards your chest, keeping your left leg extended and your foot flexed.
Step:5 Kick your right leg diagonally across your body, extending it fully and engaging your hamstrings.
Step:6 Slowly return your right leg to the starting position, maintaining control and stability.
Step:7 Repeat the movement with your left leg, alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.
name: exercise ball one legged diagonal kick hamstring curl

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and knees slightly bent.
Step:2 Hold one end of the rope in each hand, with your palms facing each other.
Step:3 Raise your arms to shoulder height, keeping your elbows slightly bent.
Step:4 Begin making alternating waves with the ropes by rapidly raising and lowering each arm.
Step:5 Continue for the desired duration or number of repetitions.
name: battling ropes

instructions: Step:1 Start by standing with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a kettlebell in each hand at shoulder height.
Step:2 Bend your knees slightly and engage your core.
Step:3 Initiate the movement by explosively extending your hips, knees, and ankles, driving the kettlebells overhead.
Step:4 As the kettlebells reach the top, press them fully overhead, locking out your arms.
Step:5 Lower the kettlebells back to the starting position by bending your elbows and bringing them back down to shoulder height.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: kettlebell double push press

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand with an overhand grip.
Step:2 Let the dumbbells hang in front of your thighs, with your arms fully extended.
Step:3 Keeping your back straight and your core engaged, exhale and lift the dumbbells straight up towards your chin, leading with your elbows.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top, then inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back down to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: dumbbell upright row

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a kettlebell in each hand at shoulder height.
Step:2 Bend your knees slightly and engage your core.
Step:3 Press the kettlebells overhead, fully extending your arms.
Step:4 Bend your knees and quickly drop into a partial squat.
Step:5 Explosively extend your hips and knees, driving the kettlebells overhead.
Step:6 Lock out your arms and catch the kettlebells overhead with your knees slightly bent.
Step:7 Stand up straight and return to the starting position.
Step:8 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: kettlebell double jerk

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold the band in front of your thighs with your palms facing down.
Step:2 Keep your arms straight and lift the band up in front of you until your arms are parallel to the ground.
Step:3 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the band back down to the starting position.
Step:4 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: band front lateral raise

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand with your palms facing your body.
Step:2 Keep your back straight and your core engaged.
Step:3 Raise one dumbbell to the side, keeping your arm straight and your palm facing down.
Step:4 Continue lifting until your arm is parallel to the ground.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbell back to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat with the other arm.
Step:7 Alternate between arms for the desired number of repetitions.
name: dumbbell standing alternate raise

instructions: Step:1 Sit on a bench with back support and hold a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder level, palms facing your body and elbows bent.
Step:2 Press the dumbbells upward until your arms are fully extended and your palms are facing forward.
Step:3 Rotate your wrists as you lift, so that your palms end up facing forward at the top of the movement.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: dumbbell arnold press v. 2

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and grasp the cable handles with an overhand grip.
Step:2 Keep your arms straight and your core engaged.
Step:3 Raise your arms out to the sides until they are parallel to the floor.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your arms back down to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: cable lateral raise

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and your knees slightly bent.
Step:2 Hold the cable handle with an overhand grip, palms facing down, and your arms fully extended in front of you.
Step:3 Keeping your arms straight, raise the cable handle up to shoulder level.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the cable handle back down to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: cable forward raise

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder level with your palms facing inwards.
Step:2 Keeping your core engaged and your back straight, press the dumbbells upwards until your arms are fully extended overhead.
Step:3 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Step:4 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: dumbbell standing palms in press

instructions: Step:1 Stand tall with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step:2 Extend your right arm across your chest, placing your left hand on your right elbow.
Step:3 Gently pull your right arm towards your left shoulder, feeling a stretch in your right shoulder.
Step:4 Hold the stretch for 15-30 seconds, then release.
Step:5 Repeat on the other side.
name: rear deltoid stretch

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand with your palms facing your thighs.
Step:2 Keep your back straight and engage your core.
Step:3 Slowly lift the dumbbells in front of you, with your arms straight, until they are at shoulder level.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbells back down to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: dumbbell front raise v. 2

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold the band in front of your thighs with your palms facing down.
Step:2 Keep your arms straight and slowly raise them forward until they are parallel to the ground.
Step:3 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your arms back down to the starting position.
Step:4 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: band front raise

instructions: Step:1 Sit on an incline bench with a dumbbell in each hand, resting on your thighs.
Step:2 Lean back on the bench and raise the dumbbells to shoulder height, palms facing forward.
Step:3 Keeping your back against the bench, exhale and raise the dumbbells above your head, fully extending your arms.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top, then inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to shoulder height.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: dumbbell incline raise

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and your knees slightly bent.
Step:2 Keep your left hand up to protect your face and your right hand by your chin.
Step:3 Rotate your hips and pivot on your left foot as you extend your left arm forward in a punching motion.
Step:4 Twist your torso and engage your core muscles to generate power in the punch.
Step:5 Snap your arm back to the starting position and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: left hook. boxing

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, facing the smith machine.
Step:2 Grasp the barbell with an overhand grip, hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Keep your back straight and your core engaged.
Step:4 Pull the barbell up towards your chin, leading with your elbows.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the barbell back down to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: smith upright row

instructions: Step:1 Sit on a bench with a dumbbell in each hand, resting on your thighs.
Step:2 Raise the dumbbells to shoulder height, palms facing forward.
Step:3 Press the dumbbells upward until your arms are fully extended overhead.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbells back to shoulder height.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: dumbbell seated shoulder press

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a kettlebell in each hand at shoulder height with your palms facing towards you.
Step:2 Engage your core and press the kettlebells overhead, rotating your palms to face forward as you extend your arms.
Step:3 Pause at the top of the movement, then slowly lower the kettlebells back to the starting position.
Step:4 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: kettlebell arnold press

instructions: Step:1 Lie face down on the floor with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing each other.
Step:2 Extend your arms straight out in front of you, keeping a slight bend in your elbows.
Step:3 Engaging your shoulder muscles, lift your arms up and out to the sides, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your arms back down to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: dumbbell lying on floor rear delt raise

instructions: Step:1 Attach a rope handle to a low pulley cable machine.
Step:2 Stand facing the machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Grasp the rope handle with an overhand grip, palms facing each other.
Step:4 Bend your knees slightly and hinge forward at the hips, keeping your back straight.
Step:5 Keep your elbows slightly bent and pull the rope towards your chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:6 Pause for a moment at the top of the movement, then slowly release the tension and return to the starting position.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: cable rear delt row (with rope)

instructions: Step:1 Adjust the seat height of the smith machine so that the bar is at shoulder level.
Step:2 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and your knees slightly bent.
Step:3 Grasp the bar with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:4 Lift the bar off the rack and step back, maintaining a stable stance.
Step:5 Position the bar behind your head, resting on your upper traps.
Step:6 Keep your core engaged and your chest lifted throughout the exercise.
Step:7 Press the bar overhead by extending your arms, fully straightening them.
Step:8 Pause briefly at the top of the movement, then slowly lower the bar back down to the starting position.
Step:9 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: smith standing behind head military press

instructions: Step:1 Adjust the seat height and backrest of the leverage machine to a comfortable position.
Step:2 Sit on the machine with your back against the backrest and your feet flat on the floor.
Step:3 Grasp the handles of the machine with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:4 Push the handles upward and forward until your arms are fully extended, but not locked.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the handles back down to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: lever shoulder press v. 3

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in one hand with an overhand grip.
Step:2 Let the dumbbell hang at arm's length in front of your thighs, with your palm facing your body.
Step:3 Keeping your back straight and your core engaged, exhale and lift the dumbbell straight up towards your chin, leading with your elbow.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top, then inhale and slowly lower the dumbbell back down to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch to the other arm.
name: dumbbell one arm upright row

instructions: Step:1 Sit on a bench with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing forward.
Step:2 Raise the dumbbells to shoulder height, elbows bent and palms facing forward.
Step:3 Press the dumbbells upward until your arms are fully extended overhead.
Step:4 Lower the dumbbells back to shoulder height.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: dumbbell w-press

instructions: Step:1 Adjust the cable machine so that the handles are at shoulder height.
Step:2 Stand facing away from the machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Grasp the handles with an overhand grip and bring them up to shoulder level, with your elbows bent and pointing outwards.
Step:4 Press the handles upwards until your arms are fully extended overhead.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the handles back down to shoulder level.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: cable shoulder press

instructions: Step:1 Start in a standing position with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step:2 Lower your body into a squat position by bending your knees and placing your hands on the floor in front of you.
Step:3 Kick your feet back into a push-up position.
Step:4 Perform a push-up, keeping your body in a straight line.
Step:5 Jump your feet back into the squat position.
Step:6 Jump up explosively, reaching your arms overhead.
Step:7 Land softly and immediately lower back into a squat position to begin the next repetition.
name: burpee

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step:2 Jump off the ground and simultaneously cross your right leg in front of your left leg.
Step:3 As you land, quickly switch legs, crossing your left leg in front of your right leg.
Step:4 Continue alternating legs and jumping as quickly as possible.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: scissor jumps (male)

instructions: Step:1 Hold the handles of the jump rope with your hands, palms facing inward.
Step:2 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and knees slightly bent.
Step:3 Swing the rope over your head and jump over it as it comes towards your feet.
Step:4 Land softly on the balls of your feet and repeat the jump as the rope comes around again.
Step:5 Continue jumping for the desired duration or number of repetitions.
name: jump rope

instructions: Step:1 Start on all fours with your hands directly under your shoulders and your knees directly under your hips.
Step:2 Lift your knees slightly off the ground, keeping your back flat and your core engaged.
Step:3 Move your right hand and left foot forward simultaneously, followed by your left hand and right foot.
Step:4 Continue crawling forward, alternating your hand and foot movements.
Step:5 Maintain a steady pace and keep your core tight throughout the exercise.
Step:6 Continue for the desired distance or time.
name: bear crawl

instructions: Step:1 Start in a plank position with your hands on the wheel and your body straight.
Step:2 Engage your core and start rolling the wheel forward by extending your arms.
Step:3 Continue rolling until your body is fully extended and your arms are overhead.
Step:4 Reverse the movement by pulling the wheel back towards your body, using your core and arms.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: wheel run

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet together and your arms by your sides.
Step:2 Jump up, spreading your feet apart and raising your arms above your head.
Step:3 As you land, quickly jump back to the starting position.
Step:4 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: jack jump (male)

instructions: Step:1 Adjust the seat height and position yourself on the cycle cross trainer.
Step:2 Place your feet on the pedals and grip the handlebars.
Step:3 Start pedaling in a smooth and controlled motion.
Step:4 Maintain a steady pace and increase the resistance if desired.
Step:5 Continue pedaling for the desired duration of your cardio workout.
name: cycle cross trainer

instructions: Step:1 Start in a standing position with your feet shoulder-width apart and a dumbbell in each hand.
Step:2 Lower your body into a squat position, placing the dumbbells on the ground in front of you.
Step:3 Kick your feet back into a push-up position, keeping your body in a straight line.
Step:4 Perform a push-up, bending your elbows and lowering your chest towards the ground.
Step:5 Jump your feet back towards your hands, landing in a squat position.
Step:6 Stand up explosively, lifting the dumbbells off the ground and bringing them to your shoulders.
Step:7 Press the dumbbells overhead, fully extending your arms.
Step:8 Lower the dumbbells back to your shoulders and repeat the entire sequence for the desired number of repetitions.
name: dumbbell burpee

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step:2 Bend your knees and lower your body down as if you were sitting back into a chair.
Step:3 Keep your chest up and your weight in your heels.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push through your heels to return to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: half knee bends (male)

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step:2 Step forward with your right foot, bending your knee and lowering your body into a lunge position.
Step:3 Push off with your right foot and step back to the starting position.
Step:4 Repeat the movement with your left foot, alternating legs with each step.
Step:5 Continue stepping back and forth, maintaining a steady pace.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired duration or number of repetitions.
name: back and forth step

instructions: Step:1 Adjust the seat height and position on the stationary bike to ensure proper alignment.
Step:2 Place your feet on the pedals and secure them with the straps if available.
Step:3 Start pedaling at a comfortable pace, keeping your back straight and core engaged.
Step:4 Maintain a steady rhythm and increase the resistance level if desired.
Step:5 Continue pedaling for the desired duration of your cardio workout.
Step:6 Cool down by gradually reducing your pace and resistance level.
Step:7 Stretch your leg muscles after the workout to prevent tightness and promote recovery.
name: stationary bike walk

instructions: Step:1 Adjust the resistance level and incline of the elliptical machine to your desired settings.
Step:2 Step onto the pedals of the machine and grip the handles lightly.
Step:3 Begin by pushing down with your feet and pulling the handles towards your body.
Step:4 Continue this motion, alternating between pushing and pulling, to simulate a walking or running motion.
Step:5 Maintain a steady pace and keep your core engaged throughout the exercise.
Step:6 Continue for the desired duration of your cardio workout.
Step:7 Gradually decrease the intensity and speed of the machine before stepping off.
name: walk elliptical cross trainer

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and your arms by your sides.
Step:2 Bend your knees slightly and jump up explosively.
Step:3 As you jump, spread your legs and extend your arms out to the sides, forming a star shape with your body.
Step:4 Land softly on the balls of your feet with your knees slightly bent.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: star jump (male)

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet hip-width apart.
Step:2 Lift your right knee up towards your chest as high as you can while balancing on your left leg.
Step:3 Step forward with your right foot and lower your body into a lunge position, bending both knees to a 90-degree angle.
Step:4 Push off with your right foot and bring your left knee up towards your chest.
Step:5 Step forward with your left foot and lower your body into a lunge position.
Step:6 Continue alternating legs and lunging forward, keeping your core engaged and maintaining a steady pace.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: walking high knees lunge

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step:2 Bend your knees and lower your body down as if you were sitting back into a chair. Emphasize blunt control.
Step:3 Keep your chest up and your weight in your heels.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push through your heels to return to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: half knee bends (male) with blunt

instructions: Step:1 Start on all fours with your hands directly under your shoulders and your knees directly under your hips.
Step:2 Lift your knees slightly off the ground, keeping your back flat and your core engaged.
Step:3 Move your right hand and left foot forward simultaneously, followed by your left hand and right foot.
Step:4 Continue crawling forward, alternating your hand and foot movements.
Step:5 Maintain a steady pace and keep your core tight throughout the exercise.
Step:6 (alternating variation) Continue for the desired distance or time.
name: alternating bear crawl

instructions: Step:1 Adjust the resistance level and incline of the elliptical machine to your desired settings.
Step:2 Step onto the pedals of the machine and grip the handles lightly.
Step:3 Begin by pushing down with your feet and pulling the handles towards your body.
Step:4 Continue this motion, alternating between pushing and pulling, to simulate a walking or running motion.
Step:5 Maintain a steady pace and keep your core engaged throughout the exercise. Emphasize pulse control.
Step:6 Continue for the desired duration of your cardio workout.
Step:7 Gradually decrease the intensity and speed of the machine before stepping off.
name: pulse style walk elliptical cross trainer

instructions: Step:1 Adjust the resistance level and incline of the elliptical machine to your desired settings.
Step:2 Step onto the pedals of the machine and grip the handles lightly.
Step:3 Begin by pushing down with your feet and pulling the handles towards your body.
Step:4 Continue this motion, alternating between pushing and pulling, to simulate a walking or running motion. Emphasize expanded control.
Step:5 Maintain a steady pace and keep your core engaged throughout the exercise.
Step:6 Continue for the desired duration of your cardio workout.
Step:7 Gradually decrease the intensity and speed of the machine before stepping off.
name: walk elliptical cross trainer - expanded variation

instructions: Step:1 Adjust the seat height so that your knees are slightly bent and your feet are flat on the footplate.
Step:2 Place your toes on the footplate, with your heels hanging off the edge.
Step:3 Grasp the handles or the sides of the machine for stability.
Step:4 Push through the balls of your feet, raising your heels as high as possible.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your heels back down to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: lever rotary calf

instructions: Step:1 Sit on a bench or chair with your back straight and your feet flat on the ground.
Step:2 Hold a dumbbell in one hand and place it on top of your thigh, palm facing up.
Step:3 Lift one leg off the ground and extend it in front of you, keeping your knee slightly bent.
Step:4 Raise your heel as high as possible by pushing through the ball of your foot.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your heel back down.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch legs and repeat.
name: dumbbell seated one leg calf raise - palm up

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a barbell across your upper back.
Step:2 Raise your heels off the ground as high as possible, balancing on the balls of your feet.
Step:3 Slowly lower your heels back down to the starting position.
Step:4 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: barbell standing rocking leg calf raise

instructions: Step:1 Sit on a bench with your feet flat on the floor and a barbell resting on your thighs.
Step:2 Place the balls of your feet on a raised platform, such as a block or step.
Step:3 Lower your heels as far as possible, feeling a stretch in your calves.
Step:4 Raise your heels as high as possible, contracting your calves.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: barbell seated calf raise

instructions: Step:1 Sit on the edge of a chair or bench with your feet flat on the ground.
Step:2 Extend one leg straight out in front of you, keeping your heel on the ground.
Step:3 Lean forward slightly, feeling a stretch in your calf muscle.
Step:4 Hold the stretch for 20-30 seconds.
Step:5 Switch legs and repeat the stretch.
name: seated calf stretch (male)

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and place a barbell across your upper back.
Step:2 Raise your heels off the ground as high as possible, using only your toes.
Step:3 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your heels back down to the starting position.
Step:4 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: barbell standing calf raise

instructions: Step:1 Place a barbell on the floor in front of you.
Step:2 Stand with the balls of your feet on the edge of the barbell, with your heels hanging off.
Step:3 Hold onto a stable object for balance if needed.
Step:4 Raise your heels as high as possible, using your calves to lift your body.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your heels back down to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: barbell floor calf raise

instructions: Step:1 Attach the band to a pull-up bar or sturdy anchor point.
Step:2 Step onto the band and grip the bar with your palms facing away from you, hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Hang with your arms fully extended, keeping your core engaged and your shoulders down and back.
Step:4 Pull your body up towards the bar by squeezing your shoulder blades together and driving your elbows down towards your hips.
Step:5 Continue pulling until your chin is above the bar, then slowly lower yourself back down to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: band assisted pull-up

instructions: Step:1 Adjust the seat height and position yourself on the machine with your knees under the pads and your feet flat on the ground.
Step:2 Grasp the handles with an underhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Sit upright with your chest out and shoulders back, maintaining a slight arch in your lower back.
Step:4 Pull the handles down towards your chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the bottom of the movement, then slowly release the handles back to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: reverse grip machine lat pulldown

instructions: Step:1 Sit on the stability ball with your feet flat on the ground and your back straight.
Step:2 Hold a dumbbell in each hand with your palms facing inwards and your arms extended down by your sides.
Step:3 Engage your core and slowly lift one arm up towards your shoulder, keeping your elbow slightly bent.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your arm back down to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat the movement with the other arm.
Step:6 Continue alternating arms for the desired number of repetitions.
name: exercise ball alternating arm ups

instructions: Step:1 Attach a rope to a cable machine and set the pulley at the highest position.
Step:2 Lie down on a bench with your head towards the cable machine.
Step:3 Hold the rope with both hands and extend your arms straight up above your chest.
Step:4 Keeping your arms straight, slowly lower the rope behind your head while maintaining control.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the bottom, then slowly raise the rope back to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: cable lying extension pullover (with rope attachment)

instructions: Step:1 Hang from a pull-up bar with your palms facing away from you and your arms fully extended.
Step:2 Engage your core and squeeze your shoulder blades together.
Step:3 Pull your body up towards the bar by bending your elbows and bringing your chest towards the bar.
Step:4 Pause at the top of the movement, then slowly lower your body back down to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: pull-up

instructions: Step:1 Sit on the edge of a chair with your feet flat on the ground.
Step:2 Place your hands on your thighs or on the sides of the chair for support.
Step:3 Slowly lean forward from your hips, keeping your back straight.
Step:4 Feel the stretch in your lower back and hold for 20-30 seconds.
Step:5 Slowly return to the starting position and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: seated lower back stretch

instructions: Step:1 Adjust the seat height so that your thighs are parallel to the ground and your feet are flat on the floor.
Step:2 Grasp the lat bar with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Sit down and lean back slightly, keeping your chest up and your back straight.
Step:4 Pull the bar down towards your chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the bottom of the movement, then slowly release the bar back up to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: cable pulldown (pro lat bar)

instructions: Step:1 Start by hanging from a pull-up bar with your palms facing away from you and your hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:2 Engage your core and pull your shoulder blades down and back.
Step:3 Bend your elbows and pull your chest towards the bar, keeping your body straight.
Step:4 Once your chest reaches the bar, push down with your hands and drive your elbows back, lifting your body above the bar.
Step:5 Pause at the top of the movement, then slowly lower yourself back down to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: weighted muscle up (on bar)

instructions: Step:1 Start by hanging from a pull-up bar with your palms facing away from you and your arms fully extended.
Step:2 Engage your core and pull your body up towards the bar, leading with your chest.
Step:3 As you reach the top of the movement, transition your grip so that your palms are facing towards you.
Step:4 Continue pulling yourself up until your chest is above the bar and your arms are fully flexed.
Step:5 Reverse the movement by slowly lowering yourself back down to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: muscle up

instructions: Step:1 Attach a straight bar to the high pulley of a cable machine.
Step:2 Stand facing the machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Grasp the bar with an overhand grip, keeping your arms straight and your palms facing down.
Step:4 Engage your lats and pull the bar down towards your thighs, keeping your arms straight throughout the movement.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the bottom, then slowly return the bar to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: cable straight arm pulldown

instructions: Step:1 Attach the band to a high anchor point, such as a pull-up bar or sturdy beam.
Step:2 Stand facing the anchor point and grab the band with an underhand grip, hands shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Step back to create tension in the band, keeping your feet hip-width apart.
Step:4 Engage your core and keep your back straight as you pull the band down towards your chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the bottom of the movement, then slowly release the band back to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: band close-grip pulldown

instructions: Step:1 Sit on the stability ball with your feet flat on the ground and your knees bent at a 90-degree angle.
Step:2 Slowly walk your feet forward, rolling the ball down your back until your lower back is resting on the ball.
Step:3 Place your hands behind your head or cross them over your chest.
Step:4 Engage your core and slowly lower your upper body towards the ground, allowing your lower back to stretch over the ball.
Step:5 Hold the stretch for a few seconds, then slowly return to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: exercise ball lower back stretch (pyramid)

instructions: Step:1 Stand facing a pull-up bar with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step:2 Reach up and grab the bar with an underhand grip, with one hand gripping the bar and the other hand holding your wrist for support.
Step:3 Hang from the bar with your arm fully extended, keeping your body straight and your core engaged.
Step:4 Pull yourself up towards the bar by bending your elbow and squeezing your back muscles.
Step:5 Continue pulling until your chin is above the bar, then slowly lower yourself back down to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch arms and repeat.
name: one arm chin-up

instructions: Step:1 Start by hanging from a pull-up bar with your palms facing away from you and your hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:2 Engage your core and pull your body up towards the bar, leading with your chest.
Step:3 As you reach the top of the movement, transition your grip so that your palms are facing towards you.
Step:4 Continue pulling yourself up until your chest reaches the bar, then pause for a moment.
Step:5 Slowly lower yourself back down to the starting position, maintaining control throughout the movement.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: weighted muscle up

instructions: Step:1 Adjust the machine to your desired assistance level.
Step:2 Stand on the foot platform and grip the handles with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Keep your chest up and shoulders back, engage your core, and slightly bend your knees.
Step:4 Pull your body up by flexing your elbows and driving your elbows down towards your sides.
Step:5 Continue pulling until your chin is above the bar.
Step:6 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your body back down to the starting position.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: assisted standing chin-up

instructions: Step:1 Start by hanging from a pull-up bar with an overhand grip, hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:2 Engage your core and use a swinging motion to generate momentum.
Step:3 As you swing forward, pull your chest towards the bar, using your lats and biceps to initiate the movement.
Step:4 Continue the upward motion until your chest reaches the bar, then transition into a dip position by pushing down on the bar and extending your arms.
Step:5 Lower yourself back down to the starting position by bending your arms and controlling the descent.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: kipping muscle up

instructions: Step:1 Lie flat on a bench with your head at one end and your feet on the floor.
Step:2 Hold a barbell with a shoulder-width grip and extend your arms straight above your chest.
Step:3 Keeping your arms straight, lower the barbell behind your head in a controlled manner until you feel a stretch in your lats.
Step:4 Pause for a moment, then raise the barbell back to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: barbell pullover

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a medicine ball in both hands at chest level.
Step:2 Bend your knees slightly and engage your core.
Step:3 Lower your body into a squat position, keeping your back straight and chest up.
Step:4 Explosively extend your hips and legs, while simultaneously throwing the medicine ball overhead.
Step:5 Release the ball at the top of the movement and catch it on the way down.
Step:6 Lower your body back into the squat position and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: medicine ball catch and overhead throw

instructions: Step:1 Hang from a pull-up bar with your palms facing towards you and your hands shoulder-width apart.
Step:2 Engage your core and pull your body up towards the bar, leading with your chest.
Step:3 Continue pulling until your chin is above the bar.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your body back down to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: chin-up

instructions: Step:1 Attach a single handle to a high pulley cable machine.
Step:2 Stand facing the machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Grasp the handle with an overhand grip and extend your arm fully.
Step:4 Keep your back straight and your core engaged.
Step:5 Pull the handle down towards your side while keeping your elbow close to your body.
Step:6 Pause for a moment at the bottom of the movement, squeezing your lat muscle.
Step:7 Slowly release the handle back to the starting position.
Step:8 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
Step:9 Switch sides and repeat the exercise with the other arm.
name: cable one arm pulldown

instructions: Step:1 Position yourself under a bar or a sturdy horizontal surface that is at chest height.
Step:2 Grab the bar or surface with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Hang with your arms fully extended and your body straight.
Step:4 Pull your chest towards the bar or surface by squeezing your shoulder blades together and bending your elbows.
Step:5 Continue pulling until your chin is above the bar or surface.
Step:6 Lower yourself back down to the starting position with control.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: bench pull-ups

instructions: Step:1 Grab the pull-up bar with a wide overhand grip, hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:2 Hang from the bar with your arms fully extended and your body straight.
Step:3 Engage your back muscles and pull your body up towards the bar, leading with your chest.
Step:4 Continue pulling until your chin is above the bar.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your body back down to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: wide grip rear pull-up

instructions: Step:1 Kneel on the ground with your knees hip-width apart and your toes pointing back.
Step:2 Extend your arms overhead and interlace your fingers.
Step:3 Keeping your back straight, slowly lean to the right side, feeling a stretch in your left lat muscle.
Step:4 Hold the stretch for 20-30 seconds, then return to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat the stretch on the left side, leaning to the left and feeling a stretch in your right lat muscle.
Step:6 Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.
name: kneeling lat stretch

instructions: Step:1 Attach a cable handle to a low pulley and stand facing the machine.
Step:2 Grasp the handle with your left hand and step away from the machine, extending your arm fully.
Step:3 Position your feet shoulder-width apart, with your knees slightly bent.
Step:4 Keep your back straight and your core engaged throughout the exercise.
Step:5 Pull the handle towards your body, rotating your torso to the right as you do so.
Step:6 Squeeze your back muscles at the end of the movement.
Step:7 Slowly return to the starting position, keeping tension on the cable.
Step:8 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch sides and perform with your right hand.
name: cable twisting pull

instructions: Step:1 Attach the band to a fixed point above you, such as a pull-up bar or sturdy beam.
Step:2 Sit down on a bench or chair facing the band, with your feet flat on the ground and your knees bent.
Step:3 Grasp the band with a close grip, palms facing towards you.
Step:4 Keep your back straight and engage your core.
Step:5 Pull the band down towards your chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:6 Pause for a moment at the bottom of the movement, feeling the contraction in your lats.
Step:7 Slowly release the band back to the starting position, maintaining control throughout the movement.
Step:8 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: band fixed back close grip pulldown

instructions: Step:1 Sit on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and hold a barbell with an overhand grip, palms facing down.
Step:2 Rest your forearms on your thighs, allowing your wrists to hang off the edge.
Step:3 Slowly curl your wrists upward, bringing the barbell towards your body.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the barbell back down to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: barbell reverse wrist curl

instructions: Step:1 Attach a straight bar to a low pulley cable machine.
Step:2 Stand facing the machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Grasp the bar with an underhand grip, palms facing up, and your hands shoulder-width apart.
Step:4 Rest your forearms on a bench or pad, with your wrists hanging off the edge.
Step:5 Keeping your forearms stationary, exhale and curl your wrists upward as far as possible.
Step:6 Pause for a moment at the top, then inhale and slowly lower the bar back to the starting position.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: cable wrist curl

instructions: Step:1 Sit on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and hold a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing up.
Step:2 Rest your forearms on your thighs, allowing your wrists to hang off the edge.
Step:3 Slowly curl your wrists upward, squeezing your forearms at the top of the movement.
Step:4 Pause for a moment, then lower your wrists back down to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: dumbbell seated palms up wrist curl

instructions: Step:1 Sit on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your knees bent.
Step:2 Hold a barbell with an overhand grip, palms facing down, and your hands shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Rest your forearms on your thighs, allowing your wrists to hang off the edge.
Step:4 Keeping your forearms stationary, exhale and curl your wrists upward as far as possible.
Step:5 Hold the contracted position for a brief pause, then inhale and slowly lower the barbell back to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: barbell revers wrist curl v. 2

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and your arms extended in front of you.
Step:2 Extend your right arm out to the side, parallel to the ground, with your palm facing down.
Step:3 With your left hand, grab your right hand and gently pull it towards your body, feeling a stretch in your right forearm.
Step:4 Hold the stretch for 15-30 seconds, then release.
Step:5 Repeat on the other side.
name: side wrist pull stretch

instructions: Step:1 Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground.
Step:2 Hold a dumbbell in each hand with your palms facing up and your arms fully extended.
Step:3 Keeping your upper arms stationary, curl the dumbbells towards your shoulders by contracting your forearms.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: dumbbell lying supination

instructions: Step:1 Attach a weight to one end of a rope or bar.
Step:2 Hold the other end of the rope or bar with both hands, palms facing down.
Step:3 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and your arms fully extended in front of you.
Step:4 Slowly roll the weight up towards your hands by flexing your wrists.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the weight back down to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: wrist rollerer

instructions: Step:1 Start in a push-up position with your hands directly under your shoulders and your body in a straight line.
Step:2 Lower your body down towards the ground by bending your elbows, keeping them close to your sides.
Step:3 Once your elbows are at a 90-degree angle, lower your forearms to the ground, keeping your elbows directly under your shoulders.
Step:4 Pause for a moment, then push through your palms to lift your forearms back up to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: modified push up to lower arms

instructions: Step:1 Sit on a bench with your back straight and hold a dumbbell in one hand, resting it on your thigh.
Step:2 Raise the dumbbell up to shoulder height, keeping your elbow close to your body.
Step:3 Rotate your forearm outward, away from your body, while keeping your upper arm stationary.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly rotate your forearm back to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch to the other arm.
name: dumbbell seated one arm rotate

instructions: Step:1 Sit on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and hold a dumbbell in one hand, palm facing down.
Step:2 Rest your forearm on the bench with your wrist hanging off the edge.
Step:3 Slowly curl your wrist upwards, bringing the dumbbell towards your forearm.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbell back down to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch to the other arm.
name: dumbbell over bench one arm reverse wrist curl

instructions: Step:1 Sit on a bench in front of a Smith machine with your feet flat on the ground.
Step:2 Grasp the barbell with an underhand grip, hands shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Rest your forearms on your thighs, allowing your wrists to hang off.
Step:4 Slowly curl your wrists upward, bringing the barbell towards your forearms.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the barbell back down to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: smith seated wrist curl

instructions: Step:1 Sit on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and hold a barbell with an underhand grip, palms facing up.
Step:2 Rest your forearms on your thighs, allowing your wrists to hang off the edge.
Step:3 Slowly curl your fingers towards your palms, squeezing the barbell tightly.
Step:4 Hold the contraction for a moment, then slowly release your fingers back to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: finger curls

instructions: Step:1 Extend your arms straight out in front of you.
Step:2 Make a fist with both hands.
Step:3 Rotate your wrists in a circular motion, keeping your arms still.
Step:4 Continue the wrist circles for the desired number of repetitions.
name: wrist circles

instructions: Step:1 Stand facing the smith machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step:2 Grasp the barbell with an overhand grip, hands shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Keep your back straight and your elbows close to your body.
Step:4 Slowly curl your wrists upwards, bringing the barbell towards your body.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the barbell back down to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: smith standing back wrist curl

instructions: Step:1 Sit on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and hold a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing down.
Step:2 Rest your forearms on the bench, allowing your wrists to hang off the edge.
Step:3 Slowly curl your wrists upward, bringing the dumbbells towards your body.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbells back down to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: dumbbell over bench revers wrist curl

instructions: Step:1 Sit on a bench or chair with your feet flat on the ground.
Step:2 Hold a dumbbell in one hand with an overhand grip, palm facing down.
Step:3 Rest your forearm on your thigh, with your wrist hanging off the edge.
Step:4 Slowly lower the dumbbell towards the ground by flexing your wrist.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the bottom, then slowly curl your wrist back up towards your body.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch to the other arm.
name: dumbbell one arm reverse wrist curl

instructions: Step:1 Sit on a bench or chair with your feet flat on the ground.
Step:2 Hold the band with both hands, palms facing up, and rest your forearms on your thighs.
Step:3 Slowly curl your wrists upward, squeezing your forearms.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your wrists back down to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: band wrist curl

instructions: Step:1 Sit on a bench or chair with your feet flat on the ground.
Step:2 Hold a dumbbell in one hand with an underhand grip, resting your forearm on your thigh.
Step:3 Allow your wrist to extend, letting the dumbbell roll down towards your fingers.
Step:4 Slowly curl your wrist back up, bringing the dumbbell towards your forearm.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch to the other hand.
name: dumbbell one arm wrist curl

instructions: Step:1 Stand facing a cable machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step:2 Hold the cable handle with an overhand grip, palms facing down.
Step:3 Keep your arms straight and your elbows close to your sides.
Step:4 Slowly curl your wrists upward, bringing the cable handle towards your body.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the cable handle back to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: cable standing back wrist curl

instructions: Step:1 Sit on a bench with your forearms resting on the bench and your palms facing up, holding a dumbbell in each hand.
Step:2 Allow your wrists to hang over the edge of the bench.
Step:3 Slowly curl your wrists upward, squeezing your forearms at the top of the movement.
Step:4 Pause for a moment, then slowly lower your wrists back down to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: dumbbell over bench wrist curl

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a weight in each hand.
Step:2 Extend your arms straight in front of you, palms facing each other.
Step:3 Squeeze your hands together as hard as you can, engaging your forearms.
Step:4 Hold the squeeze for a few seconds, then release.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: weighted standing hand squeeze

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a kettlebell in each hand with an overhand grip.
Step:2 Bend your knees slightly and hinge forward at the hips, keeping your back straight and chest up.
Step:3 Allow the kettlebells to hang in front of your body with your arms fully extended.
Step:4 In one fluid motion, explosively extend your hips, shrug your shoulders, and pull the kettlebells up towards your shoulders.

Step:5 As the kettlebells reach shoulder height, rotate your wrists and catch the kettlebells in the rack position, with your palms facing inward and the kettlebells resting on the outside of your forearms.
Step:6 Lower the kettlebells back down to the starting position and repeat the movement with the opposite arm.
Step:7 Continue alternating arms for the desired number of repetitions.
name: kettlebell alternating hang clean

instructions: Step:1 Attach a cable to a low pulley and sit on a bench facing the cable machine.
Step:2 Grasp the cable handle with an overhand grip, palms facing down.
Step:3 Rest your forearms on your thighs, with your wrists hanging off the edge.
Step:4 Keeping your forearms stationary, exhale and curl your wrists upward as far as possible.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the top, then inhale and slowly lower your wrists back down to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: cable reverse wrist curl

instructions: Step:1 Sit on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and hold a dumbbell in one hand, palm facing down.
Step:2 Rest your forearm on the bench with your wrist hanging off the edge.
Step:3 Slowly curl your wrist upwards, bringing the dumbbell towards your forearm.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbell back down to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch to the other arm.
name: dumbbell over bench one arm wrist curl

instructions: Step:1 Lie flat on a bench with your chest facing down and your arms extended straight down, holding a dumbbell in each hand.
Step:2 Rotate your palms so they are facing up.
Step:3 Keeping your upper arms stationary, exhale and curl the dumbbells as you rotate your palms to face down.
Step:4 Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: dumbbell lying pronation

instructions: Step:1 Stand on a raised platform with your toes on the edge and your heels hanging off.
Step:2 Hold onto a stable object for support.
Step:3 Raise your heels as high as possible by extending your ankles.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your heels back down to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: weighted donkey calf raise

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and place a resistance band under both feet.
Step:2 Hold the ends of the band with your hands for stability.
Step:3 Raise your heels off the ground as high as possible, using your calves.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your heels back down to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: band two legs calf raise - (band under both legs) v. 2

instructions: Step:1 Adjust the sled machine to an appropriate weight.
Step:2 Stand on the sled machine with one foot, keeping the other foot off the ground.
Step:3 Hold onto the handles for stability.
Step:4 Raise your heel as high as possible, lifting your body up on the ball of your foot.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your heel back down to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
Step:7 Switch legs and repeat the exercise.
name: hack one leg calf raise

instructions: Step:1 Adjust the seat of the leverage machine so that your shoulders are aligned with the lever pad.
Step:2 Place your toes on the lever pad, with your heels hanging off the edge.
Step:3 Grasp the handles or side supports for stability.
Step:4 Push the lever pad down by extending your ankles, contracting your calf muscles.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the bottom of the movement.
Step:6 Slowly return to the starting position by allowing your heels to rise back up.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: lever calf press

instructions: Step:1 Adjust the sled machine to a comfortable weight and position yourself on the machine with your toes on the platform and your heels hanging off.
Step:2 Place your hands on the handles or the sides of the machine for support.
Step:3 Engage your calves and slowly raise your heels as high as possible, pushing against the resistance of the sled.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top of the movement, then slowly lower your heels back down to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: sled forward angled calf raise

instructions: Step:1 Sit on the ground with your legs extended in front of you.
Step:2 Loop the rope around the ball of your foot and hold the ends of the rope with your hands.
Step:3 Gently pull the rope towards you, flexing your foot and stretching your calf muscles.
Step:4 Hold the stretch for 15-30 seconds.
Step:5 Release the tension on the rope and repeat the stretch on the other leg.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: peroneals stretch

instructions: Step:1 Adjust the seat of the leg press machine so that your knees are slightly bent when your feet are on the sled.
Step:2 Place your feet shoulder-width apart on the sled, with your toes pointing forward.
Step:3 Release the safety handles and push the sled away from you by extending your knees and ankles.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top of the movement, then slowly lower the sled back down by bending your knees and ankles.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: sled calf press on leg press

instructions: Step:1 Sit on the machine with your back against the pad and your feet on the footrest.
Step:2 Place one leg on the footrest and keep the other leg off the footrest.
Step:3 Using your calf muscles, raise your heel as high as possible.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your heel back down to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch legs and repeat.
name: smith seated one leg calf raise

instructions: Step:1 Sit on a bench or chair with your feet flat on the ground and a dumbbell resting on your thighs.
Step:2 Place one foot on a raised surface, such as a step or block, with your heel hanging off the edge.
Step:3 Hold the dumbbell with a hammer grip, meaning your palms are facing each other and your fingers are wrapped around the handle.
Step:4 Keeping your core engaged and your back straight, slowly raise your heel as high as possible by pushing through the ball of your foot.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your heel back down to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch to the other leg.
name: dumbbell seated one leg calf raise - hammer grip

instructions: Step:1 Adjust the seat of the leg press machine so that your knees are slightly bent when your feet are on the sled.
Step:2 Sit on the machine with your back against the backrest and your feet on the sled, shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Place your toes and the balls of your feet on the sled, keeping your heels off.
Step:4 Push the sled forward by extending your ankles, keeping your knees slightly bent.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the sled back down by flexing your ankles.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: sled one leg calf press on leg press

instructions: Step:1 Stand facing a wall or sturdy object with your feet hip-width apart.
Step:2 Hold the ends of the rope in each hand and place the middle of the rope around the ball of your right foot.
Step:3 Step back with your left foot, keeping your heel on the ground and your leg straight.
Step:4 Lean forward, keeping your back straight, and gently pull on the rope to stretch your calf.
Step:5 Hold the stretch for 20-30 seconds, then release.
Step:6 Repeat on the other leg.
name: calf stretch with rope

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your back against a wall and place an exercise ball between your lower back and the wall.
Step:2 Position your feet shoulder-width apart and slightly in front of you.
Step:3 Hold a dumbbell in each hand, with your arms extended by your sides.
Step:4 Place a tennis ball between your knees.
Step:5 Raise your heels off the ground, lifting your body up onto your toes.
Step:6 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your heels back down to the starting position.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: exercise ball on the wall calf raise (tennis ball between knees)

instructions: Step:1 Stand on the edge of a step or a sturdy platform with your heels hanging off and your toes on the step.
Step:2 Hold onto a railing or wall for balance if needed.
Step:3 Slowly raise your heels as high as possible, lifting your body weight onto the balls of your feet.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your heels back down to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: standing calf raise (on a staircase)

instructions: Step:1 Sit on the ground with your legs extended in front of you.
Step:2 Loop the rope around the ball of your foot and hold the ends of the rope with your hands.
Step:3 Gently pull the rope towards you, flexing your foot and stretching your calf muscles.
Step:4 Hold the stretch for 20-30 seconds.
Step:5 Release the tension on the rope and relax your foot.
Step:6 Repeat the stretch on the other leg.
name: posterior tibialis stretch

instructions: Step:1 Adjust the sled machine to a comfortable weight.
Step:2 Stand on the sled machine with your toes on the platform and your heels hanging off.
Step:3 Hold onto the handles for stability.
Step:4 Raise your heels as high as possible by pushing through the balls of your feet.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your heels back down to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: hack calf raise

instructions: Step:1 Stand on the edge of a step or platform with your heels hanging off and your toes on the step.
Step:2 Hold a dumbbell in one hand and place your other hand on a wall or railing for support.
Step:3 Raise your heel as high as possible, lifting your body up onto your toes.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your heel back down below the step.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch to the other leg.
name: dumbbell single leg calf raise

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet hip-width apart and place the band around the ball of your foot.
Step:2 Hold onto a stable object for balance.
Step:3 Slowly raise your heel off the ground, lifting your body weight onto the ball of your foot.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your heel back down to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch to the other leg.
name: band single leg reverse calf raise

instructions: Step:1 Adjust the seat height so that your knees are slightly bent and your feet are flat on the footplate.
Step:2 Place your toes on the footplate with your heels hanging off the edge.
Step:3 Grasp the handles or the sides of the seat for stability.
Step:4 Push through the balls of your feet to raise your heels as high as possible.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your heels back down to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: lever seated calf raise

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and place a barbell across your upper back.
Step:2 Raise your heels off the ground as high as possible, using your calves.
Step:3 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your heels back down to the starting position.
Step:4 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: barbell standing leg calf raise

instructions: Step:1 Stand facing a cable machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step:2 Hold onto the cable machine handles or attach a cable ankle strap to your ankles.
Step:3 Raise your heels off the ground by extending your ankles as high as possible.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your heels back down to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: cable standing calf raise

instructions: Step:1 Adjust the sled machine to a comfortable weight.
Step:2 Sit on the sled machine with your back against the pad and your feet on the platform.
Step:3 Place your toes and the balls of your feet on the edge of the platform, with your heels hanging off.
Step:4 Push the platform away from you by extending your ankles, keeping your knees slightly bent.
Step:5 Continue pushing until your calves are fully contracted.
Step:6 Hold the contraction for a moment, then slowly lower the platform back to the starting position.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: sled lying calf press

instructions: Step:1 Stand facing a wall with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step:2 Place an exercise ball between the wall and your lower back.
Step:3 Hold a dumbbell in each hand, with your arms extended by your sides.
Step:4 Place a tennis ball between your ankles.
Step:5 Raise your heels off the ground, lifting your body up onto your toes.
Step:6 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your heels back down to the starting position.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: exercise ball on the wall calf raise (tennis ball between ankles)

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your back against a wall and place an exercise ball between your lower back and the wall.
Step:2 Position your feet shoulder-width apart, with your toes pointing forward.
Step:3 Hold a dumbbell in each hand, with your arms extended by your sides.
Step:4 Raise your heels off the ground, lifting your body weight onto the balls of your feet.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your heels back down to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: exercise ball on the wall calf raise

instructions: Step:1 Stand facing a wall with your feet hip-width apart.
Step:2 Place your hands against the wall at shoulder height.
Step:3 Step your right foot back, keeping your heel on the ground and your leg straight.
Step:4 Bend your left knee and lean forward, keeping your back leg straight and your heel on the ground.
Step:5 Hold the stretch for 20-30 seconds.
Step:6 Switch legs and repeat the stretch.
name: calf stretch with hands against wall

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your toes on an elevated surface, such as a step or block.
Step:2 Place your hands on a stable support, such as a wall or railing, for balance.
Step:3 Raise your heels as high as possible, lifting your body weight onto the balls of your feet.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your heels back down to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: donkey calf raise

instructions: Step:1 Adjust the smith machine to an incline position.
Step:2 Stand facing the machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Grasp the bar with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:4 Keep your back straight and core engaged.
Step:5 Raise the barbell up towards the ceiling, leading with your elbows.
Step:6 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the barbell back down to the starting position.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: smith incline shoulder raises

instructions: Step:1 Set an incline bench to a 45-degree angle and sit on it with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing down.
Step:2 Rest the dumbbells on your thighs and lean back onto the bench.
Step:3 Use your thighs to help raise the dumbbells to shoulder height, then rotate your wrists so that your palms are facing forward.
Step:4 Exhale and slowly raise the dumbbells above your head, keeping a slight bend in your elbows.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the top, then inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to shoulder height.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: dumbbell incline shoulder raise

instructions: Step:1 Set up an incline bench at a 45-degree angle.
Step:2 Place your hands on the bench slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Position your feet on the ground, hip-width apart.
Step:4 Lower your chest towards the bench, keeping your elbows tucked in.
Step:5 As you lower, retract your shoulder blades, squeezing your scapulae together.
Step:6 Push through your palms to extend your arms and return to the starting position.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: incline scapula push up

instructions: Step:1 Set up an incline bench at a 45-degree angle.
Step:2 Sit on the bench with your back against the pad and feet flat on the ground.
Step:3 Hold a barbell with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:4 Lift the barbell up to shoulder height, keeping your elbows slightly bent.
Step:5 Slowly raise the barbell overhead, extending your arms fully.
Step:6 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the barbell back to shoulder height.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: barbell incline shoulder raise

instructions: Step:1 Start in a high plank position with your hands directly under your shoulders and your body in a straight line.
Step:2 Lower your chest towards the ground, keeping your elbows close to your body.
Step:3 As you lower, squeeze your shoulder blades together and push your chest forward.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push back up to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: scapula push-up

instructions: Step:1 Start in a high plank position with your hands directly under your shoulders and your body in a straight line.
Step:2 Lower your chest towards the ground, keeping your elbows close to your body.
Step:3 As you lower, squeeze your shoulder blades together and push your chest forward. Focus on high movement.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push back up to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: high style scapula push-up

instructions: Step:1 Place the bosu ball on the ground with the flat side up.
Step:2 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and position yourself on top of the bosu ball.
Step:3 Lower your body down by bending your knees and hips, as if sitting back into a chair.
Step:4 Keep your chest up and your weight on your heels.
Step:5 Lower yourself until your thighs are parallel to the ground.
Step:6 Pause for a moment, then push through your heels to return to the starting position.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: squat on bosu ball

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and the barbell on the floor in front of you.
Step:2 Bend your knees and hinge at the hips to lower down and grip the barbell with an overhand grip, hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Drive through your heels and extend your hips and knees to lift the barbell off the floor, keeping it close to your body.
Step:4 As the barbell reaches your thighs, explosively extend your hips, shrug your shoulders, and pull the barbell up towards your chest.
Step:5 As the barbell reaches chest height, quickly drop under it and catch it at shoulder level, with your elbows pointing forward and your palms facing up.
Step:6 From the catch position, press the barbell overhead by extending your arms and pushing the barbell straight up.
Step:7 Lower the barbell back down to the starting position and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: barbell clean and press

instructions: Step:1 Lie face down on the ground with your legs extended.
Step:2 Bend your left knee and reach back with your left hand to grab your left foot or ankle.
Step:3 Gently pull your left foot towards your glutes, feeling a stretch in your left quad.
Step:4 Hold the stretch for 20-30 seconds, then release.
Step:5 Repeat with your right leg.
name: assisted prone lying quads stretch

instructions: Step:1 Set up a barbell on a squat rack at chest height.
Step:2 Stand facing away from the rack, with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Bend your knees and lower your body down into a squat position, keeping your back straight and chest up.
Step:4 Grasp the barbell with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:5 Lift the barbell off the rack and step back, ensuring your feet are still shoulder-width apart.
Step:6 Lower your body down into a squat, keeping your knees in line with your toes.
Step:7 Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push through your heels to return to the starting position.
Step:8 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: barbell bench squat

instructions: Step:1 Stand facing away from a suspension trainer with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step:2 Extend one leg forward and place the top of your foot in the foot cradle of the suspension trainer.
Step:3 Bend your standing leg and lower your body down into a lunge position, keeping your chest up and your knee in line with your toes.
Step:4 Push through your heel to return to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch legs.
name: suspended split squat

instructions: Step:1 Lie on your side with your legs straight.
Step:2 Bend your top leg and grab your ankle or foot with your hand.
Step:3 Gently pull your ankle or foot towards your glutes until you feel a stretch in your quads.
Step:4 Hold the stretch for 20-30 seconds.
Step:5 Release the stretch and repeat on the other side.
name: lying (side) quads stretch

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and toes slightly turned out.
Step:2 Lower your body into a squat position by bending your knees and pushing your hips back.
Step:3 As you come up from the squat, raise your arms overhead and twist your torso to one side.
Step:4 Return to the starting position and repeat the squat, this time twisting your torso to the opposite side.
Step:5 Continue alternating sides with each squat repetition.
name: squat to overhead reach with twist

instructions: Step:1 Stand in front of the smith machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step:2 Place one foot behind you on a bench or step, with your toes pointing forward.
Step:3 Hold onto the smith machine bar for stability.
Step:4 Bend your front knee and lower your body down into a lunge position, keeping your back straight.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push through your front heel to return to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch legs.
name: smith single leg split squat

instructions: Step:1 Sit on the edge of a chair with your back straight and feet flat on the ground.
Step:2 Extend one leg straight out in front of you, keeping your heel on the ground.
Step:3 Lean forward slightly, feeling a stretch in your quadriceps.
Step:4 Hold this position for 20-30 seconds.
Step:5 Switch legs and repeat the stretch.
name: chair leg extended stretch

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand at your sides.
Step:2 Keeping your chest up and core engaged, slowly lower your body down by bending your knees and pushing your hips back.
Step:3 Continue lowering until your thighs are parallel to the ground, or as low as you can comfortably go.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push through your heels to return to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: dumbbell supported squat

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and toes slightly turned out.
Step:2 Hold the barbell with a wide grip, positioning it overhead with your arms fully extended.
Step:3 Engage your core and lower your body down into a squat position, keeping your chest up and knees tracking over your toes.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push through your heels to return to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: barbell overhead squat

instructions: Step:1 Start by standing with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a barbell across your upper back.
Step:2 Take a large step forward with your right foot, keeping your torso upright.
Step:3 Lower your body by bending your knees and hips until your right thigh is parallel to the ground.
Step:4 Pause for a moment, then push through your right heel to return to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat with your left leg forward for the desired number of repetitions.
name: barbell split squat v. 2

instructions: Step:1 Stand upright with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step:2 Hold onto a stable object for support.
Step:3 Bend your right knee and bring your right foot towards your glutes, grabbing the rope with your right hand.
Step:4 Slowly pull your right foot towards your glutes, feeling a stretch in your right quad.
Step:5 Hold the stretch for 20-30 seconds.
Step:6 Release the stretch and repeat on the left side.
name: intermediate hip flexor and quad stretch

instructions: Step:1 Adjust the height of the smith machine bar to a comfortable position.
Step:2 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, toes slightly turned out.
Step:3 Place the barbell across your upper back, resting it on your traps.
Step:4 Engage your core and keep your chest up as you slowly lower your body by bending your knees and hips.
Step:5 Continue lowering until your thighs are parallel to the ground, or as low as you can comfortably go.
Step:6 Pause for a moment, then push through your heels to return to the starting position.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
name: smith chair squat

instructions: Step:1 Start by standing with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a barbell across your upper back.
Step:2 Lower your body into a squat position by bending your knees and pushing your hips back.
Step:3 Explode upwards, jumping off the ground as high as you can.
Step:4 Land softly on your feet and immediately step back with one leg into a reverse lunge.
Step:5 Lower your body down until your front thigh is parallel to the ground, keeping your back straight.
Step:6 Push through your front heel to return to the starting position.
Step:7 Repeat the jump and lunge sequence on the other leg.
Step:8 Continue alternating legs for the desired number of repetitions.
name: barbell squat jump step rear lunge

instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a barbell across your upper back.
